##### Hate Speech Analysis (34 papers)

| Title & Info | Analogy Summary | Pipeline | Summary |
|:--| :---: | :----: | :---: |
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Proceedings%20of%20the%20AAAI%20Conference%20on%20Artificial%20Intelligence-blue)]()<br>[TOT: Topology-Aware Optimal Transport for Multimodal Hate Detection](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/25614) <br> Linhao Zhang，Li Jin，Xian Sun，Guangluan Xu，Zequn Zhang,Xiaoyu Li,Nayu Liu,Qing Liu,Shiyao Yan <br> 2023-06-26|强化恶意Meme的图像与文本之间的语义对齐，使用OT方法建立特征向量间的可解释|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/e8d93670/TOT.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** 为了解决多模态仇恨检测中因” 隐式对齐” 和” 模态鸿沟” 导致的图像和文本跨模态语义对齐难题 **[innovation]** 将OT用于特征对齐，将句子级对齐细粒化至向量级，为后续工作提供了“显式对齐+结构推理”的范式 **[method]** 最优传输 + 拓扑结构推理方法 TOT：CLIP 方法统一表征映射-&gt;最优传输optimal transport \(OT\)将隐式联系细粒化为向量级（这是一个数学计算过程，不涉及需要学习的参数）-&gt;类GNN迭代捕捉自身语义联系（类自注意力）（因为向量间距离意义明确）-&gt;残差连接 **[conclusion/contribution]** 达成了在两个有害 Meme 检测数据集（Harm-C, Harm-P）上的最先进性能； **[limitation/future]** 对齐和推理仍局限于特征层面，未上升到语义单元（如事件、概念）层面，OT过程为冻结无法训练的，可以训练其参数以实现更好的对齐；对于幽默等类似隐式表达容易误判">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** 为了解决多模态仇恨检测中因” 隐式对齐” 和” 模态鸿沟” 导致的图像和文本跨模态语义对齐难题<br>**[innovation]** 将OT用于特征对齐，将句子级对齐细粒化至向量级，为后续工作提供了“显式对齐+结构推理”的范式<br>**[method]** 最优传输 + 拓扑结构推理方法 TOT：CLIP 方法统一表征映射->最优传输optimal transport \(OT\)将隐式联系细粒化为向量级（这是一个数学计算过程，不涉及需要学习的参数）->类GNN迭代捕捉自身语义联系（类自注意力）（因为向量间距离意义明确）->残差连接<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** 达成了在两个有害 Meme 检测数据集（Harm-C, Harm-P）上的最先进性能；<br>**[limitation/future]** 对齐和推理仍局限于特征层面，未上升到语义单元（如事件、概念）层面，OT过程为冻结无法训练的，可以训练其参数以实现更好的对齐；对于幽默等类似隐式表达容易误判</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【多模态】最优传输OT负责回答“图片的哪个部分和文本的哪个词相关？”（实现统一且对齐的表示，从而建立跨模态的显式联系，OT方法是可解释的）。【即将CLIP生成的特征矩阵级别的对齐，细化为特征向量间的对齐，两个特征矩阵会更相像。这种显式对齐能力本质上来源于CLIP实现的隐对齐】；拓扑建模负责回答“这些相关的部分组合在一起，表达了什么更深层的含义？”（捕捉文本（图片）中互相有联系的token（patch），进行模态内的深度推理）。【这种类似GNN的方法本质上是更有层次性的自注意力机制，天然适用于处理关系型数据（图结构）】；本质上是将CLIP建立的隐式对齐细粒化为向量层级的显式对齐，进而得以使用图推理进一步学习内部联系</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-IEEE%20Transactions%20on%20Multimedia-blue)]()<br>[Flexible optimal transport with contrastive graphical modeling for multimodal hate detection](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/abstract/document/11045556) <br> Linhao Zhang，Li Jin，Xiaoyu Li,Xian Sun,Senior Member,IEEE,Xin Wang,Zequn Zhang,Jian Liu，Zhicong Lu，Graduate Student Member,IEEE,and Guangluan Xu <br> 2025|\[AI generated\] This method bridges multimodal gaps like a flexible translator, aligning implicit hateful memes through optimal transport and contrastive graphs. \[翻译\]该方法通过最优传输和图对比学习，像灵活的翻译官一样弥合模态鸿沟，对齐隐含仇恨表情包。|<div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;align-items:center"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/bd2d2e30/FLOT1.png"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/bd2d2e30/FLOT.png"></div>| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** 社媒中隐含仇恨内容检测困难，传统方法难以实现跨模态隐式对齐。 **[innovation]** 相对于同团队的TOT是改进 **[method]** 相对于同团队的TOT:1.OT的目标域不再是另一模态的特征，而是可学习的统一嵌入（OT引入可学习的参数，它们是两个模态各自对应的目标特征矩阵 $T_v$ 和 $T_t$）；2.引入了图对比学习损失，显式约束一致性（比较两个图的相似程度作为一个损失，之后才进行类GNN聚合（动态拓扑推理）） **[conclusion/contribution]** 在Harm-C、Harm-P、MET-Meme三个数据集上取得SOTA，显著提升准确率与F1 **[limitation/future]** 对于幽默等类似隐式表达容易误判">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** 社媒中隐含仇恨内容检测困难，传统方法难以实现跨模态隐式对齐。<br>**[innovation]** 相对于同团队的TOT是改进<br>**[method]** 相对于同团队的TOT:1.OT的目标域不再是另一模态的特征，而是可学习的统一嵌入（OT引入可学习的参数，它们是两个模态各自对应的目标特征矩阵 $T_v$ 和 $T_t$）；2.引入了图对比学习损失，显式约束一致性（比较两个图的相似程度作为一个损失，之后才进行类GNN聚合（动态拓扑推理））<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** 在Harm-C、Harm-P、MET-Meme三个数据集上取得SOTA，显著提升准确率与F1<br>**[limitation/future]** 对于幽默等类似隐式表达容易误判</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【多模态】</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202024-blue)]()<br>[Improving Hateful Meme Detection through Retrieval-Guided Contrastive Learning](https://aclanthology.org/2024.acl-long.291) <br> Jingbiao Mei,Jinghong Chen,Weizhe Lin,Bill Byrne,Maarcus Tomalin <br> 2024|专题强化：难学样本单拉出来与正例进行对比学习，从而提高识别能力|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/347a037f/RGCL.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** 现有CLIP等模型对仇恨表情包的图像-文本的细微差异（如“混淆样本”）敏感度不足，导致的误判。 **[innovation]** 对于易混淆的难例（与当前样本相似度最高但标签相反的），使用**动态检索**方式单拉出来，与**伪黄金正样本**（和当前样本相似度最高的标签相同的）成对，作为正反例进行对比学习。从而解决问题 **[method]** 1. 使用冻结的CLIP编码器提取图文特征； 2. 通过Faiss检索动态获取同类相似样本（伪黄金正样本）与异类相似样本（困难负样本）作为正反例； 3. 结合正反例对比损失（RGCLL）与交叉熵损失训练MLP； 4. 实现逻辑分类与KNN检索分类两种分类器，后者通过相似度加权投票进行预测。 **[conclusion/contribution]** 在HatefulMemes数据集上达到 AUROC 87.0%（SOTA），超越Flamingo-80B等大型多模态模型 **[limitation/future]** 仇恨言论的定义具有争议性与文化依赖性；系统对 细微面部表情 识别能力有限；依赖数据标注质量，可能存在标注偏差。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** 现有CLIP等模型对仇恨表情包的图像-文本的细微差异（如“混淆样本”）敏感度不足，导致的误判。<br>**[innovation]** 对于易混淆的难例（与当前样本相似度最高但标签相反的），使用**动态检索**方式单拉出来，与**伪黄金正样本**（和当前样本相似度最高的标签相同的）成对，作为正反例进行对比学习。从而解决问题<br>**[method]** 1. 使用冻结的CLIP编码器提取图文特征；<br>2. 通过Faiss检索动态获取同类相似样本（伪黄金正样本）与异类相似样本（困难负样本）作为正反例；<br>3. 结合正反例对比损失（RGCLL）与交叉熵损失训练MLP；<br>4. 实现逻辑分类与KNN检索分类两种分类器，后者通过相似度加权投票进行预测。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** 在HatefulMemes数据集上达到 AUROC 87.0%（SOTA），超越Flamingo-80B等大型多模态模型<br>**[limitation/future]** 仇恨言论的定义具有争议性与文化依赖性；系统对 细微面部表情 识别能力有限；依赖数据标注质量，可能存在标注偏差。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【多模态】\[综述考量\]这是一个典型的面向多模态内容进行感知级别任务的例子，需要在对应章节后半部分讨论（多模态理解）</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ICWSM-blue)]()<br>[Human and LLM Biases in Hate Speech Annotations: A Socio-Demographic Analysis of Annotators and T...](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/ICWSM/article/view/35837) <br> Tommaso Giorgi\*,Lorenzo Cima\*,Tiziano Fagni,Marco Avvenuti,Stefano Cresci <br> 2025-06-07|仇恨言论分析中的数据集标注如何受主观偏见影响，提示词引导的角色扮演LLM能否复刻这种偏见|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/7f71404e/HateAnaBias.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** 该领域需要大量人工标注，存在固有的主观性bias问题，需要系统性的研究 **[innovation]** The authors introduce a novel methodological framework on the Measuring Hate Speech corpus that quantifies bias through &quot;Intensity&quot; and &quot;Prevalence&quot; metrics without relying on ground truth, uniquely isolating the interplay between specific annotator profiles and target groups. \[翻译\] 指标设计：提出了偏差强度（Intensity, ??）和偏差普遍性（Prevalence, ??），无需Ground Truth即可衡量相对偏差（将**其余所有标注者（Reference Group）**的共识作为基准）。 LLM对齐分析：评估了角色扮演LLM在“复现标注偏差”任务上的能力 **[method]** Leveraging a large-scale dataset with rich demographic attributes, the methodology employs a comparative analysis using confusion matrices to measure relative labeling discrepancies between demographic groups, subsequently evaluating open-source LLMs via role-playing prompts to assess their alignment with human bias patterns. \[翻译\]通过**混淆矩阵**（行代表不具备该属性，列代表具备该属性）对比特定属性群体在评价特定属性受害者时的标签差异。计算偏差强度和普遍性\\n使用**prompt**引导LLM进行相同任务以对比 **[conclusion/contribution]** Quantitative analysis reveals that while human annotators exhibit significant &quot;in-group&quot; hypersensitivity and demographic-specific labeling variations, persona-based LLMs demonstrate a limited correlation with these human biases, failing to accurately mirror the complex social prejudices inherent in human data. \[翻译\] 人类偏差：存在显著的“组内高敏度”（即倾向于高估针对自身群体的仇恨），受人口统计学交互影响严重（如年轻人倾向低估仇恨，老年人倾向高估）。 LLM表现：M表现出自身偏差，但未能有效复现人类的特定偏差（相关性极低），**欠缺对齐能力**（高估代表更敏感） **[limitation/future]** The study&#x27;s limitations include data scarcity for specific minority groups which constrains statistical significance, and a reliance solely on prompting strategies without fine-tuning, which may restrict the models&#x27; capacity for deep behavioral mimicry. \[翻译\] 该研究的局限性包括特定少数群体的数据稀缺限制了统计显著性，以及仅仅依赖提示策略而没有进行微调，这可能限制了模型的深度行为模仿能力。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** 该领域需要大量人工标注，存在固有的主观性bias问题，需要系统性的研究<br>**[innovation]** The authors introduce a novel methodological framework on the Measuring Hate Speech corpus that quantifies bias through "Intensity" and "Prevalence" metrics without relying on ground truth, uniquely isolating the interplay between specific annotator profiles and target groups.<br>\[翻译\] 指标设计：提出了偏差强度（Intensity, ??）和偏差普遍性（Prevalence, ??），无需Ground Truth即可衡量相对偏差（将**其余所有标注者（Reference Group）**的共识作为基准）。<br>LLM对齐分析：评估了角色扮演LLM在“复现标注偏差”任务上的能力<br>**[method]** Leveraging a large-scale dataset with rich demographic attributes, the methodology employs a comparative analysis using confusion matrices to measure relative labeling discrepancies between demographic groups, subsequently evaluating open-source LLMs via role-playing prompts to assess their alignment with human bias patterns.<br>\[翻译\]通过**混淆矩阵**（行代表不具备该属性，列代表具备该属性）对比特定属性群体在评价特定属性受害者时的标签差异。计算偏差强度和普遍性\\n使用**prompt**引导LLM进行相同任务以对比<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** Quantitative analysis reveals that while human annotators exhibit significant "in-group" hypersensitivity and demographic-specific labeling variations, persona-based LLMs demonstrate a limited correlation with these human biases, failing to accurately mirror the complex social prejudices inherent in human data.<br>\[翻译\] 人类偏差：存在显著的“组内高敏度”（即倾向于高估针对自身群体的仇恨），受人口统计学交互影响严重（如年轻人倾向低估仇恨，老年人倾向高估）。<br>LLM表现：M表现出自身偏差，但未能有效复现人类的特定偏差（相关性极低），**欠缺对齐能力**（高估代表更敏感）<br>**[limitation/future]** The study's limitations include data scarcity for specific minority groups which constrains statistical significance, and a reliance solely on prompting strategies without fine-tuning, which may restrict the models' capacity for deep behavioral mimicry.<br>\[翻译\] 该研究的局限性包括特定少数群体的数据稀缺限制了统计显著性，以及仅仅依赖提示策略而没有进行微调，这可能限制了模型的深度行为模仿能力。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【偏见】\[引用句\]Serving as a foundational critique within the transition from static classification to dynamic social simulation, Giorgi et al. \(2025\) demonstrate that although human perception of hate speech is fundamentally shaped by the interplay between annotator and target demographics, current persona-based LLMs fail to faithfully emulate these emergent sociological biases, highlighting a critical gap in the development of realistic AI agents.<br>\[翻译\] 作为从静态分类向动态社会仿真过渡过程中的一项基础性批判研究，Giorgi等人（2025）证明，尽管人类对仇恨言论的感知从根本上受标注者与目标人口统计特征交互作用的影响，但当前的基于角色的LLM无法忠实地模拟这些涌现的社会学偏差，突显了构建逼真AI智能体方面的一个关键差距。</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Findings%202025-blue)]()<br>[Towards Explainable Hate Speech Detection](https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-acl.667) <br> Happy Khairunnisa Sariyanto, Diclehan Ulucan, Oguzhan Ulucan, Marc Ebner <br> 2025|\[AI generated\] A lightweight VAD-based model achieves competitive hate speech detection performance, offering an explainable and computationally efficient alternative to complex deep learning architectures. \[翻译\] 一种基于VAD的轻量级模型在仇恨言论检测任务中取得与复杂深度学习架构相媲美的性能，提供了一种可解释且计算高效的替代方案。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to address the computational cost and lack of explainability in deep learning-based hate speech detection by proposing a simpler, cost-efficient model. It explores using weighted Valence, Arousal, and Dominance \(VAD\) scores for classification, demonstrating that this interpretable approach can compete with complex neural networks.  \[翻译\] 该论文旨在解决基于深度学习的仇恨言论检测中计算成本高和可解释性不足的问题，提出了一种更简单、成本效益更高的模型。它探索使用加权效价、唤醒度和优势度（VAD）分数进行分类，证明这种可解释的方法可以与复杂的神经网络竞争。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper innovates by proposing a lightweight, explainable hate speech detection model based on weighted Valence, Arousal, and Dominance \(VAD\) scores. It demonstrates that this simple, resource-efficient approach can achieve competitive performance with complex deep learning models, offering a cost-effective and interpretable alternative for NLP tasks. \[翻译\] 本文的创新点在于提出了一种基于加权效价、唤醒度和优势度（VAD）分数的轻量级、可解释的仇恨言论检测模型。研究表明，这种简单且资源高效的方法能与复杂的深度学习模型取得相当的检测性能，为自然语言处理任务提供了一种成本效益高且可解释的替代方案。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a hate speech detection model based on a weighted sum of Valence, Arousal, and Dominance \(VAD\) scores. It analyzes hate and non-hate speech words to determine optimal weights and classification strategies, demonstrating that this simple, explainable approach can compete with complex neural network models.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种基于效价、唤醒度和优势度（VAD）分数加权和的情感分析方法。该方法通过分析仇恨与非仇恨言论词汇来确定最佳权重和分类策略，证明了这种简单、可解释的方法能够与复杂的神经网络模型相竞争。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper demonstrates that a computationally efficient model based on weighted VAD \(Valence, Arousal, Dominance\) scores can achieve competitive performance in hate speech detection, rivaling complex deep learning models. This approach offers a step towards explainable AI by providing a transparent and resource-light alternative for content moderation tasks. \[翻译\] 该论文证明，基于加权VAD（效价、唤醒度、优势度）分数的计算高效模型，在仇恨言论检测任务中能达到与复杂深度学习模型相竞争的性能。这一方法通过提供一个透明且资源消耗低的替代方案，向可解释人工智能迈出了一步，适用于内容审核任务。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed VAD-based approach, while computationally efficient and explainable, may struggle with complex linguistic phenomena like sarcasm, irony, and context-dependent hate speech that rely on broader discourse understanding beyond lexical valence. Its performance is inherently tied to the coverage and cultural nuance of the underlying VAD lexicon. \[翻译\] 所提出的基于VAD的方法虽然计算高效且可解释，但可能难以处理复杂的语言现象，如讽刺、反讽和依赖更广泛语境理解的仇恨言论。其性能本质上受限于底层VAD词典的覆盖范围和文化细微差别。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to address the computational cost and lack of explainability in deep learning-based hate speech detection by proposing a simpler, cost-efficient model. It explores using weighted Valence, Arousal, and Dominance \(VAD\) scores for classification, demonstrating that this interpretable approach can compete with complex neural networks.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文旨在解决基于深度学习的仇恨言论检测中计算成本高和可解释性不足的问题，提出了一种更简单、成本效益更高的模型。它探索使用加权效价、唤醒度和优势度（VAD）分数进行分类，证明这种可解释的方法可以与复杂的神经网络竞争。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper innovates by proposing a lightweight, explainable hate speech detection model based on weighted Valence, Arousal, and Dominance \(VAD\) scores. It demonstrates that this simple, resource-efficient approach can achieve competitive performance with complex deep learning models, offering a cost-effective and interpretable alternative for NLP tasks. \[翻译\] 本文的创新点在于提出了一种基于加权效价、唤醒度和优势度（VAD）分数的轻量级、可解释的仇恨言论检测模型。研究表明，这种简单且资源高效的方法能与复杂的深度学习模型取得相当的检测性能，为自然语言处理任务提供了一种成本效益高且可解释的替代方案。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a hate speech detection model based on a weighted sum of Valence, Arousal, and Dominance \(VAD\) scores. It analyzes hate and non-hate speech words to determine optimal weights and classification strategies, demonstrating that this simple, explainable approach can compete with complex neural network models.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种基于效价、唤醒度和优势度（VAD）分数加权和的情感分析方法。该方法通过分析仇恨与非仇恨言论词汇来确定最佳权重和分类策略，证明了这种简单、可解释的方法能够与复杂的神经网络模型相竞争。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper demonstrates that a computationally efficient model based on weighted VAD \(Valence, Arousal, Dominance\) scores can achieve competitive performance in hate speech detection, rivaling complex deep learning models. This approach offers a step towards explainable AI by providing a transparent and resource-light alternative for content moderation tasks. \[翻译\] 该论文证明，基于加权VAD（效价、唤醒度、优势度）分数的计算高效模型，在仇恨言论检测任务中能达到与复杂深度学习模型相竞争的性能。这一方法通过提供一个透明且资源消耗低的替代方案，向可解释人工智能迈出了一步，适用于内容审核任务。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed VAD-based approach, while computationally efficient and explainable, may struggle with complex linguistic phenomena like sarcasm, irony, and context-dependent hate speech that rely on broader discourse understanding beyond lexical valence. Its performance is inherently tied to the coverage and cultural nuance of the underlying VAD lexicon. \[翻译\] 所提出的基于VAD的方法虽然计算高效且可解释，但可能难以处理复杂的语言现象，如讽刺、反讽和依赖更广泛语境理解的仇恨言论。其性能本质上受限于底层VAD词典的覆盖范围和文化细微差别。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【可解释】非生成式ai方法</div></details></div></div>|

| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202025-blue)]()<br>[Silencing empowerment, allowing bigotry: Auditing the moderation of hate speech on twitch](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.1110/) <br> Prarabdh Shukla, Wei Yin Chong, Yash Patel, Brennan Schaffner, Danish Pruthi, Arjun Bhagoji <br> 2025-07 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Hate Speech Analysis](#Hate-Speech-Analysis-34-papers), [Text Detoxification and Moderation](#Text-Detoxification-and-Moderation-1-papers)]</span>||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Hate Speech Analysis",<br>    "Text Detoxification and Moderation"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Silencing empowerment, allowing bigotry: Auditing the moderation of hate speech on twitch",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "To meet the demands of content moderation, online platforms have resorted to automated systems. Newer forms of real-time engagement \(e.g., users commenting on live streams\) on platforms like Twitch exert additional pressures on the latency expected of such moderation systems. Despite their prevalence, relatively little is known about the effectiveness of these systems. In this paper, we conduct an audit of Twitch's automated moderation tool \(\\\\textttAutoMod\) to investigate its effectiveness in fl"<br>}</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202024-blue)]()<br>[Silent signals, loud impact: LLMs for word-sense disambiguation of coded dog whistles](https://aclanthology.org/2024.acl-long.675/) <br> Julia Kruk, Michela Marchini, Rijul Magu, Caleb Ziems, David Muchlinski, Diyi Yang <br> 2024-08 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Hate Speech Analysis](#Hate-Speech-Analysis-34-papers), [Macro-Level Discourse Analysis](#Macro-Level-Discourse-Analysis-8-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Silent Signals: LLMs decode covert hate speech by disambiguating dog whistles as a word-sense task, creating a large dataset for detection. \[翻译\] Silent Signals：大语言模型通过将狗哨语作为词义消歧任务进行解码，创建了一个用于检测的大型数据集。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper addresses the challenge of detecting coded hate speech, specifically &quot;dog whistles,&quot; in social media and political discourse. Dog whistles are terms with dual meanings—one innocuous and one discriminatory—allowing speakers to evade automated hate speech detection systems while maintaining plausible deniability. The authors aim to develop a method for word-sense disambiguation to reliably identify when such terms are used with their harmful, coded intent.  \[翻译\] 本文旨在解决在社交媒体和政治话语中检测编码仇恨言论（特别是“狗哨”言论）的挑战。“狗哨”术语具有双重含义——一种是无害的，另一种是歧视性的——这使得发言者能够在保持表面合理否认的同时，规避自动仇恨言论检测系统。作者的目标是开发一种词义消歧方法，以可靠地识别这些术语何时被用于其有害的编码意图。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper introduces a novel approach for detecting and disambiguating coded dog whistles in social media and political discourse using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). It frames dog whistle detection as a word-sense disambiguation \(WSD\) task, where the goal is to distinguish between the innocuous and coded, discriminatory meanings of terms. The core innovation is the creation of the &quot;Silent Signals&quot; dataset—the largest collection of disambiguated dog whistle examples \(16,550 instances\)—by applying this LLM-based WSD method to a large corpus of formal \(Congressional records\) and informal \(Reddit\) communication. This work provides a new resource and methodology for improving hate speech detection systems, which often fail to identify such covert language, and for studying the evolution of coded political communication.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种利用大语言模型（LLM）检测和消歧社交媒体及政治话语中编码化“狗哨”（dog whistle）的新方法。它将狗哨检测构建 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors propose a novel method for word-sense disambiguation \(WSD\) of coded dog whistles using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). The core approach involves a multi-stage pipeline: \(1\) collecting a large corpus of potential dog whistle instances from formal \(U.S. Congressional records, 1900-2023\) and informal \(Reddit, 2005-2022\) communication via keyword matching based on an existing glossary; \(2\) designing and evaluating LLM prompts for the specific tasks of automatic dog whistle detection and contrastive disambiguation \(identifying coded vs. non-coded uses of a given term\); \(3\) leveraging the best-performing LLM-based disambiguation method to automatically filter and label the collected instances, thereby constructing the high-confidence &quot;Silent Signals&quot; dataset of 16,550 coded dog whistle examples.  \[翻译\] 作者提出了一种利用大语言模型（LLM）对编码化狗哨语进行词义消歧的新方法。其核心 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper introduces a novel approach for word-sense disambiguation of coded dog whistles using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). It presents the &quot;Silent Signals&quot; dataset, the largest collection of disambiguated dog whistle examples, created by applying LLMs to formal \(Congressional records\) and informal \(Reddit\) communication. The work demonstrates that LLMs, particularly in few-shot settings, can effectively detect and disambiguate dog whistles, outperforming non-expert human baselines. This enables the large-scale creation of labeled data for studying the evolution of coded hate speech and improving content moderation systems. \[翻译\] 本文提出了一种利用大语言模型进行编码化“狗哨”术语词义消歧的新方法。它发布了“Silent Signals”数据集，这是通过将LLM应用于正式（国会记录）和非正式（Reddit）交流而创建的最大规模的已消歧狗哨用例集合。研究表明，LLM（特别是在少样本设置下）能够有效 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The paper presents a novel LLM-based method for word-sense disambiguation of coded dog whistles and constructs the largest dataset of disambiguated examples. However, the approach relies on keyword matching from an existing glossary, which may not capture emerging or context-dependent dog whistles not in the seed list. Additionally, the evaluation of LLMs is limited to a small synthetic dataset, and the performance, while promising, still falls short of expert-level accuracy, indicating room for improvement in model generalization and robustness for this nuanced task.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种基于LLM的新方法，用于对编码的狗哨语进行词义消歧，并构建了最大的消歧示例数据集。然而，该方法依赖于从现有词汇表中进行关键词匹配，可能无法捕捉到不在种子列表中的新兴或上下文相关的狗哨语。此外，对LLM的评估仅限于一个小的合成数据集，其性能虽有前景，但仍未达到专家级准确度，表明在该微妙任务上，">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper addresses the challenge of detecting coded hate speech, specifically "dog whistles," in social media and political discourse. Dog whistles are terms with dual meanings—one innocuous and one discriminatory—allowing speakers to evade automated hate speech detection systems while maintaining plausible deniability. The authors aim to develop a method for word-sense disambiguation to reliably identify when such terms are used with their harmful, coded intent.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文旨在解决在社交媒体和政治话语中检测编码仇恨言论（特别是“狗哨”言论）的挑战。“狗哨”术语具有双重含义——一种是无害的，另一种是歧视性的——这使得发言者能够在保持表面合理否认的同时，规避自动仇恨言论检测系统。作者的目标是开发一种词义消歧方法，以可靠地识别这些术语何时被用于其有害的编码意图。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper introduces a novel approach for detecting and disambiguating coded dog whistles in social media and political discourse using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). It frames dog whistle detection as a word-sense disambiguation \(WSD\) task, where the goal is to distinguish between the innocuous and coded, discriminatory meanings of terms. The core innovation is the creation of the "Silent Signals" dataset—the largest collection of disambiguated dog whistle examples \(16,550 instances\)—by applying this LLM-based WSD method to a large corpus of formal \(Congressional records\) and informal \(Reddit\) communication. This work provides a new resource and methodology for improving hate speech detection systems, which often fail to identify such covert language, and for studying the evolution of coded political communication.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种利用大语言模型（LLM）检测和消歧社交媒体及政治话语中编码化“狗哨”（dog whistle）的新方法。它将狗哨检测构建<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors propose a novel method for word-sense disambiguation \(WSD\) of coded dog whistles using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). The core approach involves a multi-stage pipeline: \(1\) collecting a large corpus of potential dog whistle instances from formal \(U.S. Congressional records, 1900-2023\) and informal \(Reddit, 2005-2022\) communication via keyword matching based on an existing glossary; \(2\) designing and evaluating LLM prompts for the specific tasks of automatic dog whistle detection and contrastive disambiguation \(identifying coded vs. non-coded uses of a given term\); \(3\) leveraging the best-performing LLM-based disambiguation method to automatically filter and label the collected instances, thereby constructing the high-confidence "Silent Signals" dataset of 16,550 coded dog whistle examples.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>作者提出了一种利用大语言模型（LLM）对编码化狗哨语进行词义消歧的新方法。其核心<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper introduces a novel approach for word-sense disambiguation of coded dog whistles using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). It presents the "Silent Signals" dataset, the largest collection of disambiguated dog whistle examples, created by applying LLMs to formal \(Congressional records\) and informal \(Reddit\) communication. The work demonstrates that LLMs, particularly in few-shot settings, can effectively detect and disambiguate dog whistles, outperforming non-expert human baselines. This enables the large-scale creation of labeled data for studying the evolution of coded hate speech and improving content moderation systems. \[翻译\] 本文提出了一种利用大语言模型进行编码化“狗哨”术语词义消歧的新方法。它发布了“Silent Signals”数据集，这是通过将LLM应用于正式（国会记录）和非正式（Reddit）交流而创建的最大规模的已消歧狗哨用例集合。研究表明，LLM（特别是在少样本设置下）能够有效<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The paper presents a novel LLM-based method for word-sense disambiguation of coded dog whistles and constructs the largest dataset of disambiguated examples. However, the approach relies on keyword matching from an existing glossary, which may not capture emerging or context-dependent dog whistles not in the seed list. Additionally, the evaluation of LLMs is limited to a small synthetic dataset, and the performance, while promising, still falls short of expert-level accuracy, indicating room for improvement in model generalization and robustness for this nuanced task.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种基于LLM的新方法，用于对编码的狗哨语进行词义消歧，并构建了最大的消歧示例数据集。然而，该方法依赖于从现有词汇表中进行关键词匹配，可能无法捕捉到不在种子列表中的新兴或上下文相关的狗哨语。此外，对LLM的评估仅限于一个小的合成数据集，其性能虽有前景，但仍未达到专家级准确度，表明在该微妙任务上，</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【隐式】</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202025-blue)]()<br>[Sheep's skin, wolf's deeds: Are LLMs ready for metaphorical implicit hate speech?](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.814/) <br> Jingjie Zeng, Liang Yang, Zekun Wang, Yuanyuan Sun, Hongfei Lin <br> 2025-07 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Hate Speech Analysis](#Hate-Speech-Analysis-34-papers), [Micro-Level Pragmatic Expressions](#Micro-Level-Pragmatic-Expressions-17-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Metaphorical implicit hate speech detection: LLMs struggle to detect hate speech disguised as metaphorical rhetoric, revealing a critical vulnerability in current AI safety mechanisms. \[翻译\] 隐喻性隐式仇恨言论检测：大语言模型难以识别伪装为隐喻修辞的仇恨言论，暴露了当前AI安全机制的关键漏洞。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper investigates the vulnerability of state-of-the-art Large Language Models \(LLMs\) in detecting metaphorical implicit hate speech, a covert form of harmful content that bypasses conventional safety filters by disguising hateful intent within seemingly benign metaphorical expressions. \[翻译\] 本文研究了先进大语言模型在检测隐喻性隐式仇恨言论方面的脆弱性，这类有害内容通过将仇恨意图伪装在看似良性的隐喻表达中，从而规避常规安全过滤器。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes a novel method for generating metaphorical implicit hate speech by combining Jailbreaking strategies with Energy-based Constrained Decoding, and designs a small Metaphor Analysis Module to guide the generation. This approach exposes critical vulnerabilities in state-of-the-art LLMs and specialized safety models, which frequently fail to detect such rhetorically disguised hate content. \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种结合越狱策略与基于能量的约束解码的新方法，用于生成隐喻性隐式仇恨言论，并设计了一个小型隐喻分析模块来引导生成。该方法揭示了当前最先进的大语言模型和专用安全模型的关键漏洞，这些模型经常无法检测此类修辞性伪装的仇恨内容。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors propose a method to generate metaphorical implicit hate speech by combining Jailbreaking strategies with Energy-based Constrained Decoding. A white-box attack is implemented on a 7B-parameter LLM \(Vicuna-7B-v1.5\) using Langevin dynamics to optimize an adversarial prompt. The core of the method is a custom energy function that balances three objectives: maximizing the attack success rate \(E_att\), maintaining prompt fluency \(E_flu\), and ensuring the generated output uses effective metaphorical rhetoric to convey hate \(E_met\). The metaphorical quality is evaluated by a small, fine-tuned Metaphor Analysis Module \(approx. 1.2B parameters\), which combines a contrastive learning model \(bert-large-uncased\) to measure semantic similarity and a seq2seq model \(flan-t5-large\) to interpret metaphors, guiding the generation towards covert hateful expressions.  \[翻译\] 作者提出了一种结合越狱策略和基于能量的约束解码来生成隐喻性隐 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper reveals that current LLMs are vulnerable to metaphorical implicit hate speech, which often evades detection by disguising hateful intent within seemingly harmless metaphorical expressions. The authors propose a generation method combining jailbreaking and energy-based constrained decoding to produce such content, and demonstrate that even state-of-the-art LLMs and specialized safety models frequently fail to identify or block it, highlighting a critical gap in AI safety mechanisms.  \[翻译\] 本文揭示了当前大语言模型对隐喻性隐式仇恨言论的脆弱性，这类言论常通过将仇恨意图伪装在看似无害的隐喻表达中来逃避检测。作者提出了一种结合越狱攻击和基于能量的约束解码的生成方法来制造此类内容，并证明即使是先进的大语言模型和专门的安全模型也常常无法识别或阻止它，凸显了AI安全机制中的一个关键缺陷。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] Current LLMs, including advanced models like GPT-4o and specialized detectors like ShieldGemma, exhibit significant vulnerabilities in detecting metaphorical implicit hate speech. They often misinterpret such content as harmless, failing to prevent its propagation. The proposed generation method, while effective for research, also highlights the risk of LLMs being exploited to produce such covert hate speech. \[翻译\] 当前的大语言模型，包括GPT-4o等先进模型和ShieldGemma等专用检测器，在检测隐喻性隐式仇恨言论方面存在显著漏洞。它们常将此类内容误判为无害，未能有效阻止其传播。论文提出的生成方法虽对研究有效，但也凸显了大语言模型被利用来生成此类隐蔽仇恨言论的风险。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper investigates the vulnerability of state-of-the-art Large Language Models \(LLMs\) in detecting metaphorical implicit hate speech, a covert form of harmful content that bypasses conventional safety filters by disguising hateful intent within seemingly benign metaphorical expressions. \[翻译\] 本文研究了先进大语言模型在检测隐喻性隐式仇恨言论方面的脆弱性，这类有害内容通过将仇恨意图伪装在看似良性的隐喻表达中，从而规避常规安全过滤器。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes a novel method for generating metaphorical implicit hate speech by combining Jailbreaking strategies with Energy-based Constrained Decoding, and designs a small Metaphor Analysis Module to guide the generation. This approach exposes critical vulnerabilities in state-of-the-art LLMs and specialized safety models, which frequently fail to detect such rhetorically disguised hate content. \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种结合越狱策略与基于能量的约束解码的新方法，用于生成隐喻性隐式仇恨言论，并设计了一个小型隐喻分析模块来引导生成。该方法揭示了当前最先进的大语言模型和专用安全模型的关键漏洞，这些模型经常无法检测此类修辞性伪装的仇恨内容。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors propose a method to generate metaphorical implicit hate speech by combining Jailbreaking strategies with Energy-based Constrained Decoding. A white-box attack is implemented on a 7B-parameter LLM \(Vicuna-7B-v1.5\) using Langevin dynamics to optimize an adversarial prompt. The core of the method is a custom energy function that balances three objectives: maximizing the attack success rate \(E_att\), maintaining prompt fluency \(E_flu\), and ensuring the generated output uses effective metaphorical rhetoric to convey hate \(E_met\). The metaphorical quality is evaluated by a small, fine-tuned Metaphor Analysis Module \(approx. 1.2B parameters\), which combines a contrastive learning model \(bert-large-uncased\) to measure semantic similarity and a seq2seq model \(flan-t5-large\) to interpret metaphors, guiding the generation towards covert hateful expressions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>作者提出了一种结合越狱策略和基于能量的约束解码来生成隐喻性隐<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper reveals that current LLMs are vulnerable to metaphorical implicit hate speech, which often evades detection by disguising hateful intent within seemingly harmless metaphorical expressions. The authors propose a generation method combining jailbreaking and energy-based constrained decoding to produce such content, and demonstrate that even state-of-the-art LLMs and specialized safety models frequently fail to identify or block it, highlighting a critical gap in AI safety mechanisms.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文揭示了当前大语言模型对隐喻性隐式仇恨言论的脆弱性，这类言论常通过将仇恨意图伪装在看似无害的隐喻表达中来逃避检测。作者提出了一种结合越狱攻击和基于能量的约束解码的生成方法来制造此类内容，并证明即使是先进的大语言模型和专门的安全模型也常常无法识别或阻止它，凸显了AI安全机制中的一个关键缺陷。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] Current LLMs, including advanced models like GPT-4o and specialized detectors like ShieldGemma, exhibit significant vulnerabilities in detecting metaphorical implicit hate speech. They often misinterpret such content as harmless, failing to prevent its propagation. The proposed generation method, while effective for research, also highlights the risk of LLMs being exploited to produce such covert hate speech. \[翻译\] 当前的大语言模型，包括GPT-4o等先进模型和ShieldGemma等专用检测器，在检测隐喻性隐式仇恨言论方面存在显著漏洞。它们常将此类内容误判为无害，未能有效阻止其传播。论文提出的生成方法虽对研究有效，但也凸显了大语言模型被利用来生成此类隐蔽仇恨言论的风险。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【隐式】使用一种方法成功生成看似无害的隐喻性仇恨内容，成功绕过最先进的LLM的检测，揭示了当前的LLM仍然在深层隐喻语义理解上有所欠缺，<br>This paper proposes a novel method for generating metaphorical implicit hate speech by combining Jailbreaking strategies with Energy-based Constrained Decoding</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-LREC--COLING%202024-blue)]()<br>[Humans need context, what about machines? Investigating conversational context in abusive languag...](https://aclanthology.org/2024.lrec-main.740/) <br> Tom Bourgeade, Zongmin Li, Farah Benamara, Véronique Moriceau, Jian Su, Aixin Sun <br> 2024-05||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Hate Speech Analysis"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Humans need context, what about machines? Investigating conversational context in abusive language detection",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-05"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "A crucial aspect in abusive language on social media platforms \(toxicity, hate speech, harmful stereotypes, etc.\) is its inherent contextual nature. In this paper, we focus on the role of conversational context in abusive language detection, one of the most “direct” forms of context in this domain, as given by the conversation threads \(e.g., directly preceding message, original post\). The incorporation of surrounding messages has proven vital for the accurate human annotation of harmful content."<br>}</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202025-blue)]()<br>[Graphically speaking: Unmasking abuse in social media with conversation insights](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.894/) <br> Célia Nouri, Jean-Philippe Cointet, Chloé Clavel <br> 2025-07 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Hate Speech Analysis](#Hate-Speech-Analysis-34-papers), [Macro-Level Discourse Analysis](#Macro-Level-Discourse-Analysis-8-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Modeling social media conversations as graphs to detect abusive language, akin to mapping a conversation's structure to uncover hidden toxicity patterns. \[翻译\] 将社交媒体对话建模为图以检测辱骂性语言，类似于通过对话结构映射揭示隐藏的毒性模式。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/365c16cc/Graphically Speaking.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] Detecting abusive language in social media is challenging as it often depends on conversational context, including the content and reply structure of preceding comments. Traditional models often ignore this context, while recent NLP approaches use oversimplified context representations, leading to inconsistent results. This paper aims to address these limitations by proposing a graph-based framework to model conversations for more accurate abuse detection.  \[翻译\] 社交媒体中辱骂性语言的检测具有挑战性，因为其判断通常依赖于对话上下文，包括先前评论的内容和回复结构。传统模型常忽略此上下文，而近期的NLP方法则使用过于简化的上下文表示，导致结果不一致。本文旨在通过提出一个基于图的框架来建模对话，以解决这些局限，实现更准确的辱骂检测。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes a novel graph-based framework for context-aware abusive language detection \(ALD\) on social media. It models conversation threads as reply-structure graphs and employs Graph Neural Networks \(GNNs\) to propagate contextual information. A key innovation is an affordance-based graph trimming strategy that mimics the user-visible context on platforms like Reddit, optimizing computational efficiency while preserving relevant conversational structure. The method systematically outperforms both context-agnostic and flat context-aware baselines, demonstrating the critical role of structured conversational modeling for ALD.  \[翻译\] 本文提出了一种新颖的、基于图神经网络的社交媒体上下文感知辱骂性语言检测框架。其核心创新在于将对话线程建模为回复结构图，并采用图神经网络进行上下文信息传播。其中，一个关键创新是提出了基于平台交互特性的图剪枝策略，模拟了用户在Reddit等平台上的可见上下文，在保持相关对话结构的同时优化了计算效率。该方法系统性地超越了无视上下文及扁平化上下文 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a graph-based framework for abusive language detection \(ALD\) in social media conversations. It models conversation threads as directed graphs where nodes represent comments and edges represent reply relationships. A key contribution is an &quot;affordance-based&quot; graph trimming strategy that retains only the context likely visible to a user when replying, approximating the cognitive context. The core model is a Graph Attention Network \(GNN\) that aggregates contextual information from neighboring nodes \(comments\) in the graph. Node features are initialized using BERT embeddings of the comment text. The GNN dynamically learns the importance of different contextual comments through attention mechanisms to classify the target comment as abusive or not.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种基于图的社交媒体对话辱骂性语言检测框架。它将对话线程建模为有向图，其中节点代表评论，边代表回复关系。一个关键贡献是“可供性”图剪枝策略，该策略仅保留用户回复时可能看到的上下文，以近似认知上下文。核心 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a graph-based framework for abusive language detection \(ALD\) in social media conversations, modeling reply structures as graphs and employing Graph Neural Networks \(GNNs\). It demonstrates that explicitly modeling conversational topology with GNNs significantly outperforms both context-agnostic and flat context-aware baselines, establishing GNNs as a robust method for context-aware ALD.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种用于社交媒体对话中辱骂性语言检测（ALD）的图神经网络框架，将回复结构建模为图。研究表明，利用GNN显式建模对话拓扑结构，其性能显著优于无视上下文和扁平化上下文感知的基线方法，从而确立了GNN作为上下文感知ALD的稳健方法。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed GNN-based approach for abusive language detection is primarily evaluated on Reddit data, and its generalizability to other social media platforms with different conversation structures \(e.g., non-threaded chats\) requires further validation. Additionally, the computational efficiency of the graph construction and GNN inference, while improved over large generative models, may still pose challenges for real-time, large-scale deployment.  \[翻译\] 该研究提出的基于图神经网络的辱骂性语言检测方法主要在Reddit数据上进行评估，其对于其他具有不同对话结构（例如非线程式聊天）的社交媒体平台的泛化能力仍需进一步验证。此外，图构建和图神经网络推理的计算效率虽然优于大型生成模型，但对于大规模实时部署仍可能构成挑战。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] Detecting abusive language in social media is challenging as it often depends on conversational context, including the content and reply structure of preceding comments. Traditional models often ignore this context, while recent NLP approaches use oversimplified context representations, leading to inconsistent results. This paper aims to address these limitations by proposing a graph-based framework to model conversations for more accurate abuse detection.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>社交媒体中辱骂性语言的检测具有挑战性，因为其判断通常依赖于对话上下文，包括先前评论的内容和回复结构。传统模型常忽略此上下文，而近期的NLP方法则使用过于简化的上下文表示，导致结果不一致。本文旨在通过提出一个基于图的框架来建模对话，以解决这些局限，实现更准确的辱骂检测。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes a novel graph-based framework for context-aware abusive language detection \(ALD\) on social media. It models conversation threads as reply-structure graphs and employs Graph Neural Networks \(GNNs\) to propagate contextual information. A key innovation is an affordance-based graph trimming strategy that mimics the user-visible context on platforms like Reddit, optimizing computational efficiency while preserving relevant conversational structure. The method systematically outperforms both context-agnostic and flat context-aware baselines, demonstrating the critical role of structured conversational modeling for ALD.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文提出了一种新颖的、基于图神经网络的社交媒体上下文感知辱骂性语言检测框架。其核心创新在于将对话线程建模为回复结构图，并采用图神经网络进行上下文信息传播。其中，一个关键创新是提出了基于平台交互特性的图剪枝策略，模拟了用户在Reddit等平台上的可见上下文，在保持相关对话结构的同时优化了计算效率。该方法系统性地超越了无视上下文及扁平化上下文<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a graph-based framework for abusive language detection \(ALD\) in social media conversations. It models conversation threads as directed graphs where nodes represent comments and edges represent reply relationships. A key contribution is an "affordance-based" graph trimming strategy that retains only the context likely visible to a user when replying, approximating the cognitive context. The core model is a Graph Attention Network \(GNN\) that aggregates contextual information from neighboring nodes \(comments\) in the graph. Node features are initialized using BERT embeddings of the comment text. The GNN dynamically learns the importance of different contextual comments through attention mechanisms to classify the target comment as abusive or not.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种基于图的社交媒体对话辱骂性语言检测框架。它将对话线程建模为有向图，其中节点代表评论，边代表回复关系。一个关键贡献是“可供性”图剪枝策略，该策略仅保留用户回复时可能看到的上下文，以近似认知上下文。核心<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a graph-based framework for abusive language detection \(ALD\) in social media conversations, modeling reply structures as graphs and employing Graph Neural Networks \(GNNs\). It demonstrates that explicitly modeling conversational topology with GNNs significantly outperforms both context-agnostic and flat context-aware baselines, establishing GNNs as a robust method for context-aware ALD.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种用于社交媒体对话中辱骂性语言检测（ALD）的图神经网络框架，将回复结构建模为图。研究表明，利用GNN显式建模对话拓扑结构，其性能显著优于无视上下文和扁平化上下文感知的基线方法，从而确立了GNN作为上下文感知ALD的稳健方法。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed GNN-based approach for abusive language detection is primarily evaluated on Reddit data, and its generalizability to other social media platforms with different conversation structures \(e.g., non-threaded chats\) requires further validation. Additionally, the computational efficiency of the graph construction and GNN inference, while improved over large generative models, may still pose challenges for real-time, large-scale deployment.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该研究提出的基于图神经网络的辱骂性语言检测方法主要在Reddit数据上进行评估，其对于其他具有不同对话结构（例如非线程式聊天）的社交媒体平台的泛化能力仍需进一步验证。此外，图构建和图神经网络推理的计算效率虽然优于大型生成模型，但对于大规模实时部署仍可能构成挑战。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Hate Speech Analysis"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Graphically speaking: Unmasking abuse in social media with conversation insights",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Detecting abusive language in social media conversations poses significant challenges, as identifying abusiveness often depends on the conversational context, characterized by the content and topology of preceding comments. Traditional Abusive Language Detection \(ALD\) models often overlook this context, which can lead to unreliable performance metrics. Recent Natural Language Processing \(NLP\) approaches that incorporate conversational context often rely on limited or overly simplified representa"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-The%202023%20Conference%20on%20Empirical%20Methods%20in%20Natural%20Language%20Processing-blue)]()<br>[CoSyn: Detecting implicit hate speech in online conversations using a context synergized hyperbol...](https://openreview.net/forum?id=di1Foopybz) <br> Sreyan Ghosh, Manan Suri, Purva Chiniya, Utkarsh Tyagi, Sonal Kumar, Dinesh Manocha <br> 2023-12-01|\[AI generated\] CoSyn: A hyperbolic neural network synergizing user and conversational contexts to detect implicit hate speech in online dialogues. \[翻译\] CoSyn：一种双曲神经网络，协同用户与对话上下文以检测在线对话中的隐含仇恨言论。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/127a11c0/CoSyn.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] CoSyn addresses the critical challenge of detecting implicit hate speech in online conversations, which lacks overt lexical signals and heavily relies on conversational and user context for interpretation. Prior work largely focuses on explicit hate speech, leaving a gap in identifying subtle, context-dependent hatred. \[翻译\] CoSyn旨在解决在线对话中隐式仇恨言论检测这一关键挑战，此类言论缺乏明显的词汇信号，其解读严重依赖于对话和用户上下文。先前的研究主要集中于显式仇恨言论，在识别微妙且依赖上下文的仇恨表达方面存在空白。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] CoSyn introduces a context-synergized hyperbolic network for implicit hate speech detection, explicitly modeling user historical and social contexts alongside conversational context through novel hyperbolic modules \(HFAN, HGCN, CSHT\) to capture scale-free social dynamics and noisy context interplay. \[翻译\] CoSyn提出了一种上下文协同的双曲网络用于隐式仇恨言论检测，通过新颖的双曲模块（HFAN、HGCN、CSHT）显式建模用户历史、社交及对话上下文，以捕捉社交媒体的无标度动态和噪声上下文间的交互。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] CoSyn is a context-synergized hyperbolic network designed for implicit hate speech detection in online conversations. It integrates user historical context via a Hyperbolic Fourier Attention Network \(HFAN\) and user social context via a Hyperbolic Graph Convolutional Network \(HGCN\). These are jointly modeled with conversational context using a novel Context-Synergized Hyperbolic Tree-LSTM \(CSHT\), all operating in hyperbolic space to capture the scale-free dynamics of social media.  \[翻译\] CoSyn是一个用于在线对话中隐式仇恨言论检测的上下文协同双曲网络。它通过双曲傅里叶注意力网络（HFAN）整合用户历史上下文，通过双曲图卷积网络（HGCN）整合用户社交上下文。这些信息通过新颖的上下文协同双曲树LSTM（CSHT）与对话上下文进行联合建模，所有操作均在双曲空间中进行，以捕捉社交媒体的无标度动态特性。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] CoSyn introduces a novel neural network architecture for detecting implicit hate speech in online conversations by synergistically modeling user historical context, social context, and conversational context within hyperbolic space. It demonstrates superior performance over baselines across six datasets, with absolute improvements ranging from 1.24% to 57.8%.  \[翻译\] CoSyn提出了一种新颖的神经网络架构，用于检测在线对话中的隐性仇恨言论。该架构通过在双曲空间中协同建模用户历史背景、社交背景和对话背景来实现。实验表明，其在六个数据集上的性能均优于基线模型，绝对性能提升范围在1.24%至57.8%之间。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] CoSyn&#x27;s limitations include its reliance on external context \(user history and conversational structure\), which may not always be available, and its computational complexity due to hyperbolic space operations and multi-component architecture. \[翻译\] CoSyn的局限性包括其对外部上下文（用户历史和对话结构）的依赖（这些信息可能并非总是可用），以及由于双曲空间操作和多组件架构带来的计算复杂性。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] CoSyn addresses the critical challenge of detecting implicit hate speech in online conversations, which lacks overt lexical signals and heavily relies on conversational and user context for interpretation. Prior work largely focuses on explicit hate speech, leaving a gap in identifying subtle, context-dependent hatred. \[翻译\] CoSyn旨在解决在线对话中隐式仇恨言论检测这一关键挑战，此类言论缺乏明显的词汇信号，其解读严重依赖于对话和用户上下文。先前的研究主要集中于显式仇恨言论，在识别微妙且依赖上下文的仇恨表达方面存在空白。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] CoSyn introduces a context-synergized hyperbolic network for implicit hate speech detection, explicitly modeling user historical and social contexts alongside conversational context through novel hyperbolic modules \(HFAN, HGCN, CSHT\) to capture scale-free social dynamics and noisy context interplay. \[翻译\] CoSyn提出了一种上下文协同的双曲网络用于隐式仇恨言论检测，通过新颖的双曲模块（HFAN、HGCN、CSHT）显式建模用户历史、社交及对话上下文，以捕捉社交媒体的无标度动态和噪声上下文间的交互。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] CoSyn is a context-synergized hyperbolic network designed for implicit hate speech detection in online conversations. It integrates user historical context via a Hyperbolic Fourier Attention Network \(HFAN\) and user social context via a Hyperbolic Graph Convolutional Network \(HGCN\). These are jointly modeled with conversational context using a novel Context-Synergized Hyperbolic Tree-LSTM \(CSHT\), all operating in hyperbolic space to capture the scale-free dynamics of social media.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>CoSyn是一个用于在线对话中隐式仇恨言论检测的上下文协同双曲网络。它通过双曲傅里叶注意力网络（HFAN）整合用户历史上下文，通过双曲图卷积网络（HGCN）整合用户社交上下文。这些信息通过新颖的上下文协同双曲树LSTM（CSHT）与对话上下文进行联合建模，所有操作均在双曲空间中进行，以捕捉社交媒体的无标度动态特性。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] CoSyn introduces a novel neural network architecture for detecting implicit hate speech in online conversations by synergistically modeling user historical context, social context, and conversational context within hyperbolic space. It demonstrates superior performance over baselines across six datasets, with absolute improvements ranging from 1.24% to 57.8%.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>CoSyn提出了一种新颖的神经网络架构，用于检测在线对话中的隐性仇恨言论。该架构通过在双曲空间中协同建模用户历史背景、社交背景和对话背景来实现。实验表明，其在六个数据集上的性能均优于基线模型，绝对性能提升范围在1.24%至57.8%之间。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] CoSyn's limitations include its reliance on external context \(user history and conversational structure\), which may not always be available, and its computational complexity due to hyperbolic space operations and multi-component architecture. \[翻译\] CoSyn的局限性包括其对外部上下文（用户历史和对话结构）的依赖（这些信息可能并非总是可用），以及由于双曲空间操作和多组件架构带来的计算复杂性。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【隐式】</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-COLING%202025-blue)]()<br>[Learn from failure: Causality-guided contrastive learning for generalizable implicit hate speech ...](https://aclanthology.org/2025.coling-main.593/) <br> Tianming Jiang <br> 2025-01||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【隐式】</div></details></div>|

| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-EMNLP%202024-blue)]()<br>[M3Hop-CoT: Misogynous meme identification with multimodal multi-hop chain-of-thought](https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-main.1234/) <br> Gitanjali Kumari, Kirtan Jain, Asif Ekbal <br> 2024-11||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【多模态】</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202024-blue)]()<br>[Explainability and hate speech: Structured explanations make social media moderators faster](https://aclanthology.org/2024.acl-short.38/) <br> Agostina Calabrese, Leonardo Neves, Neil Shah, Maarten Bos, Björn Ross, Mirella Lapata, Francesco Barbieri <br> 2024-08||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【可解释】</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Findings%202024-blue)]()<br>[HateCOT: An explanation-enhanced dataset for generalizable offensive speech detection via large l...](https://aclanthology.org/2024.findings-emnlp.343/) <br> Huy Nghiem, Hal Daumé Iii <br> 2024-11|\[AI generated\] HateCOT: A large-scale explanation-enhanced dataset for training LLMs to generalize across diverse offensive speech detection tasks. \[翻译\] HateCOT：一个大规模、解释增强的数据集，用于训练大语言模型以泛化至多样的冒犯性言论检测任务。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The widespread use of social media necessitates reliable detection of offensive content to mitigate its harmful effects. However, existing models often fail to generalize across datasets due to varying definitions and labeling of &quot;offensive content,&quot; and they typically lack transparency in their decision-making. This paper aims to address these challenges by introducing HateCOT, a large-scale, explanation-enhanced dataset designed to improve the generalization and explainability of offensive speech detection using large language models. \[翻译\] 社交媒体的广泛使用使得可靠检测有害内容以减轻其负面影响变得至关重要。然而，现有模型由于对“有害内容”的定义和标注标准不一，往往难以在不同数据集间泛化，并且通常缺乏决策透明度。本文旨在通过引入HateCOT这一大规模、解释增强的数据集来解决这些挑战，该数据集旨在利用大语言模型提升有害言论检测的泛化能力和可解释性。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] HateCOT introduces a large-scale, explanation-enhanced dataset for offensive speech detection, generated by GPT-3.5-Turbo and human-curated, to improve the cross-dataset generalization and explainability of open-source LLMs through pretraining and few-shot fine-tuning. \[翻译\] HateCOT提出了一个大规模、解释增强的冒犯性言论检测数据集，该数据集由GPT-3.5-Turbo生成并经人工校验，旨在通过预训练和少样本微调提升开源大语言模型的跨数据集泛化能力和可解释性。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors construct HateCOT, a large-scale explanation-enhanced dataset for offensive speech detection. The method involves: \(1\) selecting and merging eight existing offensive speech datasets based on criteria of size, label diversity, and availability of definitions and rationales; \(2\) using GPT-3.5-Turbo to generate multiple explanatory rationales for each sample, guided by human-provided labels, targets, and rationales; \(3\) empirically optimizing the dataset&#x27;s class balance \(neutral vs. non-neutral\) and the number of explanations per sample via pilot experiments with a 7B-parameter LLM; and \(4\) creating the final corpus of over 52,000 samples, each containing the original text, a label, and three generated explanations.  \[翻译\] 作者构建了HateCOT，一个用于攻击性言论检测的大规模解释增强数据集。其方法包括：\(1\) 基于规模、标签多样性和定义与依据的可获得性等标准，筛选并合并 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The HateCOT dataset, comprising over 52,000 samples with GPT-3.5-generated and human-curated explanations, significantly enhances the generalization and explanation quality of open-source LLMs for offensive speech detection across diverse domains and tasks in zero-shot, few-shot, and K-shot fine-tuning settings. \[翻译\] HateCOT数据集包含超过52,000个带有GPT-3.5生成并经人工校验解释的样本，显著提升了开源大语言模型在零样本、少样本及K样本微调场景下，针对跨领域和跨任务的冒犯性言论检测的泛化能力和解释质量。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The dataset relies on GPT-3.5-Turbo for explanation generation, which may inherit its biases and limitations, and the human curation process, while enhancing quality, does not guarantee the elimination of all errors or subjective interpretations. \[翻译\] 该数据集依赖GPT-3.5-Turbo生成解释，可能继承其偏见和局限性，且人工审核过程虽提升了质量，但无法保证完全消除所有错误或主观解释。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The widespread use of social media necessitates reliable detection of offensive content to mitigate its harmful effects. However, existing models often fail to generalize across datasets due to varying definitions and labeling of "offensive content," and they typically lack transparency in their decision-making. This paper aims to address these challenges by introducing HateCOT, a large-scale, explanation-enhanced dataset designed to improve the generalization and explainability of offensive speech detection using large language models. \[翻译\] 社交媒体的广泛使用使得可靠检测有害内容以减轻其负面影响变得至关重要。然而，现有模型由于对“有害内容”的定义和标注标准不一，往往难以在不同数据集间泛化，并且通常缺乏决策透明度。本文旨在通过引入HateCOT这一大规模、解释增强的数据集来解决这些挑战，该数据集旨在利用大语言模型提升有害言论检测的泛化能力和可解释性。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] HateCOT introduces a large-scale, explanation-enhanced dataset for offensive speech detection, generated by GPT-3.5-Turbo and human-curated, to improve the cross-dataset generalization and explainability of open-source LLMs through pretraining and few-shot fine-tuning. \[翻译\] HateCOT提出了一个大规模、解释增强的冒犯性言论检测数据集，该数据集由GPT-3.5-Turbo生成并经人工校验，旨在通过预训练和少样本微调提升开源大语言模型的跨数据集泛化能力和可解释性。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors construct HateCOT, a large-scale explanation-enhanced dataset for offensive speech detection. The method involves: \(1\) selecting and merging eight existing offensive speech datasets based on criteria of size, label diversity, and availability of definitions and rationales; \(2\) using GPT-3.5-Turbo to generate multiple explanatory rationales for each sample, guided by human-provided labels, targets, and rationales; \(3\) empirically optimizing the dataset's class balance \(neutral vs. non-neutral\) and the number of explanations per sample via pilot experiments with a 7B-parameter LLM; and \(4\) creating the final corpus of over 52,000 samples, each containing the original text, a label, and three generated explanations.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>作者构建了HateCOT，一个用于攻击性言论检测的大规模解释增强数据集。其方法包括：\(1\) 基于规模、标签多样性和定义与依据的可获得性等标准，筛选并合并<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The HateCOT dataset, comprising over 52,000 samples with GPT-3.5-generated and human-curated explanations, significantly enhances the generalization and explanation quality of open-source LLMs for offensive speech detection across diverse domains and tasks in zero-shot, few-shot, and K-shot fine-tuning settings. \[翻译\] HateCOT数据集包含超过52,000个带有GPT-3.5生成并经人工校验解释的样本，显著提升了开源大语言模型在零样本、少样本及K样本微调场景下，针对跨领域和跨任务的冒犯性言论检测的泛化能力和解释质量。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The dataset relies on GPT-3.5-Turbo for explanation generation, which may inherit its biases and limitations, and the human curation process, while enhancing quality, does not guarantee the elimination of all errors or subjective interpretations. \[翻译\] 该数据集依赖GPT-3.5-Turbo生成解释，可能继承其偏见和局限性，且人工审核过程虽提升了质量，但无法保证完全消除所有错误或主观解释。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【可解释】</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Findings%202025-blue)]()<br>[Enhancing LLM-based hatred and toxicity detection with meta-toxic knowledge graph](https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-acl.1270/) <br> Yibo Zhao, Jiapeng Zhu, Can Xu, Yao Liu, Xiang Li <br> 2025-07|\[AI generated\] MetaTox enhances LLM-based toxicity detection by constructing a meta-toxic knowledge graph to supplement domain-specific knowledge, addressing both false negatives and false positives. \[翻译\] MetaTox通过构建元毒性知识图谱来补充领域特定知识，从而增强基于LLM的毒性检测，同时解决假阴性和假阳性问题。|<div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;align-items:center"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/197228fd/MetaTox1.png"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/197228fd/MetaTox2.png"></div>| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper addresses two key limitations of using Large Language Models \(LLMs\) for online toxicity detection: their lack of domain-specific knowledge leading to false negatives, and their excessive sensitivity causing false positives that may restrict free speech. To overcome these challenges, it proposes a novel method that constructs and queries a meta-toxic knowledge graph to provide LLMs with accurate, relevant external knowledge, thereby enhancing detection performance and mitigating ethical risks. \[翻译\] 本文针对大语言模型（LLM）用于在线毒性检测时的两个关键局限展开研究：一是缺乏领域特定知识导致漏报，二是过度敏感导致误报并可能限制言论自由。为克服这些挑战，论文提出了一种新方法，通过构建并查询一个元毒性知识图谱，为LLM提供准确、相关的外部知识，从而提升检测性能并降低伦理风险。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] MetaTox introduces a meta-toxic knowledge graph to enhance LLM-based hate speech detection, addressing both false negatives \(lack of domain knowledge\) and false positives \(excessive sensitivity\). It constructs the graph via a three-step LLM pipeline \(rationale reasoning, triplet extraction, entity resolution\) and uses graph retrieval to inject precise toxic knowledge into the LLM&#x27;s reasoning process, improving performance especially in out-of-domain settings. \[翻译\] MetaTox 提出了一种元毒性知识图谱来增强基于大语言模型的仇恨言论检测，同时解决了假阴性（缺乏领域知识）和假阳性（过度敏感）问题。它通过一个三步的大语言模型流程（原理推理、三元组抽取、实体解析）构建图谱，并利用图谱检索将精确的毒性知识注入大语言模型的推理过程，尤其在域外场景下提升了性能。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] MetaTox is a training-free method that enhances LLM-based toxicity detection by constructing and querying a meta-toxic knowledge graph. It first builds the graph from toxic datasets using a three-step LLM-driven pipeline: rationale reasoning to explain toxicity, triplet extraction to capture toxic entities and relations, and entity resolution to merge similar elements. For detection, it queries the graph via entity extraction, node mapping, path retrieval, and ranking to retrieve relevant toxic triplets, which are then injected as prompts to guide the LLM&#x27;s judgment, thereby supplementing domain-specific knowledge and reducing false positives/negatives.  \[翻译\] MetaTox 是一种免训练方法，通过构建和查询元毒性知识图谱来增强基于大语言模型的毒性检测。它首先使用一个三步大语言模型驱动流程从毒性数据集中构建图谱：通过原理推理解释毒性、三元组提取捕获毒性实体与关系、实体解析合并相似元素。在检测时，该方法通过实体提取、节点映射、 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] MetaTox enhances LLM-based toxicity detection by constructing a meta-toxic knowledge graph to supplement domain-specific knowledge, effectively reducing both false negatives and false positives. The method demonstrates superior performance in out-of-domain settings, while revealing that smaller models can be more effective for in-domain detection. \[翻译\] MetaTox通过构建元毒性知识图谱来补充领域特定知识，从而增强了基于LLM的毒性检测，有效减少了假阴性和假阳性。该方法在域外场景中表现出优越性能，同时揭示了较小模型在域内检测中可能更有效。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The method relies on the quality of the automatically constructed knowledge graph, which may inherit biases or errors from the source datasets and the LLM extraction process. Its performance is also contingent on the effectiveness of the graph retrieval and ranking steps for unseen or highly implicit toxic content. \[翻译\] 该方法依赖于自动构建的知识图谱的质量，该图谱可能继承源数据集和LLM提取过程中的偏见或错误。其性能也取决于图谱检索和排序步骤对于未见或高度隐晦毒性内容的有效性。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper addresses two key limitations of using Large Language Models \(LLMs\) for online toxicity detection: their lack of domain-specific knowledge leading to false negatives, and their excessive sensitivity causing false positives that may restrict free speech. To overcome these challenges, it proposes a novel method that constructs and queries a meta-toxic knowledge graph to provide LLMs with accurate, relevant external knowledge, thereby enhancing detection performance and mitigating ethical risks. \[翻译\] 本文针对大语言模型（LLM）用于在线毒性检测时的两个关键局限展开研究：一是缺乏领域特定知识导致漏报，二是过度敏感导致误报并可能限制言论自由。为克服这些挑战，论文提出了一种新方法，通过构建并查询一个元毒性知识图谱，为LLM提供准确、相关的外部知识，从而提升检测性能并降低伦理风险。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] MetaTox introduces a meta-toxic knowledge graph to enhance LLM-based hate speech detection, addressing both false negatives \(lack of domain knowledge\) and false positives \(excessive sensitivity\). It constructs the graph via a three-step LLM pipeline \(rationale reasoning, triplet extraction, entity resolution\) and uses graph retrieval to inject precise toxic knowledge into the LLM's reasoning process, improving performance especially in out-of-domain settings. \[翻译\] MetaTox 提出了一种元毒性知识图谱来增强基于大语言模型的仇恨言论检测，同时解决了假阴性（缺乏领域知识）和假阳性（过度敏感）问题。它通过一个三步的大语言模型流程（原理推理、三元组抽取、实体解析）构建图谱，并利用图谱检索将精确的毒性知识注入大语言模型的推理过程，尤其在域外场景下提升了性能。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] MetaTox is a training-free method that enhances LLM-based toxicity detection by constructing and querying a meta-toxic knowledge graph. It first builds the graph from toxic datasets using a three-step LLM-driven pipeline: rationale reasoning to explain toxicity, triplet extraction to capture toxic entities and relations, and entity resolution to merge similar elements. For detection, it queries the graph via entity extraction, node mapping, path retrieval, and ranking to retrieve relevant toxic triplets, which are then injected as prompts to guide the LLM's judgment, thereby supplementing domain-specific knowledge and reducing false positives/negatives.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>MetaTox 是一种免训练方法，通过构建和查询元毒性知识图谱来增强基于大语言模型的毒性检测。它首先使用一个三步大语言模型驱动流程从毒性数据集中构建图谱：通过原理推理解释毒性、三元组提取捕获毒性实体与关系、实体解析合并相似元素。在检测时，该方法通过实体提取、节点映射、<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] MetaTox enhances LLM-based toxicity detection by constructing a meta-toxic knowledge graph to supplement domain-specific knowledge, effectively reducing both false negatives and false positives. The method demonstrates superior performance in out-of-domain settings, while revealing that smaller models can be more effective for in-domain detection. \[翻译\] MetaTox通过构建元毒性知识图谱来补充领域特定知识，从而增强了基于LLM的毒性检测，有效减少了假阴性和假阳性。该方法在域外场景中表现出优越性能，同时揭示了较小模型在域内检测中可能更有效。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The method relies on the quality of the automatically constructed knowledge graph, which may inherit biases or errors from the source datasets and the LLM extraction process. Its performance is also contingent on the effectiveness of the graph retrieval and ranking steps for unseen or highly implicit toxic content. \[翻译\] 该方法依赖于自动构建的知识图谱的质量，该图谱可能继承源数据集和LLM提取过程中的偏见或错误。其性能也取决于图谱检索和排序步骤对于未见或高度隐晦毒性内容的有效性。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【可解释】</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-The%202023%20Conference%20on%20Empirical%20Methods%20in%20Natural%20Language%20Processing-blue)]()<br>[HARE: Explainable hate speech detection with step-by-step reasoning](https://openreview.net/forum?id=gQUDsNE3Lh) <br> Yongjin Yang, Joonkee Kim, Yujin Kim, Namgyu Ho, James Thorne, Se-Young Yun <br> 2023-12-01|\[AI generated\] HARE bridges reasoning gaps in hate speech detection by using LLM-generated step-by-step rationales to supervise detection models, outperforming human-annotated baselines. \[翻译\] HARE通过使用LLM生成的逐步推理来填补仇恨言论检测中的推理空白，从而监督检测模型，其性能超越了基于人工标注的基线方法。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/887916be/HARE.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper addresses the critical challenge of detecting nuanced hate speech on social media, where existing benchmarks often contain significant reasoning gaps in their human-annotated explanations. These gaps hinder the effective supervision of detection models. To overcome this, the paper introduces the HARE framework, which leverages the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) to generate step-by-step rationales. This approach aims to fill the logical leaps in existing annotations, thereby providing more comprehensive supervision to improve both detection performance and the quality of model-generated explanations.  \[翻译\] 该论文旨在解决社交媒体上检测微妙仇恨言论的关键挑战，现有基准数据集中的人工标注解释往往存在显著的推理断层。这些断层阻碍了检测模型的有效监督。为此，论文提出了HARE框架，利用大语言模型（LLMs）的推理能力生成逐步推理的依据。该方法旨在填补现有标注中的逻辑跳跃，从而提供更全面的监督，以提升检测性能和模型生成解释的质量。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] HARE introduces a framework that leverages the reasoning capabilities of large language models \(LLMs\) to generate step-by-step rationales for hate speech detection, addressing the logical gaps in existing human-annotated explanations. This approach, implemented through two strategies \(Fr-HARE and Co-HARE\), enhances both detection performance and the quality of generated explanations, while improving model generalization to unseen datasets. \[翻译\] HARE 提出了一个框架，利用大语言模型（LLM）的推理能力为仇恨言论检测生成逐步推理的解释，以弥补现有人工标注解释中的逻辑断层。该框架通过两种策略（Fr-HARE 和 Co-HARE）实现，不仅提升了检测性能与解释生成质量，还增强了模型在未见数据集上的泛化能力。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] HARE introduces a framework that leverages the reasoning capabilities of large language models \(LLMs\) to generate step-by-step rationales for hate speech detection. It proposes two variants: Fr-HARE, which uses zero-shot chain-of-thought prompting to generate explanations from scratch, and Co-HARE, which incorporates existing human annotations \(target groups and implied statements\) into the prompts to guide the rationale generation. These LLM-generated rationales are then used to fine-tune smaller detection models, enabling them to both classify hate speech and produce more comprehensive, logically-grounded explanations.  \[翻译\] HARE 提出了一个框架，利用大语言模型（LLM）的推理能力为仇恨言论检测生成逐步推理的解释。它提出了两个变体：Fr-HARE 使用零样本思维链提示从头生成解释；Co-HARE 则将现有的人工标注（目标群体和隐含陈述）融入提示中以引导推理生成。这些由LLM生成的解释随后用于微调较小的检测模型， **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] HARE introduces a framework that leverages LLMs&#x27; reasoning capabilities to generate step-by-step explanations for hate speech detection, addressing gaps in existing human annotations. This approach, implemented as Fr-HARE and Co-HARE, consistently improves detection accuracy and explanation quality on benchmarks like SBIC and Implicit Hate, while also enhancing model generalization to unseen datasets. \[翻译\] HARE提出了一种利用大语言模型推理能力为仇恨言论检测生成逐步解释的框架，以弥补现有人工标注的不足。该方法以Fr-HARE和Co-HARE两种变体实现，在SBIC和Implicit Hate等基准测试上持续提升了检测准确性和解释质量，同时增强了模型对未见数据集的泛化能力。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The HARE framework relies on LLM-generated rationales, whose quality and consistency are contingent on the underlying LLM&#x27;s reasoning capabilities and may inherit its biases. The method&#x27;s effectiveness is also tied to the availability of human annotations for the Co-HARE variant, limiting its application to datasets without such resources. \[翻译\] HARE框架依赖于LLM生成的推理链，其质量和一致性受限于底层LLM的推理能力，并可能继承其偏见。此外，Co-HARE变体的有效性依赖于人工标注的存在，这限制了其在缺乏此类标注资源的数据集上的应用。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper addresses the critical challenge of detecting nuanced hate speech on social media, where existing benchmarks often contain significant reasoning gaps in their human-annotated explanations. These gaps hinder the effective supervision of detection models. To overcome this, the paper introduces the HARE framework, which leverages the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) to generate step-by-step rationales. This approach aims to fill the logical leaps in existing annotations, thereby providing more comprehensive supervision to improve both detection performance and the quality of model-generated explanations.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文旨在解决社交媒体上检测微妙仇恨言论的关键挑战，现有基准数据集中的人工标注解释往往存在显著的推理断层。这些断层阻碍了检测模型的有效监督。为此，论文提出了HARE框架，利用大语言模型（LLMs）的推理能力生成逐步推理的依据。该方法旨在填补现有标注中的逻辑跳跃，从而提供更全面的监督，以提升检测性能和模型生成解释的质量。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] HARE introduces a framework that leverages the reasoning capabilities of large language models \(LLMs\) to generate step-by-step rationales for hate speech detection, addressing the logical gaps in existing human-annotated explanations. This approach, implemented through two strategies \(Fr-HARE and Co-HARE\), enhances both detection performance and the quality of generated explanations, while improving model generalization to unseen datasets. \[翻译\] HARE 提出了一个框架，利用大语言模型（LLM）的推理能力为仇恨言论检测生成逐步推理的解释，以弥补现有人工标注解释中的逻辑断层。该框架通过两种策略（Fr-HARE 和 Co-HARE）实现，不仅提升了检测性能与解释生成质量，还增强了模型在未见数据集上的泛化能力。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] HARE introduces a framework that leverages the reasoning capabilities of large language models \(LLMs\) to generate step-by-step rationales for hate speech detection. It proposes two variants: Fr-HARE, which uses zero-shot chain-of-thought prompting to generate explanations from scratch, and Co-HARE, which incorporates existing human annotations \(target groups and implied statements\) into the prompts to guide the rationale generation. These LLM-generated rationales are then used to fine-tune smaller detection models, enabling them to both classify hate speech and produce more comprehensive, logically-grounded explanations.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>HARE 提出了一个框架，利用大语言模型（LLM）的推理能力为仇恨言论检测生成逐步推理的解释。它提出了两个变体：Fr-HARE 使用零样本思维链提示从头生成解释；Co-HARE 则将现有的人工标注（目标群体和隐含陈述）融入提示中以引导推理生成。这些由LLM生成的解释随后用于微调较小的检测模型，<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] HARE introduces a framework that leverages LLMs' reasoning capabilities to generate step-by-step explanations for hate speech detection, addressing gaps in existing human annotations. This approach, implemented as Fr-HARE and Co-HARE, consistently improves detection accuracy and explanation quality on benchmarks like SBIC and Implicit Hate, while also enhancing model generalization to unseen datasets. \[翻译\] HARE提出了一种利用大语言模型推理能力为仇恨言论检测生成逐步解释的框架，以弥补现有人工标注的不足。该方法以Fr-HARE和Co-HARE两种变体实现，在SBIC和Implicit Hate等基准测试上持续提升了检测准确性和解释质量，同时增强了模型对未见数据集的泛化能力。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The HARE framework relies on LLM-generated rationales, whose quality and consistency are contingent on the underlying LLM's reasoning capabilities and may inherit its biases. The method's effectiveness is also tied to the availability of human annotations for the Co-HARE variant, limiting its application to datasets without such resources. \[翻译\] HARE框架依赖于LLM生成的推理链，其质量和一致性受限于底层LLM的推理能力，并可能继承其偏见。此外，Co-HARE变体的有效性依赖于人工标注的存在，这限制了其在缺乏此类标注资源的数据集上的应用。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【可解释】</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202023-blue)]()<br>[Rule by example: Harnessing logical rules for explainable hate speech detection](https://aclanthology.org/2023.acl-long.22/) <br> Christopher Clarke, Matthew Hall, Gaurav Mittal, Ye Yu, Sandra Sajeev, Jason Mars, Mei Chen <br> 2023-07|\[AI generated\] Rule By Example: A hybrid approach that combines the interpretability of rule-based systems with the robustness of deep learning for hate speech detection. \[翻译\] Rule By Example：一种结合了基于规则系统的可解释性与深度学习鲁棒性的混合方法，用于仇恨言论检测。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] Rule-based hate speech detection is transparent but fragile, while deep learning models are robust but lack explainability. This paper aims to bridge this gap by proposing a method that combines the interpretability of logical rules with the generalization power of deep neural networks for explainable content moderation. \[翻译\] 基于规则的仇恨言论检测方法透明但脆弱，而深度学习模型鲁棒但缺乏可解释性。本文旨在弥合这一差距，提出一种将逻辑规则的可解释性与深度神经网络的泛化能力相结合的方法，以实现可解释的内容审核。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] Rule By Example \(RBE\) introduces a novel exemplar-based contrastive learning framework that integrates logical rules with deep learning for explainable hate speech detection. It employs a dual-encoder architecture to learn joint embeddings of text and rule exemplars, enabling rule-grounded predictions that enhance both performance and interpretability. \[翻译\] Rule By Example \(RBE\) 提出了一种新颖的基于范例的对比学习框架，将逻辑规则与深度学习相结合，用于可解释的仇恨言论检测。它采用双编码器架构来学习文本和规则范例的联合嵌入，实现了基于规则的可解释预测，从而同时提升了性能和可解释性。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] Rule By Example \(RBE\) is a novel exemplar-based contrastive learning framework for explainable hate speech detection. It employs a dual-encoder architecture, consisting of a rule encoder and a text encoder, to jointly learn embedding representations for both hateful content and the logical rules that govern them. Through a contrastive learning objective, the model learns to maximize the similarity between hateful text examples and the clusters of rule exemplars that correctly cover them, while minimizing similarity for benign examples incorrectly covered by rules. This approach enables &quot;rule-grounding,&quot; allowing predictions to be traced back to specific, interpretable logical rules and their exemplars, thereby providing explainability alongside competitive performance.  \[翻译\] Rule By Example \(RBE\) 是一种新颖的基于范例的对比学习框架，用于可解释的仇恨言论检测。它采用双编码器架构，包含一个规则编码器和一个文本编码器，共同学习仇恨内容及其所遵循的逻辑规则的嵌入表示。通过对比学习目标，该 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] Rule By Example \(RBE\) introduces a novel exemplar-based contrastive learning framework that effectively integrates the interpretability of logical rules with the robustness of deep learning for hate speech detection. It outperforms state-of-the-art classifiers across multiple datasets while providing explainable, rule-grounded predictions.  \[翻译\] Rule By Example \(RBE\) 提出了一种新颖的基于范例的对比学习框架，有效地将逻辑规则的可解释性与深度学习的鲁棒性相结合，用于仇恨言论检测。它在多个数据集上超越了最先进的分类器，同时提供可解释的、基于规则的预测。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The Rule By Example \(RBE\) framework requires a predefined ruleset and corresponding exemplars for training, which may limit its adaptability to emerging hate speech patterns not covered by existing rules. Additionally, the reliance on rule exemplars could introduce biases inherent in the rule construction process. \[翻译\] Rule By Example \(RBE\) 框架的训练依赖于预定义的规则集和对应的示例，这可能限制了其对现有规则未覆盖的新兴仇恨言论模式的适应性。此外，对规则示例的依赖可能引入规则构建过程中固有的偏见。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] Rule-based hate speech detection is transparent but fragile, while deep learning models are robust but lack explainability. This paper aims to bridge this gap by proposing a method that combines the interpretability of logical rules with the generalization power of deep neural networks for explainable content moderation. \[翻译\] 基于规则的仇恨言论检测方法透明但脆弱，而深度学习模型鲁棒但缺乏可解释性。本文旨在弥合这一差距，提出一种将逻辑规则的可解释性与深度神经网络的泛化能力相结合的方法，以实现可解释的内容审核。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] Rule By Example \(RBE\) introduces a novel exemplar-based contrastive learning framework that integrates logical rules with deep learning for explainable hate speech detection. It employs a dual-encoder architecture to learn joint embeddings of text and rule exemplars, enabling rule-grounded predictions that enhance both performance and interpretability. \[翻译\] Rule By Example \(RBE\) 提出了一种新颖的基于范例的对比学习框架，将逻辑规则与深度学习相结合，用于可解释的仇恨言论检测。它采用双编码器架构来学习文本和规则范例的联合嵌入，实现了基于规则的可解释预测，从而同时提升了性能和可解释性。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] Rule By Example \(RBE\) is a novel exemplar-based contrastive learning framework for explainable hate speech detection. It employs a dual-encoder architecture, consisting of a rule encoder and a text encoder, to jointly learn embedding representations for both hateful content and the logical rules that govern them. Through a contrastive learning objective, the model learns to maximize the similarity between hateful text examples and the clusters of rule exemplars that correctly cover them, while minimizing similarity for benign examples incorrectly covered by rules.
