The moderation level varies across platforms; even over time, it can evolve in a platform. For example, Parler, a fringe social media platform popular among conservative users, was known to have the least restrictive moderation policies, claiming to have open discussion spaces for their users. However, after linking the 2021 US Capitol Riots and the activity of some groups o"<br>}</div></details></div>|

##### Controversy Analysis (1 papers)

| Title & Info | Analogy Summary | Pipeline | Summary |
|:--| :---: | :----: | :---: |
|[![Star](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/gvrkiran/controversy-detection.svg?style=social&label=Star)](https://github.com/gvrkiran/controversy-detection) [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-World%20Wide%20Web-blue)]()<br>[A text and GNN based controversy detection method on social media](https://doi.org/10.1007/s11280-022-01116-0) <br> Samy Benslimane, Jér?me Azé, Sandra Bringay, Maximilien Servajean, Caroline Mollevi <br> 2023-03-01|To identify controversial topics from discussion inputs, the method aggregates historical comments into user node features to encode intrinsic user and network attributes, and subsequently applies GNN methods on the user-centric social network to achieve binary classification.<br>\[翻译\] 在争议话题识别中，输入是一段讨论，为了编码用户本身特征和用户网络特征，将用户历史评论聚合为用户节点特征，在以用户为节点的社交网络上，进行GNN方法，实现二分类。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/6d0fb44d/HRL-GCN.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** Existing controversy detection approaches often treat structural patterns and semantic content in isolation or rely solely on post-reply trees, neglecting the critical role of user entities and their interaction dynamics in driving social polarization. \[翻译\] 现有的争议检测方法通常将结构模式和语义内容割裂处理，或单纯依赖帖子回复树结构，忽视了用户实体及其交互动态在驱动社会极化中的关键作用。 **[innovation]** The study shifts focus from message-level dependencies to user-centric interaction graphs. A key innovation lies in aggregating individual comments into unified user features, which allows the model to encode network-level information and actor stances simultaneously within the graph structure. \[翻译\] 该研究将焦点从消息级依赖关系转移到了以用户为中心的交互图上。其主要创新在于将分散的评论聚合为统一的用户特征，这使得模型能够在图结构中同时编码网络层面的信息和行动者的立场。 **[method]** The framework constructs a user graph where node features are initialized with BERT-encoded textual features aggregated from user historical comments. Subsequently, two GNN methods—Hierarchical Representation Learning via differentiable pooling \(HRL-GCN\) or Attention-based Representation Learning \(ARL-GAT\)—are employed to capture structural patterns, ultimately performing graph-level binary classification. \[翻译\] 该框架构建了一个用户图，其中节点特征初始化为从用户历史评论中聚合的BERT编码文本特征。随后，利用通过可微池化实现的分层表示学习（HRL-GCN）或基于注意力的表示学习（ARL-GAT）两种GNN方法来捕捉结构模式，最终完成图级二分类。 **[conclusion/contribution]** Empirical evaluations on Reddit and Twitter datasets demonstrate that the hierarchical pooling strategy \(HRL-GCN\) achieves superior performance by effectively capturing community-level structures, validating that combining aggregated user semantics with interaction topology significantly outperforms structure-only baselines. \[翻译\] 在Reddit和Twitter数据集上的实证评估表明，分层池化策略（HRL-GCN）通过有效捕捉社区级结构实现了更优的性能，证实了结合聚合的用户语义与交互拓扑结构显著优于仅依赖基线的结构。 **[limitation/future]** Standard pre-trained language models perform suboptimally on noisy Twitter data without domain-specific fine-tuning. Additionally, the approach is currently limited to homogeneous graphs, suggesting that future work could explore heterogeneous graph modeling to integrate multiple interaction types. \[翻译\] 标准预训练语言模型在未经领域微调的情况下在噪声较大的Twitter数据上表现欠佳，以及仅限于同构图，未来可以探索异构图建模以整合多种交互类型。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** Existing controversy detection approaches often treat structural patterns and semantic content in isolation or rely solely on post-reply trees, neglecting the critical role of user entities and their interaction dynamics in driving social polarization.<br>\[翻译\] 现有的争议检测方法通常将结构模式和语义内容割裂处理，或单纯依赖帖子回复树结构，忽视了用户实体及其交互动态在驱动社会极化中的关键作用。<br>**[innovation]** The study shifts focus from message-level dependencies to user-centric interaction graphs. A key innovation lies in aggregating individual comments into unified user features, which allows the model to encode network-level information and actor stances simultaneously within the graph structure.<br>\[翻译\] 该研究将焦点从消息级依赖关系转移到了以用户为中心的交互图上。其主要创新在于将分散的评论聚合为统一的用户特征，这使得模型能够在图结构中同时编码网络层面的信息和行动者的立场。<br>**[method]** The framework constructs a user graph where node features are initialized with BERT-encoded textual features aggregated from user historical comments. Subsequently, two GNN methods—Hierarchical Representation Learning via differentiable pooling \(HRL-GCN\) or Attention-based Representation Learning \(ARL-GAT\)—are employed to capture structural patterns, ultimately performing graph-level binary classification.<br>\[翻译\] 该框架构建了一个用户图，其中节点特征初始化为从用户历史评论中聚合的BERT编码文本特征。随后，利用通过可微池化实现的分层表示学习（HRL-GCN）或基于注意力的表示学习（ARL-GAT）两种GNN方法来捕捉结构模式，最终完成图级二分类。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** Empirical evaluations on Reddit and Twitter datasets demonstrate that the hierarchical pooling strategy \(HRL-GCN\) achieves superior performance by effectively capturing community-level structures, validating that combining aggregated user semantics with interaction topology significantly outperforms structure-only baselines.<br>\[翻译\] 在Reddit和Twitter数据集上的实证评估表明，分层池化策略（HRL-GCN）通过有效捕捉社区级结构实现了更优的性能，证实了结合聚合的用户语义与交互拓扑结构显著优于仅依赖基线的结构。<br>**[limitation/future]** Standard pre-trained language models perform suboptimally on noisy Twitter data without domain-specific fine-tuning. Additionally, the approach is currently limited to homogeneous graphs, suggesting that future work could explore heterogeneous graph modeling to integrate multiple interaction types.<br>\[翻译\] 标准预训练语言模型在未经领域微调的情况下在噪声较大的Twitter数据上表现欠佳，以及仅限于同构图，未来可以探索异构图建模以整合多种交互类型。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">\[引用文\]To better capture the dynamics of social polarization in the field of controversial content identification, Benslimane et al. \(2023\) shift from content-based analysis to a user-centric perspective. They argue that the structural relationships between users and the users' inherent attributes are pivotal for understanding controversy. Unlike traditional methods that treat posts as isolated nodes, their approach constructs a user interaction graph where node representations are derived by aggregating the semantic content of each user’s historical comments. This design encodes complex network information and individual stances into a unified feature space. By employing GNN methods such as Hierarchical Graph Representation Learning \(HRL-GCN\), the model performs multi-layer aggregation on the graph to capture high-level community structures, demonstrating that the fusion of user features with social interaction patterns provides an effective mechanism for controversy identification in social media.<br>\[翻译\] 为了更好地在争议内容识别领域捕捉社会极化的动态，Benslimane等人 \(2023\) 的方法从基于内容的分析转变为以用户为中心视角。他们认为，用户之间的结构关系以及用户本身的属性对于理解争议至关重要。与将帖子视为孤立节点的传统方法不同，他们的方法构建了一个用户交互图，其中节点表示通过聚合每个用户历史评论的语义内容得到。这种设计将复杂的网络信息和个人立场编码到了统一的特征空间中。通过采用分层图表示学习（HRL-GCN）等GNN的方法，该模型对图进行多层聚合以捕捉高层级的社区结构，证明了用户特征与社交交互模式的融合为识别社交媒体中的争议识别提供了一种有效的机制。</div></details></div></div>|

##### Machine-Generated Content Detection (15 papers)

| Title & Info | Analogy Summary | Pipeline | Summary |
|:--| :---: | :----: | :---: |
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202024-blue)]()<br>[MAGE: Machine-generated text detection in the wild](https://aclanthology.org/2024.acl-long.3/) <br> Yafu Li, Qintong Li, Leyang Cui, Wei Bi, Zhilin Wang, Longyue Wang, Linyi Yang, Shuming Shi, Yue Zhang <br> 2024-08|\[AI generated\] Detecting machine-generated text is akin to finding a needle in a haystack, as LLMs produce content increasingly indistinguishable from human writing. \[翻译\] 检测机器生成文本如同大海捞针，因为大语言模型生成的内容与人类写作的界限日益模糊。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper addresses the challenge of detecting machine-generated text in real-world scenarios, where detectors must handle texts from diverse domains and unknown language models without prior knowledge of their sources. It aims to evaluate the feasibility of existing detection methods in such open and out-of-distribution settings.  \[翻译\] 该论文旨在解决现实场景中机器生成文本的检测难题，即检测器必须在未知文本来源（领域或生成模型）的情况下，处理来自多样化领域和不同语言模型的文本。其目标是评估现有检测方法在此类开放且分布外环境下的可行性。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces MAGE, a comprehensive testbed for evaluating machine-generated text detection in real-world scenarios. Its key innovation lies in moving beyond domain- or model-specific evaluations by constructing a diverse benchmark from various human writings and texts generated by different LLMs. This approach reveals the significant challenge detectors face with out-of-distribution texts, while also demonstrating the feasibility of practical application through a top-performing detector&#x27;s ability to identify 84.12% of such texts from a novel LLM.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了MAGE，一个用于在真实场景中评估机器生成文本检测的综合测试平台。其主要创新点在于超越了针对特定领域或模型的评估，通过整合来自多样化人类写作和不同大语言模型生成文本构建了一个全面的基准。这种方法揭示了检测器在处理分布外文本时面临的重大挑战，同时通过表现最佳的检测器能够识别出来自新大语言模型的84.12%的此类文本，证明了实际应用的可行性。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The study constructs a comprehensive testbed by collecting texts from diverse human-written sources and deepfake texts generated by various LLMs. It then empirically evaluates the performance of mainstream detection methods on this testbed, focusing on their ability to generalize across different domains and unseen LLMs. \[翻译\] 该研究通过收集来自多样化人类写作来源的文本以及由不同大语言模型生成的深度伪造文本来构建一个全面的测试平台。随后，它在该平台上对主流检测方法进行实证评估，重点关注这些方法在不同领域和未见过的模型上的泛化能力。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper MAGE constructs a comprehensive testbed for machine-generated text detection, evaluating methods across diverse domains and LLMs. It reveals significant challenges in out-of-distribution scenarios, where linguistic distinctions diminish, yet demonstrates the practical feasibility with a top detector achieving 84.12% accuracy on texts from a novel LLM. \[翻译\] 论文MAGE构建了一个用于机器生成文本检测的综合测试平台，评估了跨不同领域和LLM的方法。它揭示了在分布外场景中的显著挑战，即语言差异减小，但通过顶级检测器在新LLM生成的文本上达到84.12%的准确率，证明了实际应用的可行性。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study&#x27;s primary limitation is its evaluation scope, which remains confined to a controlled testbed despite its diversity. The detector&#x27;s performance in truly open-world, real-time social media environments, where texts are mixed with multimedia and evolve rapidly, remains unverified. Furthermore, the diminishing linguistic gap between human and machine-generated text suggests that purely linguistic detection methods may face a fundamental ceiling, necessitating exploration of non-linguistic features or hybrid approaches.  \[翻译\] 该研究的主要局限在于其评估范围，尽管构建了多样化的测试集，但仍局限于受控环境。检测器在真正开放、实时且文本与多媒体混杂、快速演变的社交媒体环境中的性能尚未得到验证。此外，人类与机器生成文本之间语言差异的缩小表明，纯语言特征的检测方法可能面临根本性的性能上限，需要探索非语言特征或混合方法。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper addresses the challenge of detecting machine-generated text in real-world scenarios, where detectors must handle texts from diverse domains and unknown language models without prior knowledge of their sources. It aims to evaluate the feasibility of existing detection methods in such open and out-of-distribution settings.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文旨在解决现实场景中机器生成文本的检测难题，即检测器必须在未知文本来源（领域或生成模型）的情况下，处理来自多样化领域和不同语言模型的文本。其目标是评估现有检测方法在此类开放且分布外环境下的可行性。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces MAGE, a comprehensive testbed for evaluating machine-generated text detection in real-world scenarios. Its key innovation lies in moving beyond domain- or model-specific evaluations by constructing a diverse benchmark from various human writings and texts generated by different LLMs. This approach reveals the significant challenge detectors face with out-of-distribution texts, while also demonstrating the feasibility of practical application through a top-performing detector's ability to identify 84.12% of such texts from a novel LLM.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了MAGE，一个用于在真实场景中评估机器生成文本检测的综合测试平台。其主要创新点在于超越了针对特定领域或模型的评估，通过整合来自多样化人类写作和不同大语言模型生成文本构建了一个全面的基准。这种方法揭示了检测器在处理分布外文本时面临的重大挑战，同时通过表现最佳的检测器能够识别出来自新大语言模型的84.12%的此类文本，证明了实际应用的可行性。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The study constructs a comprehensive testbed by collecting texts from diverse human-written sources and deepfake texts generated by various LLMs. It then empirically evaluates the performance of mainstream detection methods on this testbed, focusing on their ability to generalize across different domains and unseen LLMs. \[翻译\] 该研究通过收集来自多样化人类写作来源的文本以及由不同大语言模型生成的深度伪造文本来构建一个全面的测试平台。随后，它在该平台上对主流检测方法进行实证评估，重点关注这些方法在不同领域和未见过的模型上的泛化能力。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper MAGE constructs a comprehensive testbed for machine-generated text detection, evaluating methods across diverse domains and LLMs. It reveals significant challenges in out-of-distribution scenarios, where linguistic distinctions diminish, yet demonstrates the practical feasibility with a top detector achieving 84.12% accuracy on texts from a novel LLM. \[翻译\] 论文MAGE构建了一个用于机器生成文本检测的综合测试平台，评估了跨不同领域和LLM的方法。它揭示了在分布外场景中的显著挑战，即语言差异减小，但通过顶级检测器在新LLM生成的文本上达到84.12%的准确率，证明了实际应用的可行性。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study's primary limitation is its evaluation scope, which remains confined to a controlled testbed despite its diversity. The detector's performance in truly open-world, real-time social media environments, where texts are mixed with multimedia and evolve rapidly, remains unverified. Furthermore, the diminishing linguistic gap between human and machine-generated text suggests that purely linguistic detection methods may face a fundamental ceiling, necessitating exploration of non-linguistic features or hybrid approaches.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该研究的主要局限在于其评估范围，尽管构建了多样化的测试集，但仍局限于受控环境。检测器在真正开放、实时且文本与多媒体混杂、快速演变的社交媒体环境中的性能尚未得到验证。此外，人类与机器生成文本之间语言差异的缩小表明，纯语言特征的检测方法可能面临根本性的性能上限，需要探索非语言特征或混合方法。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Machine-generated Content Detection"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "MAGE: Machine-generated text detection in the wild",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-08"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Large language models \(LLMs\) have achieved human-level text generation, emphasizing the need for effective deepfake text detection to mitigate risks like the spread of fake news and plagiarism. Existing research has been constrained by evaluating detection methods o specific domains or particular language models. In practical scenarios, however, the detector faces texts from various domains or LLMs without knowing their sources. To this end, we build a comprehensive testbed by gathering texts fr"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Findings%202024-blue)]()<br>[A survey on detection of LLMs-generated content](https://aclanthology.org/2024.findings-emnlp.572/) <br> Xianjun Yang, Liangming Pan, Xuandong Zhao, Haifeng Chen, Linda Ruth Petzold, William Yang Wang, Wei Cheng <br> 2024-11|\[AI generated\] Detecting LLM-generated content is akin to distinguishing synthetic diamonds from natural ones, requiring specialized tools to identify subtle, model-specific patterns. \[翻译\] 检测大语言模型生成内容类似于区分合成钻石与天然钻石，需要专门工具来识别细微的、模型特有的模式。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The proliferation of advanced large language models \(LLMs\) has led to a significant increase in synthetic content generation, posing challenges across media, cybersecurity, public discourse, and education. Consequently, the ability to detect LLM-generated content has become critically important. This survey aims to provide a comprehensive overview of existing detection strategies and benchmarks, analyzing their differences and identifying key challenges and future directions to advocate for more robust and adaptable detection models.  \[翻译\] 先进大语言模型（LLM）的激增导致了合成内容生成的显著增加，给媒体、网络安全、公共话语和教育等领域带来了挑战。因此，检测LLM生成内容的能力变得至关重要。本综述旨在全面概述现有的检测策略和基准，分析其差异，并识别关键挑战与未来方向，以倡导开发更鲁棒、更自适应的检测模型。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This survey is the first comprehensive work to systematically review detection methods for LLM-generated content, establishing a foundational taxonomy and benchmarking framework for this emerging field. It advocates for a multi-faceted defense strategy to address the evolving capabilities of generative models.  \[翻译\] 本综述是首个系统梳理大语言模型生成内容检测方法的综合性工作，为该新兴领域建立了基础的分类学和基准框架。它倡导采用多层面的防御策略以应对生成模型不断演进的能力。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews existing detection strategies for LLM-generated content, categorizing them into distinct methodological families. It examines and compares these approaches, highlighting their respective strengths, limitations, and underlying principles. The work advocates for the development of more robust and adaptable detection models to counter the evolving capabilities of advanced LLMs.  \[翻译\] 本综述系统性地回顾了现有的大语言模型生成内容检测策略，并将其归类为不同的方法体系。它审视并比较了这些方法，突出了它们各自的优势、局限性和基本原理。该工作倡导开发更鲁棒、适应性更强的检测模型，以应对先进大语言模型不断发展的能力。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This survey provides a comprehensive overview of the current landscape, key challenges, and future directions in detecting LLM-generated content, highlighting the necessity for robust, adaptable, and multifaceted detection strategies to counter the evolving capabilities of generative models.  \[翻译\] 本综述全面概述了检测大语言模型生成内容的当前格局、关键挑战与未来方向，强调了需要开发鲁棒、自适应且多层面的检测策略，以应对生成模型不断演进的能力。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] Current detection methods primarily rely on statistical or stylistic features, which may become less effective as LLMs evolve to generate more human-like content. The lack of standardized, large-scale, and multi-domain benchmarks further hinders the development and fair comparison of robust detectors. Additionally, most approaches are vulnerable to adversarial attacks and often fail to generalize across different LLMs, languages, and content types.  \[翻译\] 当前的检测方法主要依赖于统计或风格特征，随着大语言模型生成内容越来越类人，这些方法的有效性可能会降低。缺乏标准化、大规模、多领域的基准测试，进一步阻碍了鲁棒检测器的开发和公平比较。此外，大多数方法容易受到对抗性攻击，并且通常难以在不同的模型、语言和内容类型之间泛化。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The proliferation of advanced large language models \(LLMs\) has led to a significant increase in synthetic content generation, posing challenges across media, cybersecurity, public discourse, and education. Consequently, the ability to detect LLM-generated content has become critically important. This survey aims to provide a comprehensive overview of existing detection strategies and benchmarks, analyzing their differences and identifying key challenges and future directions to advocate for more robust and adaptable detection models.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>先进大语言模型（LLM）的激增导致了合成内容生成的显著增加，给媒体、网络安全、公共话语和教育等领域带来了挑战。因此，检测LLM生成内容的能力变得至关重要。本综述旨在全面概述现有的检测策略和基准，分析其差异，并识别关键挑战与未来方向，以倡导开发更鲁棒、更自适应的检测模型。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This survey is the first comprehensive work to systematically review detection methods for LLM-generated content, establishing a foundational taxonomy and benchmarking framework for this emerging field. It advocates for a multi-faceted defense strategy to address the evolving capabilities of generative models.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述是首个系统梳理大语言模型生成内容检测方法的综合性工作，为该新兴领域建立了基础的分类学和基准框架。它倡导采用多层面的防御策略以应对生成模型不断演进的能力。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews existing detection strategies for LLM-generated content, categorizing them into distinct methodological families. It examines and compares these approaches, highlighting their respective strengths, limitations, and underlying principles. The work advocates for the development of more robust and adaptable detection models to counter the evolving capabilities of advanced LLMs.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述系统性地回顾了现有的大语言模型生成内容检测策略，并将其归类为不同的方法体系。它审视并比较了这些方法，突出了它们各自的优势、局限性和基本原理。该工作倡导开发更鲁棒、适应性更强的检测模型，以应对先进大语言模型不断发展的能力。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This survey provides a comprehensive overview of the current landscape, key challenges, and future directions in detecting LLM-generated content, highlighting the necessity for robust, adaptable, and multifaceted detection strategies to counter the evolving capabilities of generative models.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述全面概述了检测大语言模型生成内容的当前格局、关键挑战与未来方向，强调了需要开发鲁棒、自适应且多层面的检测策略，以应对生成模型不断演进的能力。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] Current detection methods primarily rely on statistical or stylistic features, which may become less effective as LLMs evolve to generate more human-like content. The lack of standardized, large-scale, and multi-domain benchmarks further hinders the development and fair comparison of robust detectors. Additionally, most approaches are vulnerable to adversarial attacks and often fail to generalize across different LLMs, languages, and content types.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>当前的检测方法主要依赖于统计或风格特征，随着大语言模型生成内容越来越类人，这些方法的有效性可能会降低。缺乏标准化、大规模、多领域的基准测试，进一步阻碍了鲁棒检测器的开发和公平比较。此外，大多数方法容易受到对抗性攻击，并且通常难以在不同的模型、语言和内容类型之间泛化。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Machine-generated Content Detection"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "A survey on detection of LLMs-generated content",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-11"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "The burgeoning capabilities of advanced large language models \(LLMs\) such as ChatGPT have led to an increase in synthetic content generation with implications across a variety of sectors, including media, cybersecurity, public discourse, and education. As such, the ability to detect LLMs-generated content has become of paramount importance. We aim to provide a detailed overview of existing detection strategies and benchmarks, scrutinizing their differences and identifying key challenges and pros"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202025-blue)]()<br>[MultiSocial: Multilingual benchmark of machine-generated text detection of social-media texts](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.36/) <br> Dominik Macko, Jakub Kopál, Robert Moro, Ivan Srba <br> 2025-07|\[AI generated\] A multilingual benchmark for detecting machine-generated social media texts, addressing the gap in short, informal content across platforms. \[翻译\] 一个用于检测机器生成社交媒体文本的多语言基准，填补了跨平台短文本、非正式内容检测的空白。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper addresses the gap in machine-generated text detection for short, informal social-media texts across multiple languages and platforms, where existing research and benchmarks are predominantly focused on English and longer formal texts. \[翻译\] 该论文旨在填补针对简短、非正式社交媒体文本的多语言机器生成内容检测的研究空白，现有研究和基准主要集中在英语及较长的正式文本上。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces MultiSocial, the first multilingual \(22 languages\) and multi-platform \(5 social media platforms\) benchmark dataset for machine-generated text detection specifically tailored to the social-media domain, addressing a gap in existing research which primarily focuses on English and longer, formal texts. \[翻译\]该论文提出了MultiSocial，这是首个针对社交媒体领域机器生成文本检测的多语言（22种语言）和多平台（5个社交媒体平台）基准数据集，弥补了现有研究主要集中于英语及较长、正式文本的空白。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors constructed the MultiSocial dataset by collecting human-written texts from five social media platforms and generating corresponding machine-written texts using seven multilingual LLMs. They then benchmarked existing detection methods in both zero-shot and fine-tuned settings. \[翻译\] 作者通过从五个社交媒体平台收集人类撰写的文本，并使用七个多语言大语言模型生成对应的机器撰写文本，构建了MultiSocial数据集。随后，他们在零样本和微调两种设置下对现有检测方法进行了基准测试。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces MultiSocial, the first multilingual and multi-platform benchmark dataset for detecting machine-generated social media texts. It demonstrates that fine-tuned detectors can effectively adapt to the unique characteristics of social media content, and that the choice of social media platform for training data significantly impacts detection performance. \[翻译\] 该论文提出了首个用于检测机器生成社交媒体文本的多语言、多平台基准数据集MultiSocial。研究表明，经过微调的检测器能有效适应社交媒体内容的独特特征，且训练数据所选的社交媒体平台对检测性能有显著影响。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The benchmark is limited to 22 languages and 5 social media platforms, and the detection performance may vary across different languages and platforms. The dataset primarily focuses on short-form social media texts, and its applicability to longer or more formal text genres requires further validation. \[翻译\]该基准仅限于22种语言和5个社交媒体平台，检测性能在不同语言和平台间可能存在差异。数据集主要关注短文本社交媒体内容，其对于更长或更正式文本类型的适用性仍需进一步验证。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper addresses the gap in machine-generated text detection for short, informal social-media texts across multiple languages and platforms, where existing research and benchmarks are predominantly focused on English and longer formal texts. \[翻译\] 该论文旨在填补针对简短、非正式社交媒体文本的多语言机器生成内容检测的研究空白，现有研究和基准主要集中在英语及较长的正式文本上。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces MultiSocial, the first multilingual \(22 languages\) and multi-platform \(5 social media platforms\) benchmark dataset for machine-generated text detection specifically tailored to the social-media domain, addressing a gap in existing research which primarily focuses on English and longer, formal texts. \[翻译\]该论文提出了MultiSocial，这是首个针对社交媒体领域机器生成文本检测的多语言（22种语言）和多平台（5个社交媒体平台）基准数据集，弥补了现有研究主要集中于英语及较长、正式文本的空白。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors constructed the MultiSocial dataset by collecting human-written texts from five social media platforms and generating corresponding machine-written texts using seven multilingual LLMs. They then benchmarked existing detection methods in both zero-shot and fine-tuned settings. \[翻译\] 作者通过从五个社交媒体平台收集人类撰写的文本，并使用七个多语言大语言模型生成对应的机器撰写文本，构建了MultiSocial数据集。随后，他们在零样本和微调两种设置下对现有检测方法进行了基准测试。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces MultiSocial, the first multilingual and multi-platform benchmark dataset for detecting machine-generated social media texts. It demonstrates that fine-tuned detectors can effectively adapt to the unique characteristics of social media content, and that the choice of social media platform for training data significantly impacts detection performance. \[翻译\] 该论文提出了首个用于检测机器生成社交媒体文本的多语言、多平台基准数据集MultiSocial。研究表明，经过微调的检测器能有效适应社交媒体内容的独特特征，且训练数据所选的社交媒体平台对检测性能有显著影响。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The benchmark is limited to 22 languages and 5 social media platforms, and the detection performance may vary across different languages and platforms. The dataset primarily focuses on short-form social media texts, and its applicability to longer or more formal text genres requires further validation. \[翻译\]该基准仅限于22种语言和5个社交媒体平台，检测性能在不同语言和平台间可能存在差异。数据集主要关注短文本社交媒体内容，其对于更长或更正式文本类型的适用性仍需进一步验证。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Machine-generated Content Detection"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "MultiSocial: Multilingual benchmark of machine-generated text detection of social-media texts",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Recent LLMs are able to generate high-quality multilingual texts, indistinguishable for humans from authentic human-written ones. Research in machine-generated text detection is however mostly focused on the English language and longer texts, such as news articles, scientific papers or student essays. Social-media texts are usually much shorter and often feature informal language, grammatical errors, or distinct linguistic items \(e.g., emoticons, hashtags\). There is a gap in studying the ability"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202025-blue)]()<br>[Are we in the AI-generated text world already? Quantifying and monitoring AIGT on social media](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.1120/) <br> Zhen Sun, Zongmin Zhang, Xinyue Shen, Ziyi Zhang, Yule Liu, Michael Backes, Yang Zhang, Xinlei He <br> 2025-07|\[AI generated\] This paper quantifies AI-generated text prevalence on social media by building a detection benchmark and applying the best detector to track its growth across platforms. \[翻译\] 本文通过构建检测基准并应用最佳检测器追踪跨平台增长，量化了社交媒体上AI生成文本的普遍性。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper aims to quantify the prevalence and monitor the evolution of AI-Generated Texts \(AIGTs\) on major social media platforms, addressing a critical gap in understanding their real-world impact on public opinion and misinformation.  \[翻译\] 本文旨在量化主要社交媒体平台上AI生成文本的普遍性并监测其演变，以解决在理解其对公众舆论和虚假信息的现实影响方面的一个关键空白。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper introduces a comprehensive framework for quantifying and monitoring AI-generated text \(AIGT\) on social media. Its key innovations include constructing a large-scale, multi-platform social media dataset \(SM-D\) and a diverse benchmark \(AIGTBench\) for training and evaluating detectors. It identifies a high-performing detector \(OSM-Det\) and applies it to provide the first longitudinal, platform-specific quantification of AIGT prevalence, revealing significant growth on Medium and Quora but slower adoption on Reddit. The work further pioneers a comparative analysis of AIGT versus human content across linguistic, topical, and engagement dimensions.  \[翻译\] 本文提出了一个量化与监测社交媒体上AI生成文本（AIGT）的综合框架。其核心创新点在于构建了一个大规模、多平台的社交媒体数据集（SM-D）和一个用于训练与评估检测器的多样化基准（AIGTBench）。研究识别出高性能检测器（OSM-Det），并首次应用其提供了AIGT流行度的 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The study constructs a large-scale social media dataset \(SM-D\) and a benchmark \(AIGTBench\) to train and evaluate AI-generated text detectors. It identifies the best-performing detector \(OSM-Det\) and applies it to quantify the prevalence of AI-generated content across platforms over time using the AI Attribution Rate \(AAR\) metric.  \[翻译\] 该研究构建了一个大规模社交媒体数据集（SM-D）和一个基准测试集（AIGTBench）来训练和评估AI生成文本检测器。它确定了性能最佳的检测器（OSM-Det），并使用AI归因率（AAR）指标将其应用于量化不同平台上AI生成内容随时间的流行程度。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper quantifies the prevalence of AI-Generated Text \(AIGT\) on major social media platforms, revealing a significant and rapid increase on Medium and Quora, while growth on Reddit remains slower. It establishes a comprehensive benchmark \(AIGTBench\) and identifies a high-performing detector \(OSM-Det\) for this task. The analysis further demonstrates that AIGTs exhibit distinct linguistic, topical, and engagement patterns compared to human-written content.  \[翻译\] 该论文量化了主流社交媒体平台上AI生成文本的普遍性，揭示了其在Medium和Quora上显著且快速的增长，而在Reddit上的增长则较为缓慢。研究建立了一个全面的基准数据集并确定了一个高性能的检测器。进一步的分析表明，AI生成文本在语言模式、话题分布和参与度等方面与人类撰写的内容存在显著差异。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study&#x27;s primary limitation lies in its reliance on a single detector \(OSM-Det\) for the large-scale quantification of AIGTs across platforms, whose performance directly influences the reported AI Attribution Rates \(AAR\). The temporal analysis is constrained by the dataset&#x27;s timeframe \(Jan 2022–Oct 2024\), and the findings on platform-specific AIGT growth may not generalize to other social media ecosystems. Furthermore, the analysis of differences between AIGTs and human-written texts, while insightful, is correlational and does not establish causal mechanisms.  \[翻译\] 该研究的主要局限在于其依赖单一检测器（OSM-Det）对跨平台AI生成文本进行大规模量化，该检测器的性能直接影响所报告的AI归因率。时间序列分析受限于数据集的时间范围（2022年1月至2024年10月），且关于平台特定AIGT增长的发现可能无法推广到其他社交媒体生态。此外，对AIGT与人类撰写文本">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper aims to quantify the prevalence and monitor the evolution of AI-Generated Texts \(AIGTs\) on major social media platforms, addressing a critical gap in understanding their real-world impact on public opinion and misinformation.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文旨在量化主要社交媒体平台上AI生成文本的普遍性并监测其演变，以解决在理解其对公众舆论和虚假信息的现实影响方面的一个关键空白。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper introduces a comprehensive framework for quantifying and monitoring AI-generated text \(AIGT\) on social media. Its key innovations include constructing a large-scale, multi-platform social media dataset \(SM-D\) and a diverse benchmark \(AIGTBench\) for training and evaluating detectors. It identifies a high-performing detector \(OSM-Det\) and applies it to provide the first longitudinal, platform-specific quantification of AIGT prevalence, revealing significant growth on Medium and Quora but slower adoption on Reddit. The work further pioneers a comparative analysis of AIGT versus human content across linguistic, topical, and engagement dimensions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文提出了一个量化与监测社交媒体上AI生成文本（AIGT）的综合框架。其核心创新点在于构建了一个大规模、多平台的社交媒体数据集（SM-D）和一个用于训练与评估检测器的多样化基准（AIGTBench）。研究识别出高性能检测器（OSM-Det），并首次应用其提供了AIGT流行度的<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The study constructs a large-scale social media dataset \(SM-D\) and a benchmark \(AIGTBench\) to train and evaluate AI-generated text detectors. It identifies the best-performing detector \(OSM-Det\) and applies it to quantify the prevalence of AI-generated content across platforms over time using the AI Attribution Rate \(AAR\) metric.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该研究构建了一个大规模社交媒体数据集（SM-D）和一个基准测试集（AIGTBench）来训练和评估AI生成文本检测器。它确定了性能最佳的检测器（OSM-Det），并使用AI归因率（AAR）指标将其应用于量化不同平台上AI生成内容随时间的流行程度。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper quantifies the prevalence of AI-Generated Text \(AIGT\) on major social media platforms, revealing a significant and rapid increase on Medium and Quora, while growth on Reddit remains slower. It establishes a comprehensive benchmark \(AIGTBench\) and identifies a high-performing detector \(OSM-Det\) for this task. The analysis further demonstrates that AIGTs exhibit distinct linguistic, topical, and engagement patterns compared to human-written content.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文量化了主流社交媒体平台上AI生成文本的普遍性，揭示了其在Medium和Quora上显著且快速的增长，而在Reddit上的增长则较为缓慢。研究建立了一个全面的基准数据集并确定了一个高性能的检测器。进一步的分析表明，AI生成文本在语言模式、话题分布和参与度等方面与人类撰写的内容存在显著差异。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study's primary limitation lies in its reliance on a single detector \(OSM-Det\) for the large-scale quantification of AIGTs across platforms, whose performance directly influences the reported AI Attribution Rates \(AAR\). The temporal analysis is constrained by the dataset's timeframe \(Jan 2022–Oct 2024\), and the findings on platform-specific AIGT growth may not generalize to other social media ecosystems. Furthermore, the analysis of differences between AIGTs and human-written texts, while insightful, is correlational and does not establish causal mechanisms.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该研究的主要局限在于其依赖单一检测器（OSM-Det）对跨平台AI生成文本进行大规模量化，该检测器的性能直接影响所报告的AI归因率。时间序列分析受限于数据集的时间范围（2022年1月至2024年10月），且关于平台特定AIGT增长的发现可能无法推广到其他社交媒体生态。此外，对AIGT与人类撰写文本</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Machine-generated Content Detection",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Are we in the AI-generated text world already? Quantifying and monitoring AIGT on social media",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Social media platforms are experiencing a growing presence of AI-Generated Texts \(AIGTs\). However, the misuse of AIGTs could have profound implications for public opinion, such as spreading misinformation and manipulating narratives. Despite its importance, it remains unclear how prevalent AIGTs are on social media. To address this gap, this paper aims to quantify and monitor the AIGTs on online social media platforms. We first collect a dataset \(SM-D\) with around 2.4M posts from 3 major social "<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Findings%202024-blue)]()<br>[Probing the uniquely identifiable linguistic patterns of conversational AI agents](https://aclanthology.org/2024.findings-acl.274/) <br> Iqra Zahid, Tharindu Madusanka, Riza Batista-Navarro, Youcheng Sun <br> 2024-08|\[AI generated\] This study treats different conversational AI agents as distinct authors, using authorship attribution techniques to identify their unique linguistic fingerprints. \[翻译\] 该研究将不同的对话式AI智能体视为不同的作者，利用作者归属技术来识别其独特的语言指纹。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper investigates the existence of uniquely identifiable linguistic patterns \(UILPs\) across different conversational AI agents \(CAAs\). It aims to move beyond simple human-vs-machine detection by applying authorship attribution techniques to distinguish between specific AI models based on their textual outputs, establishing a baseline for this refined task.  \[翻译\] 本文旨在探究不同对话式AI代理（CAA）之间是否存在唯一可识别的语言模式（UILP）。其研究动机超越了简单的人机文本区分，转而应用作者归属技术，根据文本输出来区分特定的AI模型，从而为这一更精细的任务建立基线。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This work innovatively applies authorship attribution techniques to distinguish between different conversational AI agents \(CAAs\) rather than just humans vs. machines. It constructs unique linguistic profiles for CAAs, achieving high attribution accuracy and identifying their most informative features, establishing a baseline for CAA authorship analysis.  \[翻译\] 本研究创新性地将作者归属技术应用于区分不同的对话式AI智能体，而非仅区分人类与机器生成内容。它为多个CAAs构建了独特的语言特征档案，实现了高精度的归属判定，并识别出其最具信息量的特征，为CAA的作者归属分析建立了基线。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The study employs authorship attribution techniques to identify uniquely identifiable linguistic patterns \(UILPs\) in conversational AI agents \(CAAs\). It constructs linguistic profiles for five CAAs and uses principal component analysis \(PCA\) to extract the top 100 most informative features for each model, enabling high-accuracy attribution based on original and style-specified texts.  \[翻译\] 该研究采用作者归属技术来识别对话式AI代理（CAA）中唯一可识别的语言模式（UILP）。它为五个CAA构建了语言特征档案，并使用主成分分析（PCA）为每个模型提取了前100个最具信息量的特征，从而能够基于原始文本和指定风格的文本实现高精度的模型归属。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper demonstrates that Conversational AI Agents \(CAAs\) possess uniquely identifiable linguistic patterns \(UILPs\), enabling highly accurate authorship attribution between different models. By applying authorship attribution techniques, the study successfully constructs distinct linguistic profiles for five CAAs, achieving a weighted F1-score of 96.94% for attribution based on original texts and 95.84% for attribution based on writing style. Principal Component Analysis \(PCA\) further identifies the top 100 most informative features per model, with attribution performance ranging from 86.04% to 97.93% and an overall weighted F1-score of 93.86%.  \[翻译\] 该论文证明了对话式AI智能体（CAAs）具有唯一可识别的语言模式（UILPs），能够实现不同模型间的高精度作者归属。研究通过应用作者归属技术，成功为五个CAAs构建了不同的语言特征画像，在基于原始文本的归属任务中实现了96.94%的加权F1 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study is limited to analyzing five specific CAAs, and its generalizability to other models or evolving architectures remains unverified. The reliance on linguistic features may not fully capture semantic or pragmatic nuances, and the performance could degrade with adversarial text manipulation or stylistic imitation.  \[翻译\] 该研究仅限于分析五种特定的对话式AI代理，其结论对其他模型或不断发展的架构的普适性尚未得到验证。对语言特征的依赖可能无法完全捕捉语义或语用上的细微差别，且在遭遇对抗性文本操纵或风格模仿时，检测性能可能会下降。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper investigates the existence of uniquely identifiable linguistic patterns \(UILPs\) across different conversational AI agents \(CAAs\). It aims to move beyond simple human-vs-machine detection by applying authorship attribution techniques to distinguish between specific AI models based on their textual outputs, establishing a baseline for this refined task.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文旨在探究不同对话式AI代理（CAA）之间是否存在唯一可识别的语言模式（UILP）。其研究动机超越了简单的人机文本区分，转而应用作者归属技术，根据文本输出来区分特定的AI模型，从而为这一更精细的任务建立基线。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This work innovatively applies authorship attribution techniques to distinguish between different conversational AI agents \(CAAs\) rather than just humans vs. machines. It constructs unique linguistic profiles for CAAs, achieving high attribution accuracy and identifying their most informative features, establishing a baseline for CAA authorship analysis.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本研究创新性地将作者归属技术应用于区分不同的对话式AI智能体，而非仅区分人类与机器生成内容。它为多个CAAs构建了独特的语言特征档案，实现了高精度的归属判定，并识别出其最具信息量的特征，为CAA的作者归属分析建立了基线。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The study employs authorship attribution techniques to identify uniquely identifiable linguistic patterns \(UILPs\) in conversational AI agents \(CAAs\). It constructs linguistic profiles for five CAAs and uses principal component analysis \(PCA\) to extract the top 100 most informative features for each model, enabling high-accuracy attribution based on original and style-specified texts.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该研究采用作者归属技术来识别对话式AI代理（CAA）中唯一可识别的语言模式（UILP）。它为五个CAA构建了语言特征档案，并使用主成分分析（PCA）为每个模型提取了前100个最具信息量的特征，从而能够基于原始文本和指定风格的文本实现高精度的模型归属。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper demonstrates that Conversational AI Agents \(CAAs\) possess uniquely identifiable linguistic patterns \(UILPs\), enabling highly accurate authorship attribution between different models. By applying authorship attribution techniques, the study successfully constructs distinct linguistic profiles for five CAAs, achieving a weighted F1-score of 96.94% for attribution based on original texts and 95.84% for attribution based on writing style. Principal Component Analysis \(PCA\) further identifies the top 100 most informative features per model, with attribution performance ranging from 86.04% to 97.93% and an overall weighted F1-score of 93.86%.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文证明了对话式AI智能体（CAAs）具有唯一可识别的语言模式（UILPs），能够实现不同模型间的高精度作者归属。研究通过应用作者归属技术，成功为五个CAAs构建了不同的语言特征画像，在基于原始文本的归属任务中实现了96.94%的加权F1<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study is limited to analyzing five specific CAAs, and its generalizability to other models or evolving architectures remains unverified. The reliance on linguistic features may not fully capture semantic or pragmatic nuances, and the performance could degrade with adversarial text manipulation or stylistic imitation.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该研究仅限于分析五种特定的对话式AI代理，其结论对其他模型或不断发展的架构的普适性尚未得到验证。对语言特征的依赖可能无法完全捕捉语义或语用上的细微差别，且在遭遇对抗性文本操纵或风格模仿时，检测性能可能会下降。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Machine-generated Content Detection"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Probing the uniquely identifiable linguistic patterns of conversational AI agents",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-08"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "The proliferation of Conversational AI agents \(CAAs\) has emphasised the need to distinguish between human and machine-generated texts, with implications spanning digital forensics and cybersecurity. While prior research primarily focussed on distinguishing human from machine-generated text, our study takes a more refined approach by analysing different CAAs. | [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202025-blue)]()<br>[On the risk of evidence pollution for malicious social text detection in the era of LLMs](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.480/) <br> Herun Wan, Minnan Luo, Zhixiong Su, Guang Dai, Xiang Zhao <br> 2025-07|\[AI generated\] Evidence-enhanced detectors are like a detective relying on witness testimony, but LLMs can fabricate convincing false testimony to mislead the investigation. \[翻译\] 证据增强的检测器如同依赖证人证词的侦探，而大语言模型能伪造令人信服的伪证以误导调查。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper investigates the risk of evidence pollution in malicious social text detection caused by LLMs, aiming to assess its impact and explore potential defense strategies. \[翻译\] 该论文研究了由大语言模型引发的恶意社交文本检测中的证据污染风险，旨在评估其影响并探索潜在的防御策略。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper identifies a novel security threat termed &quot;evidence pollution&quot; against evidence-enhanced malicious text detectors, where LLMs can manipulate or generate deceptive evidence to degrade detector performance. It systematically explores attack scenarios and proposes corresponding defense strategies, highlighting a critical vulnerability in the era of generative AI.  \[翻译\] 本文提出了一种针对证据增强型恶意文本检测器的新型安全威胁，即“证据污染”，其中大型语言模型可以操纵或生成欺骗性证据以降低检测器性能。它系统地探索了攻击场景并提出了相应的防御策略，凸显了生成式人工智能时代的一个关键脆弱性。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes three defense strategies against evidence pollution in malicious social text detection: machine-generated text detection, a mixture of experts, and parameter updating. These strategies are designed to mitigate the performance degradation caused by LLM-manipulated evidence.  \[翻译\] 该论文针对恶意社交文本检测中的证据污染问题，提出了三种防御策略：机器生成文本检测、专家混合模型和参数更新。这些策略旨在减轻由大语言模型操纵证据所导致的性能下降。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper investigates the risk of evidence pollution in malicious social text detection caused by LLMs, demonstrating that LLM-generated evidence can significantly degrade detector performance. It proposes and evaluates several defense strategies, highlighting their limitations and the broader negative impacts of pollution on model calibration and robustness.  \[翻译\] 本文研究了由大语言模型引发的恶意社交文本检测中的证据污染风险，证明LLM生成的证据会显著降低检测器性能。文章提出并评估了多种防御策略，指出了其局限性，并揭示了污染对模型校准和鲁棒性的广泛负面影响。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study demonstrates the vulnerability of evidence-enhanced detectors to LLM-generated evidence pollution and proposes initial defense strategies. However, the proposed defenses face practical limitations, and the work primarily focuses on encoder-based language models, leaving the impact on and mitigation for decoder-based LLMs \(e.g., GPT\) less explored. \[翻译\] 该研究证明了证据增强检测器易受LLM生成的证据污染影响，并提出了初步的防御策略。然而，所提出的防御策略存在实际应用限制，且工作主要关注基于编码器的语言模型，对基于解码器的LLMs（如GPT）的影响和缓解措施探索不足。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper investigates the risk of evidence pollution in malicious social text detection caused by LLMs, aiming to assess its impact and explore potential defense strategies. \[翻译\] 该论文研究了由大语言模型引发的恶意社交文本检测中的证据污染风险，旨在评估其影响并探索潜在的防御策略。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper identifies a novel security threat termed "evidence pollution" against evidence-enhanced malicious text detectors, where LLMs can manipulate or generate deceptive evidence to degrade detector performance. It systematically explores attack scenarios and proposes corresponding defense strategies, highlighting a critical vulnerability in the era of generative AI.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文提出了一种针对证据增强型恶意文本检测器的新型安全威胁，即“证据污染”，其中大型语言模型可以操纵或生成欺骗性证据以降低检测器性能。它系统地探索了攻击场景并提出了相应的防御策略，凸显了生成式人工智能时代的一个关键脆弱性。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes three defense strategies against evidence pollution in malicious social text detection: machine-generated text detection, a mixture of experts, and parameter updating. These strategies are designed to mitigate the performance degradation caused by LLM-manipulated evidence.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文针对恶意社交文本检测中的证据污染问题，提出了三种防御策略：机器生成文本检测、专家混合模型和参数更新。这些策略旨在减轻由大语言模型操纵证据所导致的性能下降。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper investigates the risk of evidence pollution in malicious social text detection caused by LLMs, demonstrating that LLM-generated evidence can significantly degrade detector performance. It proposes and evaluates several defense strategies, highlighting their limitations and the broader negative impacts of pollution on model calibration and robustness.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文研究了由大语言模型引发的恶意社交文本检测中的证据污染风险，证明LLM生成的证据会显著降低检测器性能。文章提出并评估了多种防御策略，指出了其局限性，并揭示了污染对模型校准和鲁棒性的广泛负面影响。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study demonstrates the vulnerability of evidence-enhanced detectors to LLM-generated evidence pollution and proposes initial defense strategies. However, the proposed defenses face practical limitations, and the work primarily focuses on encoder-based language models, leaving the impact on and mitigation for decoder-based LLMs \(e.g., GPT\) less explored. \[翻译\] 该研究证明了证据增强检测器易受LLM生成的证据污染影响，并提出了初步的防御策略。然而，所提出的防御策略存在实际应用限制，且工作主要关注基于编码器的语言模型，对基于解码器的LLMs（如GPT）的影响和缓解措施探索不足。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Malicious User Detection",<br>    "Machine-generated Content Detection",<br>    "Robustness and Adversarial Attacks"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "On the risk of evidence pollution for malicious social text detection in the era of LLMs",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Evidence-enhanced detectors present remarkable abilities in identifying malicious social text. However, the rise of large language models \(LLMs\) brings potential risks of evidence pollution to confuse detectors. This paper explores potential manipulation scenarios including basic pollution, and rephrasing or generating evidence by LLMs. To mitigate the negative impact, we propose three defense strategies from the data and model sides, including machine-generated text detection, a mixture of expe"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Findings%202025-blue)]()<br>[When detection fails: The power of fine-tuned models to generate human-like social media text](https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-acl.695/) <br> Hillary Dawkins, Kathleen C. Fraser, Svetlana Kiritchenko <br> 2025-07|\[AI generated\] Fine-tuning LLMs for social media text generation is akin to a forger mastering a specific handwriting style, making detection tools struggle to distinguish it from genuine human writing. \[翻译\] 针对社交媒体文本生成对LLM进行微调，如同伪造者掌握了特定笔迹风格，使检测工具难以将其与真实人类书写区分开来。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper investigates the challenge of detecting AI-generated social media posts, particularly those created by fine-tuned language models, which pose a significant threat to online discourse by enabling scalable influence campaigns. \[翻译\] 本文研究了检测AI生成社交媒体帖子的挑战，特别是那些由经过微调的语言模型生成的帖子，它们通过实现可扩展的影响力活动，对在线舆论构成了重大威胁。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper demonstrates that fine-tuned LLMs can generate social media text that evades detection by current algorithms, highlighting a critical vulnerability in machine-generated content detection under realistic threat scenarios. \[翻译\] 本文证明，经过微调的大语言模型能够生成规避现有算法检测的社交媒体文本，揭示了在现实威胁场景下机器生成内容检测的一个关键漏洞。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors constructed a dataset of 505,159 AI-generated social media posts using a combination of open-source, closed-source, and fine-tuned LLMs across 11 controversial topics. They evaluated detection performance under two threat models: one with full knowledge of the generating models and a more realistic scenario where an attacker&#x27;s fine-tuned model is not publicly available. Ablation experiments were conducted to assess the vulnerability of various detection algorithms to fine-tuned LLMs.  \[翻译\] 作者使用开源、闭源和微调的大语言模型组合，围绕11个争议性主题，构建了一个包含505,159条AI生成社交媒体帖子的数据集。他们在两种威胁模型下评估检测性能：一种是完全了解生成模型，另一种是攻击者的微调模型不公开的更现实场景。作者还进行了消融实验，以评估不同检测算法对微调大语言模型的脆弱性。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The study demonstrates that fine-tuned LLMs can generate social media text that evades detection by current algorithms, particularly when the attacker&#x27;s model is not publicly accessible. This highlights a critical vulnerability in machine-generated content detection for online influence campaigns.  \[翻译\] 该研究表明，经过微调的大语言模型生成的社交媒体文本能够规避现有检测算法，尤其是在攻击者模型未公开的情况下。这揭示了在线影响力活动中，机器生成内容检测存在一个关键漏洞。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study&#x27;s primary limitation is its focus on detection under the assumption of model secrecy, which, while realistic, restricts the evaluation to scenarios where the attacker&#x27;s fine-tuned model is unavailable. This setup precludes the exploration of detection strategies that might leverage knowledge from the fine-tuning process or data. \[翻译\] 该研究的主要局限在于其检测场景建立在模型保密的前提下，这虽然现实，但将评估限制在了攻击者的微调模型不可用的情境中。这种设置阻碍了对可能利用微调过程或数据知识的检测策略的探索。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper investigates the challenge of detecting AI-generated social media posts, particularly those created by fine-tuned language models, which pose a significant threat to online discourse by enabling scalable influence campaigns. \[翻译\] 本文研究了检测AI生成社交媒体帖子的挑战，特别是那些由经过微调的语言模型生成的帖子，它们通过实现可扩展的影响力活动，对在线舆论构成了重大威胁。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper demonstrates that fine-tuned LLMs can generate social media text that evades detection by current algorithms, highlighting a critical vulnerability in machine-generated content detection under realistic threat scenarios. \[翻译\] 本文证明，经过微调的大语言模型能够生成规避现有算法检测的社交媒体文本，揭示了在现实威胁场景下机器生成内容检测的一个关键漏洞。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors constructed a dataset of 505,159 AI-generated social media posts using a combination of open-source, closed-source, and fine-tuned LLMs across 11 controversial topics. They evaluated detection performance under two threat models: one with full knowledge of the generating models and a more realistic scenario where an attacker's fine-tuned model is not publicly available. Ablation experiments were conducted to assess the vulnerability of various detection algorithms to fine-tuned LLMs.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>作者使用开源、闭源和微调的大语言模型组合，围绕11个争议性主题，构建了一个包含505,159条AI生成社交媒体帖子的数据集。他们在两种威胁模型下评估检测性能：一种是完全了解生成模型，另一种是攻击者的微调模型不公开的更现实场景。作者还进行了消融实验，以评估不同检测算法对微调大语言模型的脆弱性。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The study demonstrates that fine-tuned LLMs can generate social media text that evades detection by current algorithms, particularly when the attacker's model is not publicly accessible. This highlights a critical vulnerability in machine-generated content detection for online influence campaigns.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该研究表明，经过微调的大语言模型生成的社交媒体文本能够规避现有检测算法，尤其是在攻击者模型未公开的情况下。这揭示了在线影响力活动中，机器生成内容检测存在一个关键漏洞。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study's primary limitation is its focus on detection under the assumption of model secrecy, which, while realistic, restricts the evaluation to scenarios where the attacker's fine-tuned model is unavailable. This setup precludes the exploration of detection strategies that might leverage knowledge from the fine-tuning process or data. \[翻译\] 该研究的主要局限在于其检测场景建立在模型保密的前提下，这虽然现实，但将评估限制在了攻击者的微调模型不可用的情境中。这种设置阻碍了对可能利用微调过程或数据知识的检测策略的探索。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Machine-generated Content Detection",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "When detection fails: The power of fine-tuned models to generate human-like social media text",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Detecting AI-generated text is a difficult problem to begin with; detecting AI-generated text on social media is made even more difficult due to the short text length and informal, idiosyncratic language of the internet. It is nonetheless important to tackle this problem, as social media represents a significant attack vector in online influence campaigns, which may be bolstered through the use of mass-produced AI-generated posts supporting \(or opposing\) particular policies, decisions, or events"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
|[DetectGPT: Zero-shot machine-generated text detection using probability curvature](http://arxiv.org/abs/2301.11305) <br> Eric Mitchell, Yoonho Lee, Alexander Khazatsky, Christopher D. Manning, Chelsea Finn <br> 2023-07-23|\[AI generated\] DetectGPT: Detecting machine-generated text by measuring its probability curvature, akin to spotting a forged painting by analyzing subtle brushstroke anomalies. \[翻译\] DetectGPT：通过测量概率曲率检测机器生成文本，类似于通过分析细微笔触异常来识别赝品画作。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] DetectGPT aims to address the need for reliable detection of machine-generated text without requiring separate training data, classifiers, or watermarks. It leverages an intrinsic property of LLMs—that their generated text tends to lie in regions of negative log probability curvature—to create a zero-shot detection method.  \[翻译\] DetectGPT旨在满足对机器生成文本进行可靠检测的需求，且无需单独的训练数据、分类器或水印。它利用大语言模型的一个内在特性——其生成的文本倾向于位于对数概率函数的负曲率区域——来创建一种零样本检测方法。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] DetectGPT introduces a zero-shot detection method for machine-generated text by leveraging the curvature of an LLM&#x27;s log probability function, identifying that model-generated samples tend to reside in regions of negative curvature. This approach eliminates the need for training separate classifiers, collecting labeled datasets, or applying watermarks, relying instead on the target model&#x27;s own probability scores and perturbations from a generic pre-trained model.  \[翻译\] DetectGPT提出了一种零样本机器生成文本检测方法，其核心创新在于利用大语言模型对数概率函数的曲率特性，识别出模型生成的文本倾向于位于概率函数的负曲率区域。该方法无需训练单独的分类器、收集标注数据集或添加水印，仅依赖于目标模型自身的概率分数和一个通用预训练模型生成的文本扰动。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] DetectGPT introduces a zero-shot detection method that leverages the curvature of an LLM&#x27;s log probability function. It posits that machine-generated text tends to lie in regions of negative curvature. The method calculates a detection score by comparing the original log probability of a text passage with the average log probability of its perturbations generated by another pre-trained language model \(e.g., T5\), without requiring additional training or datasets.  \[翻译\] DetectGPT提出了一种零样本检测方法，该方法利用了大语言模型对数概率函数的曲率特性。其核心观点是机器生成的文本倾向于位于对数概率函数的负曲率区域。该方法通过比较原始文本段落的对数概率与由另一个预训练语言模型（如T5）生成的扰动文本的平均对数概率来计算检测分数，无需额外训练或数据集。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] DetectGPT introduces a zero-shot method for detecting machine-generated text by leveraging the curvature of an LLM&#x27;s log probability function, demonstrating that model-generated samples tend to lie in regions of negative curvature. This approach outperforms existing zero-shot baselines without requiring additional training data, separate classifiers, or watermarks. \[翻译\] DetectGPT提出了一种零样本机器生成文本检测方法，其核心是利用大语言模型对数概率函数的曲率特性，证明了模型生成的文本倾向于位于负曲率区域。该方法无需额外训练数据、独立分类器或水印，性能优于现有的零样本基线。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] DetectGPT&#x27;s detection performance is contingent on the availability of the target LLM&#x27;s log probability outputs and requires a separate pre-trained model for generating perturbations. Its effectiveness may degrade when applied to text generated by unknown models or significantly edited human-written text. \[翻译\] DetectGPT的检测性能依赖于获取目标大语言模型的概率对数输出，且需要一个独立的预训练模型来生成扰动文本。当应用于未知模型生成的文本或经过显著编辑的人类撰写文本时，其有效性可能会下降。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] DetectGPT aims to address the need for reliable detection of machine-generated text without requiring separate training data, classifiers, or watermarks. It leverages an intrinsic property of LLMs—that their generated text tends to lie in regions of negative log probability curvature—to create a zero-shot detection method.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>DetectGPT旨在满足对机器生成文本进行可靠检测的需求，且无需单独的训练数据、分类器或水印。它利用大语言模型的一个内在特性——其生成的文本倾向于位于对数概率函数的负曲率区域——来创建一种零样本检测方法。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] DetectGPT introduces a zero-shot detection method for machine-generated text by leveraging the curvature of an LLM's log probability function, identifying that model-generated samples tend to reside in regions of negative curvature. This approach eliminates the need for training separate classifiers, collecting labeled datasets, or applying watermarks, relying instead on the target model's own probability scores and perturbations from a generic pre-trained model.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>DetectGPT提出了一种零样本机器生成文本检测方法，其核心创新在于利用大语言模型对数概率函数的曲率特性，识别出模型生成的文本倾向于位于概率函数的负曲率区域。该方法无需训练单独的分类器、收集标注数据集或添加水印，仅依赖于目标模型自身的概率分数和一个通用预训练模型生成的文本扰动。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] DetectGPT introduces a zero-shot detection method that leverages the curvature of an LLM's log probability function. It posits that machine-generated text tends to lie in regions of negative curvature. The method calculates a detection score by comparing the original log probability of a text passage with the average log probability of its perturbations generated by another pre-trained language model \(e.g., T5\), without requiring additional training or datasets.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>DetectGPT提出了一种零样本检测方法，该方法利用了大语言模型对数概率函数的曲率特性。其核心观点是机器生成的文本倾向于位于对数概率函数的负曲率区域。该方法通过比较原始文本段落的对数概率与由另一个预训练语言模型（如T5）生成的扰动文本的平均对数概率来计算检测分数，无需额外训练或数据集。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] DetectGPT introduces a zero-shot method for detecting machine-generated text by leveraging the curvature of an LLM's log probability function, demonstrating that model-generated samples tend to lie in regions of negative curvature. This approach outperforms existing zero-shot baselines without requiring additional training data, separate classifiers, or watermarks. \[翻译\] DetectGPT提出了一种零样本机器生成文本检测方法，其核心是利用大语言模型对数概率函数的曲率特性，证明了模型生成的文本倾向于位于负曲率区域。该方法无需额外训练数据、独立分类器或水印，性能优于现有的零样本基线。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] DetectGPT's detection performance is contingent on the availability of the target LLM's log probability outputs and requires a separate pre-trained model for generating perturbations. Its effectiveness may degrade when applied to text generated by unknown models or significantly edited human-written text. \[翻译\] DetectGPT的检测性能依赖于获取目标大语言模型的概率对数输出，且需要一个独立的预训练模型来生成扰动文本。当应用于未知模型生成的文本或经过显著编辑的人类撰写文本时，其有效性可能会下降。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">Comment: ICML 2023</div></details></div></div>|
|[Longformer: The long-document transformer](http://arxiv.org/abs/2004.05150) <br> Iz Beltagy, Matthew E. Peters, Arman Cohan <br> 2020-12-02|\[AI generated\] Longformer: A transformer model that replaces quadratic self-attention with linear-complexity attention, enabling efficient processing of long documents through local windowed and task-specific global attention. \[翻译\] Longformer：一种用线性复杂度注意力替代二次自注意力的Transformer模型，通过局部窗口注意力与任务驱动的全局注意力实现长文档高效处理。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The Longformer addresses the fundamental limitation of standard Transformer models in processing long sequences, as their self-attention mechanism scales quadratically with sequence length. The paper aims to overcome this by introducing a novel attention mechanism that scales linearly, enabling efficient handling of documents with thousands of tokens or more.  \[翻译\] Longformer旨在解决标准Transformer模型处理长序列的根本性局限，因其自注意力机制的计算复杂度随序列长度呈二次方增长。本文的目标是通过引入一种计算复杂度呈线性增长的新型注意力机制来克服这一局限，从而实现对数千个标记或更长的文档进行高效处理。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The Longformer introduces a novel attention mechanism that scales linearly with sequence length, enabling efficient processing of long documents. Its key innovation is a hybrid attention pattern combining local windowed attention with task-specific global attention, serving as a drop-in replacement for standard quadratic self-attention. This design allows for effective pre-training and fine-tuning on downstream tasks, leading to state-of-the-art performance on long-document benchmarks and enabling new generative sequence-to-sequence models like LED for summarization.  \[翻译\] Longformer提出了一种新颖的注意力机制，其计算复杂度与序列长度呈线性关系，从而能够高效处理长文档。其核心创新在于一种混合注意力模式，它结合了局部窗口注意力与面向任务的全局注意力，可作为标准二次复杂度自注意力的直接替代。该设计支持对下游任务进行有效的预训练和微调，在长文档基准测试中取得了最先进的性能，并催生了如用于摘要任务的LED等新的生成式序列到序列模型。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] Longformer employs a hybrid attention mechanism combining local windowed attention with task-motivated global attention, scaling linearly with sequence length to process long documents efficiently. \[翻译\] Longformer采用了一种混合注意力机制，结合了局部窗口注意力与任务驱动的全局注意力，其计算复杂度随序列长度线性增长，从而能够高效处理长文档。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The Longformer addresses the quadratic scaling limitation of standard Transformer self-attention by introducing a novel linear-scaling attention mechanism. This mechanism combines local windowed attention with task-specific global attention, enabling efficient processing of long documents. The model achieves state-of-the-art results on character-level language modeling benchmarks \(text8, enwik8\). Furthermore, its pretrained version outperforms RoBERTa on various long-document downstream tasks, setting new records on WikiHop and TriviaQA. The paper also presents the Longformer-Encoder-Decoder \(LED\), a variant for long-document generative sequence-to-sequence tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness on arXiv summarization.  \[翻译\] Longformer通过引入一种新颖的线性复杂度注意力机制，解决了标准Transformer自注意力二次方扩展的限制。该机制结合了局部窗口注意力与任务驱动的全局注意力，从而能够高效处理长文档。该模型在字符级语言建模基准（text8, enwik8）上取得了最先进的结果。此外，其 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The Longformer&#x27;s linear attention mechanism enables processing long documents, but its performance on extremely long sequences \(e.g., &gt; 16K tokens\) may still degrade, and the global attention design requires task-specific patterns, limiting its generalizability across all long-context scenarios. \[翻译\] Longformer的线性注意力机制使其能够处理长文档，但其在极长序列（例如超过16K个标记）上的性能可能仍然会下降，且其全局注意力设计需要任务特定的模式，这限制了其在所有长上下文场景中的普适性。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The Longformer addresses the fundamental limitation of standard Transformer models in processing long sequences, as their self-attention mechanism scales quadratically with sequence length. The paper aims to overcome this by introducing a novel attention mechanism that scales linearly, enabling efficient handling of documents with thousands of tokens or more.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>Longformer旨在解决标准Transformer模型处理长序列的根本性局限，因其自注意力机制的计算复杂度随序列长度呈二次方增长。本文的目标是通过引入一种计算复杂度呈线性增长的新型注意力机制来克服这一局限，从而实现对数千个标记或更长的文档进行高效处理。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The Longformer introduces a novel attention mechanism that scales linearly with sequence length, enabling efficient processing of long documents. Its key innovation is a hybrid attention pattern combining local windowed attention with task-specific global attention, serving as a drop-in replacement for standard quadratic self-attention. This design allows for effective pre-training and fine-tuning on downstream tasks, leading to state-of-the-art performance on long-document benchmarks and enabling new generative sequence-to-sequence models like LED for summarization.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>Longformer提出了一种新颖的注意力机制，其计算复杂度与序列长度呈线性关系，从而能够高效处理长文档。其核心创新在于一种混合注意力模式，它结合了局部窗口注意力与面向任务的全局注意力，可作为标准二次复杂度自注意力的直接替代。该设计支持对下游任务进行有效的预训练和微调，在长文档基准测试中取得了最先进的性能，并催生了如用于摘要任务的LED等新的生成式序列到序列模型。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] Longformer employs a hybrid attention mechanism combining local windowed attention with task-motivated global attention, scaling linearly with sequence length to process long documents efficiently. \[翻译\] Longformer采用了一种混合注意力机制，结合了局部窗口注意力与任务驱动的全局注意力，其计算复杂度随序列长度线性增长，从而能够高效处理长文档。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The Longformer addresses the quadratic scaling limitation of standard Transformer self-attention by introducing a novel linear-scaling attention mechanism. This mechanism combines local windowed attention with task-specific global attention, enabling efficient processing of long documents. The model achieves state-of-the-art results on character-level language modeling benchmarks \(text8, enwik8\). Furthermore, its pretrained version outperforms RoBERTa on various long-document downstream tasks, setting new records on WikiHop and TriviaQA. The paper also presents the Longformer-Encoder-Decoder \(LED\), a variant for long-document generative sequence-to-sequence tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness on arXiv summarization.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>Longformer通过引入一种新颖的线性复杂度注意力机制，解决了标准Transformer自注意力二次方扩展的限制。该机制结合了局部窗口注意力与任务驱动的全局注意力，从而能够高效处理长文档。该模型在字符级语言建模基准（text8, enwik8）上取得了最先进的结果。此外，其<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The Longformer's linear attention mechanism enables processing long documents, but its performance on extremely long sequences \(e.g., > 16K tokens\) may still degrade, and the global attention design requires task-specific patterns, limiting its generalizability across all long-context scenarios. \[翻译\] Longformer的线性注意力机制使其能够处理长文档，但其在极长序列（例如超过16K个标记）上的性能可能仍然会下降，且其全局注意力设计需要任务特定的模式，这限制了其在所有长上下文场景中的普适性。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">Comment: Version 2 introduces the Longformer-Encoder-Decoder \(LED\) model</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202025-blue)]()<br>[MIND: A multi-agent framework for zero-shot harmful meme detection](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.46/) <br> Ziyan Liu, Chunxiao Fan, Haoran Lou, Yuexin Wu, Kaiwei Deng <br> 2025-07 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Meme and Multimodal Understanding](#Meme-and-Multimodal-Understanding-7-papers), [Machine-Generated Content Detection](#Machine-Generated-Content-Detection-15-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] MIND employs a multi-agent debate mechanism akin to a panel of experts deliberating over ambiguous memes, achieving zero-shot detection without annotated data. \[翻译\] MIND采用多智能体辩论机制，类似于专家小组对模糊梗图进行审议，实现了无需标注数据的零样本检测。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper addresses the challenge of detecting harmful memes in a zero-shot setting, where traditional data-driven methods fail due to the lack of up-to-date annotated data for evolving meme content. It proposes a multi-agent framework to overcome this limitation by leveraging contextual information from similar memes and employing debate mechanisms for robust decision-making.  \[翻译\] 该论文旨在解决有害表情包在零样本场景下的检测挑战，传统数据驱动方法因缺乏针对不断演变的表情包内容的实时标注数据而失效。它提出一个多智能体框架，通过利用相似表情包的上下文信息并采用辩论机制进行鲁棒决策，以克服这一限制。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces MIND, a novel multi-agent framework for zero-shot harmful meme detection. Its core innovation lies in a three-stage pipeline that leverages unannotated data: retrieving similar memes for context, employing a bi-directional insight derivation mechanism for comprehensive understanding, and utilizing a multi-agent debate mechanism for robust, reasoned decision-making. This approach eliminates reliance on annotated data, offering strong generalization across model architectures and scales.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了MIND，一个用于零样本有害模因检测的新型多智能体框架。其核心创新在于一个利用未标注数据的三阶段流程：检索相似模因以提供上下文，采用双向洞察推导机制以获得全面理解，并利用多智能体辩论机制进行稳健、有推理的决策。该方法摆脱了对标注数据的依赖，在不同模型架构和规模上均展现出强大的泛化能力。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The MIND framework employs a multi-agent debate mechanism for zero-shot harmful meme detection. It first retrieves contextually similar memes from an unannotated reference set. A bi-directional insight derivation mechanism then extracts comprehensive semantic understanding from these similar memes. Finally, multiple reasoning agents engage in a structured debate, where their arguments are arbitrated to reach a robust, consensus-based detection decision.  \[翻译\] MIND框架采用多智能体辩论机制进行零样本有害模因检测。它首先从一个未标注的参考集中检索上下文相似的模因。随后，一个双向洞察推导机制从这些相似模因中提取全面的语义理解。最后，多个推理智能体参与结构化辩论，通过仲裁它们的论点来达成一个稳健的、基于共识的检测决策。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The MIND framework introduces a multi-agent, zero-shot approach for harmful meme detection, effectively addressing the data scarcity and evolving nature of memes by leveraging contextual retrieval, bi-directional insight derivation, and multi-agent debate. It demonstrates superior performance and strong generalization across datasets and model architectures.  \[翻译\] MIND框架提出了一种用于有害梗图检测的多智能体零样本方法，通过利用上下文检索、双向洞察推导和多智能体辩论，有效解决了梗图数据稀缺和不断演变的问题。该框架在多个数据集和模型架构上展现了优越的性能和强大的泛化能力。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The framework&#x27;s reliance on retrieving similar memes from an unannotated reference set may limit its effectiveness for highly novel or isolated harmful memes that lack visual or semantic parallels in the reference corpus. Its computational overhead from multi-agent debate also presents a scalability challenge for real-time, large-scale social media platforms.  \[翻译\] 该框架依赖于从无标注参考集中检索相似模因，对于高度新颖或孤立、在参考语料库中缺乏视觉或语义相似性的有害模因，其检测效果可能受限。此外，多智能体辩论机制带来的计算开销，也给实时、大规模社交媒体平台的应用带来了可扩展性挑战。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper addresses the challenge of detecting harmful memes in a zero-shot setting, where traditional data-driven methods fail due to the lack of up-to-date annotated data for evolving meme content. It proposes a multi-agent framework to overcome this limitation by leveraging contextual information from similar memes and employing debate mechanisms for robust decision-making.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文旨在解决有害表情包在零样本场景下的检测挑战，传统数据驱动方法因缺乏针对不断演变的表情包内容的实时标注数据而失效。它提出一个多智能体框架，通过利用相似表情包的上下文信息并采用辩论机制进行鲁棒决策，以克服这一限制。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces MIND, a novel multi-agent framework for zero-shot harmful meme detection. Its core innovation lies in a three-stage pipeline that leverages unannotated data: retrieving similar memes for context, employing a bi-directional insight derivation mechanism for comprehensive understanding, and utilizing a multi-agent debate mechanism for robust, reasoned decision-making. This approach eliminates reliance on annotated data, offering strong generalization across model architectures and scales.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了MIND，一个用于零样本有害模因检测的新型多智能体框架。其核心创新在于一个利用未标注数据的三阶段流程：检索相似模因以提供上下文，采用双向洞察推导机制以获得全面理解，并利用多智能体辩论机制进行稳健、有推理的决策。该方法摆脱了对标注数据的依赖，在不同模型架构和规模上均展现出强大的泛化能力。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The MIND framework employs a multi-agent debate mechanism for zero-shot harmful meme detection. It first retrieves contextually similar memes from an unannotated reference set. A bi-directional insight derivation mechanism then extracts comprehensive semantic understanding from these similar memes. Finally, multiple reasoning agents engage in a structured debate, where their arguments are arbitrated to reach a robust, consensus-based detection decision.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>MIND框架采用多智能体辩论机制进行零样本有害模因检测。它首先从一个未标注的参考集中检索上下文相似的模因。随后，一个双向洞察推导机制从这些相似模因中提取全面的语义理解。最后，多个推理智能体参与结构化辩论，通过仲裁它们的论点来达成一个稳健的、基于共识的检测决策。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The MIND framework introduces a multi-agent, zero-shot approach for harmful meme detection, effectively addressing the data scarcity and evolving nature of memes by leveraging contextual retrieval, bi-directional insight derivation, and multi-agent debate. It demonstrates superior performance and strong generalization across datasets and model architectures.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>MIND框架提出了一种用于有害梗图检测的多智能体零样本方法，通过利用上下文检索、双向洞察推导和多智能体辩论，有效解决了梗图数据稀缺和不断演变的问题。该框架在多个数据集和模型架构上展现了优越的性能和强大的泛化能力。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The framework's reliance on retrieving similar memes from an unannotated reference set may limit its effectiveness for highly novel or isolated harmful memes that lack visual or semantic parallels in the reference corpus. Its computational overhead from multi-agent debate also presents a scalability challenge for real-time, large-scale social media platforms.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该框架依赖于从无标注参考集中检索相似模因，对于高度新颖或孤立、在参考语料库中缺乏视觉或语义相似性的有害模因，其检测效果可能受限。此外，多智能体辩论机制带来的计算开销，也给实时、大规模社交媒体平台的应用带来了可扩展性挑战。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Meme and Multimodal Understanding"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "MIND: A multi-agent framework for zero-shot harmful meme detection",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "The rapid expansion of memes on social media has highlighted the urgent need for effective approaches to detect harmful content. <details><summary title="**[motivation]** 为了解决多模态内容中因隐喻对齐导致**情感误判**的问题：之前的方法本质上无法理解隐喻,专注于直接的语义对齐，无法捕捉如文字“眼泪”与图片“河流”的这种隐式联系 **[innovation]** 将多模态间隐含联系的对齐思想应用于多模态情感识别领域 **[method]** 使用了基于条件生成与解耦上下文适应的CAMEL框架:隐喻对齐建模（条件生成）\(强制模型按照模版输出（CMT和SPV两种技术）；使用图片、标题、文本的交叉注意力机制，使用一个\[CLS\] token聚合全局信息）-&gt;上下文语义适应（特征融合）（使用两个异构模型，分别输入字面特征（CAMEL-C，CMT）和隐喻特征（CAMEL-S，SPV）生成两个向量矩阵，通过隐喻查字面实现多头注意力）-&gt;解耦对比匹配（上下文正则化）（确保不偏离语境。采用解耦学习思想，隐喻特征中分离出代表“主导上下文类别”的离散分布，通过对比学习，使字面特征推出的分布与之对齐） **[conclusion/contribution]** 达成了对隐含情感更精准、鲁棒的识别效果 **[limitation/future]** 为了解决多模态内容中因隐喻对齐导致**情感误判**的问题：之前的方法本质上无法理解隐喻,专注于直接的语义对齐，无法捕捉如文字“眼泪”与图片“河流”的这种隐式联系<br>**[innovation]** 将多模态间隐含联系的对齐思想应用于多模态情感识别领域<br>**[method]** 使用了基于条件生成与解耦上下文适应的CAMEL框架:隐喻对齐建模（条件生成）\(强制模型按照模版输出（CMT和SPV两种技术）；使用图片、标题、文本的交叉注意力机制，使用一个\[CLS\] 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** Addresses the limitations of single-dominant emotion assumptions by tackling the challenge of multi-label emotion co-occurrence and generalization 将多模态知识蒸馏与标签一致性校准损失（LCC）相结合，减轻了模型对简单标签的过拟合（保证置信度相近）；构建了一个利用蒸馏方法的整体框架，其目的是为了融合各模态能力 **[method]** Employing a Multimodal Transformer Network where mode-specific peer branches \(visual, audio, textual\) collaboratively distill learned probabilistic uncertainty into a fusion branch via cross-network attention and noise contrastive estimation.…… \[翻译\] 将三个特定模态的对等分支通过跨网络注意力和噪声对比估计，协同地将其学习到的概率蒸馏到融合分支中，构建了一个拥有四个分支的整体预测模型。\[值得关注\]视频使用Tubelet embedding技术，将视频切分为时空小块（Spatial-Temporal Tubes），保留时空信息 **[conclusion/contribution]** Demonstrates state-of-the-art performance on MOSEI, EmoReact, and ElderReact datasets, achieving an approximate 17% improvement in weighted F1-score during cross-dataset evaluations, thereby validating robustness in age-diverse scenarios. \[翻译\] 在MOSEI、EmoReact和ElderReact数据集上最先进的性能，跨数据集评估有约17%的加权F1提升，在跨年龄场景下具有鲁棒性。 **[limitation/future]** The approach necessitates the reduction of complex emotion categories to basic subsets
