embedding mechanism.<br>\[翻译\] 为了保持跨数据集一致性，要将复杂情感类归约为基础子集，由于采用时空Tubelet嵌入机制，导致了显著的计算开销</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【面向结果模型训练】\[引用句\]"Transscending the traditional paradigm of identifying single dominant emotions, Anand et al. \[2023\] proposed SeMuL-PCD to enhance the granularity of affective perception in diverse social contexts; by leveraging a collaborative distillation mechanism that calibrates mode-specific feedback, their model robustly disentangles multi-label emotional co-occurrences across varying demographic backgrounds \(e.g., children and the elderly\), thereby providing a more nuanced foundation for socially adaptive agents."<br>\[翻译\] “为了超越识别单一主导情感的传统范式，Anand等人\[2023\]提出了SeMuL-PCD，旨在增强不同社会语境下情感感知的粒度；通过利用一种校准模态特定反馈的协作蒸馏机制，该模型能够在不同的人口统计背景（如儿童和老人）下鲁棒地解耦多标签情感的共现关系，从而为具备社会适应能力的智能体提供了更精细的情感理解基础。”</div></details></div></div>|
|[![Star](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/dess-mannheim/temporal-adapters.svg?style=social&label=Star)](https://github.com/dess-mannheim/temporal-adapters) [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ICWSM-blue)]()<br>[Extracting affect aggregates from longitudinal social media data with temporal adapters for large...](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/ICWSM/article/view/35801) <br> Georg Ahnert, Max Pellert, David Garcia, Markus Strohmaier <br> 2025-06-07|对于每周，训练一个LoRA作为时间适配器，使模型获得了根据时间段预测情感的能力|<div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;align-items:center"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/445f40eb/Temporal Adapters.png"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/445f40eb/Temporal Adapters2.png"></div>| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** Addresses the temporal misalignment inherent in prompt-based in silico surveys and the scalability bottlenecks of traditional affective computing, which heavily relies on resource-intensive labeled datasets or static dictionaries. \[翻译\] 解决了基于提示词的in silico（计算机模拟）调查中固有的时间错位问题，以及传统情感计算严重依赖资源密集型标注数据集或静态词典的可扩展性瓶颈 **[innovation]** 利用LoRA作为模块化学习元件的“时间适配器”，以捕捉特定时期独有的时间与语言特征。通过将这些轻量级适配器与冻结基座模型的固有推理能力产生协同作用，该框架实现了高效且可扩展的纵向情感预测。 **[method]** Employs a two-stage framework: first, fine-tuning weekly LoRA adapters on longitudinal Twitter timelines via a self-supervised causal language modeling objective; second, probing the adapted models with standard psychometric questionnaires to extract aggregate affect via token probability distributions. \[翻译\] 采用双阶段框架：首先，通过自监督的因果语言建模目标在纵向Twitter时间线上微调每周的LoRA适配器；其次，使用标准心理测量问卷探测适配后的模型，通过Token概率分布提取聚合情感 \[通俗核心\]在每周分别进行LoRA自监督微调，让模型的预测尽可能和原数据一样。使用专业问卷作为prompt，模拟模型回答问卷，最终得到一个情感概率随时间的分布，与公众真实分布对比 **[conclusion/contribution]** Demonstrates strong, significant correlations with representative polling data \(YouGov\) during the COVID-19 pandemic, achieving performance comparable to supervised baselines \(e.g., TweetNLP\) while offering superior flexibility in querying diverse and complex collective attitudes. \[翻译\] 展示了在COVID-19大流行期间与代表性民调数据（YouGov）的强显著相关性，实现了与监督基线模型（如TweetNLP）相当的性能，同时在查询多样化且复杂的集体态度方面提供了更优越的灵活性。 **[limitation/future]** Primarily effective for longitudinal trend analysis rather than absolute cross-sectional calibration, and the representativeness of the emergent sentiment is constrained by the demographic biases inherent in the social media training corpora. \[翻译\] 主要在纵向趋势分析而非绝对横截面校准方面有效，且涌现出的情感代表性受限于社交媒体训练语料库中固有的人口统计学偏差">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** Addresses the temporal misalignment inherent in prompt-based in silico surveys and the scalability bottlenecks of traditional affective computing, which heavily relies on resource-intensive labeled datasets or static dictionaries.<br>\[翻译\] 解决了基于提示词的in silico（计算机模拟）调查中固有的时间错位问题，以及传统情感计算严重依赖资源密集型标注数据集或静态词典的可扩展性瓶颈<br>**[innovation]** 利用LoRA作为模块化学习元件的“时间适配器”，以捕捉特定时期独有的时间与语言特征。通过将这些轻量级适配器与冻结基座模型的固有推理能力产生协同作用，该框架实现了高效且可扩展的纵向情感预测。<br>**[method]** Employs a two-stage framework: first, fine-tuning weekly LoRA adapters on longitudinal Twitter timelines via a self-supervised causal language modeling objective; second, probing the adapted models with standard psychometric questionnaires to extract aggregate affect via token probability distributions.<br>\[翻译\] 采用双阶段框架：首先，通过自监督的因果语言建模目标在纵向Twitter时间线上微调每周的LoRA适配器；其次，使用标准心理测量问卷探测适配后的模型，通过Token概率分布提取聚合情感<br>\[通俗核心\]在每周分别进行LoRA自监督微调，让模型的预测尽可能和原数据一样。使用专业问卷作为prompt，模拟模型回答问卷，最终得到一个情感概率随时间的分布，与公众真实分布对比<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** Demonstrates strong, significant correlations with representative polling data \(YouGov\) during the COVID-19 pandemic, achieving performance comparable to supervised baselines \(e.g., TweetNLP\) while offering superior flexibility in querying diverse and complex collective attitudes.<br>\[翻译\] 展示了在COVID-19大流行期间与代表性民调数据（YouGov）的强显著相关性，实现了与监督基线模型（如TweetNLP）相当的性能，同时在查询多样化且复杂的集体态度方面提供了更优越的灵活性。<br>**[limitation/future]** Primarily effective for longitudinal trend analysis rather than absolute cross-sectional calibration, and the representativeness of the emergent sentiment is constrained by the demographic biases inherent in the social media training corpora.<br>\[翻译\] 主要在纵向趋势分析而非绝对横截面校准方面有效，且涌现出的情感代表性受限于社交媒体训练语料库中固有的人口统计学偏差</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【集体情感分析】通过LoRA自监督学到的是该特定时间段内公众的语言风格和关注点，结合基础模型的固有能力获得了预测特定时间内公众情感的能力，很神奇，这是一个通用方法<br>\[引用文\]Moving beyond traditional supervised classifiers, Ahnert et al. \(2025\) demonstrate a shift toward the social simulation paradigm by proposing Temporal Adapters. Instead of training models to explicitly recognize emotion patterns, they utilize self-supervised learning to train lightweight LoRA modules as learning components, aligning the frozen LLM with specific temporal and linguistic contexts derived from longitudinal social media data. This equips the model with the capability to predict public sentiment within specific timeframes. Their approach validates that scalable and accurate tracking of public opinion dynamic<br>\[翻译\]<br>超越了传统的监督分类器，Ahnert等人 \(2025\) 通过提出 “时间适配器” 展示了向社会仿真范式的转变。他们不再训练模型去显式地识别情感模式，而是通过自监督学习训练轻量级的LoRA模块作为学习元件，将冻结的大语言模型与源自纵向社交媒体数据的特定时间及语言语境相对齐。使模型获得了预测特定时间内公众情感的能力。他们的方法证实，通过时间对齐而非标签监督，即可实现对民意动态的可扩展且准确的追踪。</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Companion%20Proceedings%20of%20the%20ACM%20Web%20Conference%202023-blue)]()<br>[LLMs to the Moon? Reddit Market Sentiment Analysis with Large Language Models](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3543873.3587605) <br> Xiang Deng, Vasilisa Bashlovkina, Feng Han, Simon Baumgartner, Michael Bendersky <br> 2023-04-30|TLDR: This case study conducts a case study approaching market sentiment analysis on social media content with semi-supervised learning using a large language model \(LLM\), finding that prompting the LLM to produce Chain-of-Thought summaries and forcing it through several reasoning paths helps generate more stable and accurate labels.|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/adfb2fb1/LLMs to the Moon.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This work addresses the challenge of market sentiment analysis on social media, which is hindered by the scarcity of high-quality labeled data due to the specialized knowledge required. It proposes a semi-supervised pipeline using an LLM to generate weak labels for Reddit posts, subsequently distilling this knowledge into a smaller, production-ready model.  \[翻译\] 本研究旨在解决社交媒体市场情绪分析面临的挑战，该挑战因需要专业知识而导致高质量标注数据稀缺。论文提出了一种利用大语言模型为Reddit帖子生成弱标签的半监督流程，随后将这些知识提炼到一个更小、可用于生产环境的模型中。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces a semi-supervised pipeline that leverages an LLM to generate weak financial sentiment labels for Reddit posts, addressing the scarcity of high-quality annotated data. Its key innovation lies in using Chain-of-Thought prompting and multiple reasoning paths to enhance label stability and accuracy, followed by distilling this knowledge into a small, production-suitable model via a regression loss, achieving performance competitive with supervised models.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种半监督流程，利用大语言模型为Reddit帖子生成弱金融情感标签，以解决高质量标注数据稀缺的问题。其核心创新在于使用思维链提示和多推理路径来提升标签的稳定性和准确性，随后通过回归损失将知识蒸馏到一个适用于生产环境的小型模型中，取得了与监督模型相媲美的性能。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The method employs a semi-supervised pipeline. It first uses an LLM with Chain-of-Thought prompting and multiple reasoning paths to generate weak financial sentiment labels for Reddit posts. These labels are then used to train a smaller student model via a regression loss for efficient production deployment.  \[翻译\] 该方法采用半监督流程。首先使用具备思维链提示和多推理路径的大语言模型为Reddit帖子生成弱金融情感标签。随后，这些标签通过回归损失被用于训练一个更小的学生模型，以实现高效的生产部署。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This work demonstrates the feasibility of using LLMs for semi-supervised financial sentiment analysis on Reddit. By employing Chain-of-Thought prompting and multi-path reasoning to generate weak labels, and subsequently distilling this knowledge into a smaller model via regression loss, the approach achieves performance competitive with supervised models. This highlights the potential of LLMs to alleviate the need for extensive skill-intensive manual annotation in specialized domains like market sentiment analysis.  \[翻译\] 本研究证明了使用大语言模型对Reddit进行半监督金融情感分析的可行性。该方法通过思维链提示和多路径推理生成弱标签，并利用回归损失将知识蒸馏到一个小型模型中，最终达到了与监督模型相当的性能。这凸显了大语言模型在减轻如市场情绪分析等专业领域对大量技能密集型人工标注需求的潜力。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study&#x27;s primary limitation lies in its reliance on LLM-generated weak labels, which may inherit the model&#x27;s biases and lack the nuanced financial expertise of human experts, potentially limiting the model&#x27;s accuracy in complex market sentiment scenarios. Furthermore, the ethical considerations surrounding the use of automated sentiment analysis for financial markets are acknowledged as a significant constraint for direct production deployment.  \[翻译\] 该研究的主要局限在于其依赖LLM生成的弱标签，这些标签可能继承模型的偏见且缺乏人类专家的细致金融知识，这可能在复杂的市场情绪场景中限制模型的准确性。此外，围绕将自动化情绪分析用于金融市场的伦理考量，被认为是阻碍其直接投入生产应用的一个重要制约因素。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This work addresses the challenge of market sentiment analysis on social media, which is hindered by the scarcity of high-quality labeled data due to the specialized knowledge required. It proposes a semi-supervised pipeline using an LLM to generate weak labels for Reddit posts, subsequently distilling this knowledge into a smaller, production-ready model.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本研究旨在解决社交媒体市场情绪分析面临的挑战，该挑战因需要专业知识而导致高质量标注数据稀缺。论文提出了一种利用大语言模型为Reddit帖子生成弱标签的半监督流程，随后将这些知识提炼到一个更小、可用于生产环境的模型中。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces a semi-supervised pipeline that leverages an LLM to generate weak financial sentiment labels for Reddit posts, addressing the scarcity of high-quality annotated data. Its key innovation lies in using Chain-of-Thought prompting and multiple reasoning paths to enhance label stability and accuracy, followed by distilling this knowledge into a small, production-suitable model via a regression loss, achieving performance competitive with supervised models.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种半监督流程，利用大语言模型为Reddit帖子生成弱金融情感标签，以解决高质量标注数据稀缺的问题。其核心创新在于使用思维链提示和多推理路径来提升标签的稳定性和准确性，随后通过回归损失将知识蒸馏到一个适用于生产环境的小型模型中，取得了与监督模型相媲美的性能。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The method employs a semi-supervised pipeline. It first uses an LLM with Chain-of-Thought prompting and multiple reasoning paths to generate weak financial sentiment labels for Reddit posts. These labels are then used to train a smaller student model via a regression loss for efficient production deployment.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该方法采用半监督流程。首先使用具备思维链提示和多推理路径的大语言模型为Reddit帖子生成弱金融情感标签。随后，这些标签通过回归损失被用于训练一个更小的学生模型，以实现高效的生产部署。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This work demonstrates the feasibility of using LLMs for semi-supervised financial sentiment analysis on Reddit. By employing Chain-of-Thought prompting and multi-path reasoning to generate weak labels, and subsequently distilling this knowledge into a smaller model via regression loss, the approach achieves performance competitive with supervised models. This highlights the potential of LLMs to alleviate the need for extensive skill-intensive manual annotation in specialized domains like market sentiment analysis.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本研究证明了使用大语言模型对Reddit进行半监督金融情感分析的可行性。该方法通过思维链提示和多路径推理生成弱标签，并利用回归损失将知识蒸馏到一个小型模型中，最终达到了与监督模型相当的性能。这凸显了大语言模型在减轻如市场情绪分析等专业领域对大量技能密集型人工标注需求的潜力。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study's primary limitation lies in its reliance on LLM-generated weak labels, which may inherit the model's biases and lack the nuanced financial expertise of human experts, potentially limiting the model's accuracy in complex market sentiment scenarios. Furthermore, the ethical considerations surrounding the use of automated sentiment analysis for financial markets are acknowledged as a significant constraint for direct production deployment.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该研究的主要局限在于其依赖LLM生成的弱标签，这些标签可能继承模型的偏见且缺乏人类专家的细致金融知识，这可能在复杂的市场情绪场景中限制模型的准确性。此外，围绕将自动化情绪分析用于金融市场的伦理考量，被认为是阻碍其直接投入生产应用的一个重要制约因素。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Sentiment Analysis",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "LLMs to the Moon? Reddit Market Sentiment Analysis with Large Language Models",<br>    "publication_date": "2023-04-30"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Market sentiment analysis on social media content requires knowledge of both financial markets and social media jargon, which makes it a challenging task for human raters. The resulting lack of high-quality labeled data stands in the way of conventional supervised learning methods. In this work, we conduct a case study approaching this problem with semi-supervised learning using a large language model \(LLM\). We select Reddit as the target social media platform due to its broad coverage of topics"<br>}<br>DeepSeek Analysis Data<br>        Updated: 2026/3/8 22:19:07<br>        Status: matched<br>        Suggestion: None<br>        <br>        {<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Sentiment Analysis"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|

|[![Project](https://img.shields.io/badge/Project-View-blue)](https://netsys.surrey.ac.uk/datasets/slashdot/) [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ICWSM-blue)]()<br>[Conversation Kernels: A Flexible Mechanism to Learn Relevant Context for Online Conversation Unde...](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/ICWSM/article/view/35800) <br> Vibhor Agarwal,Arjoo Gupta,Suparna De,Nishanth Sastry <br> 2025-06-07 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Discourse and Pragmatic Analysis](#Discourse-and-Pragmatic-Analysis-26-papers), [Macro-Level Discourse Analysis](#Macro-Level-Discourse-Analysis-8-papers)]</span>|A flexible, structural context discovery framework that enhances conversation understanding by learning to attend to relevant topological neighborhoods within conversation trees.\[翻译\] 一个灵活的结构化上下文发现框架，通过学习关注对话树内相关的拓扑邻域来增强对话理解能力。|<div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;align-items:center"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/01e95642/Conversation Kernels.png"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/01e95642/Conversation Kernels2.png"></div>| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** Addressing the sparsity and context-dependency of individual online utterances, where traditional models often fail to capture implicit dependencies within conversation trees or introduce noise through indiscriminate context inclusion. \[翻译\] 针对在线言论固有的稀疏性和语境依赖性问题，即传统模型往往难以捕捉对话树内的隐式依赖关系，或因无差别地引入上下文而产生噪声 **[innovation]** The proposal of &quot;Conversation Kernels,&quot; a general-purpose mechanism that employs flexible topological shapes to retrieve fine-grained, structured conversational context, distinguishing itself by identifying the &quot;right&quot; structural neighborhood rather than merely increasing context length. \[翻译\] 提出了“对话核”这一通用机制，利用灵活的拓扑形状来检索细粒度的结构化对话上下文；其独到之处在于通过识别“正确”的结构邻域而非单纯增加上下文长度来理解对话。 **[method]** An end-to-end trained probabilistic framework that first retrieves relevant structural windows \(e.g., ancestors, neighbors\) via similarity scoring, and subsequently fuses these contexts through a weighted marginalization of the predictive distributions generated by a RoBERTa-based encoder. \[翻译\] 一个端到端训练的概率框架，首先通过相似度评分检索相关的结构化窗口（如祖先、邻居），随后通过对RoBERTa编码器生成的预测分布进行加权求和（边缘化），从而融合这些上下文信息。\[通俗核心\]针对目标评论构建回复树，取几个窗口（如父评论窗口、1跳窗口），每个窗口中所有评论与原评论拼接，并进行预测，最后不同窗口与原评论的相关性经过softmax作为权重，对所有预测置信度加权和，得到最终结果 **[conclusion/contribution]** Extensive experiments on Slashdot data demonstrate that context-augmented kernels outperform baseline transformers by up to 20% in accuracy and surpass general-purpose LLMs \(GPT-3.5/4\) in specific categorization tasks, revealing that different tasks require distinct structural context patterns. \[翻译\] 在Slashdot数据上的广泛实验表明，上下文增强的核机制在准确率上比基线Transformer模型高出20%，并在特定分类任务中超越了通用大语言模型（GPT-3.5/4），揭示了不同任务需要截然不同的结构化上下文模式。 **[limitation/future]** The approach relies heavily on explicit tree-structured reply threads, limiting applicability in flat discussion formats, and may face cold-start challenges in early-stage conversations with sparse context. \[翻译\] 该方法严重依赖显式的树状回复线索，限制了其在扁平化讨论形式中的适用性，并且在上下文稀疏的对话早期阶段可能面临冷启动挑战。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** Addressing the sparsity and context-dependency of individual online utterances, where traditional models often fail to capture implicit dependencies within conversation trees or introduce noise through indiscriminate context inclusion.<br>\[翻译\] 针对在线言论固有的稀疏性和语境依赖性问题，即传统模型往往难以捕捉对话树内的隐式依赖关系，或因无差别地引入上下文而产生噪声<br>**[innovation]** The proposal of "Conversation Kernels," a general-purpose mechanism that employs flexible topological shapes to retrieve fine-grained, structured conversational context, distinguishing itself by identifying the "right" structural neighborhood rather than merely increasing context length.<br>\[翻译\] 提出了“对话核”这一通用机制，利用灵活的拓扑形状来检索细粒度的结构化对话上下文；其独到之处在于通过识别“正确”的结构邻域而非单纯增加上下文长度来理解对话。<br>**[method]** An end-to-end trained probabilistic framework that first retrieves relevant structural windows \(e.g., ancestors, neighbors\) via similarity scoring, and subsequently fuses these contexts through a weighted marginalization of the predictive distributions generated by a RoBERTa-based encoder.<br>\[翻译\] 一个端到端训练的概率框架，首先通过相似度评分检索相关的结构化窗口（如祖先、邻居），随后通过对RoBERTa编码器生成的预测分布进行加权求和（边缘化），从而融合这些上下文信息。\[通俗核心\]针对目标评论构建回复树，取几个窗口（如父评论窗口、1跳窗口），每个窗口中所有评论与原评论拼接，并进行预测，最后不同窗口与原评论的相关性经过softmax作为权重，对所有预测置信度加权和，得到最终结果<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** Extensive experiments on Slashdot data demonstrate that context-augmented kernels outperform baseline transformers by up to 20% in accuracy and surpass general-purpose LLMs \(GPT-3.5/4\) in specific categorization tasks, revealing that different tasks require distinct structural context patterns.<br>\[翻译\] 在Slashdot数据上的广泛实验表明，上下文增强的核机制在准确率上比基线Transformer模型高出20%，并在特定分类任务中超越了通用大语言模型（GPT-3.5/4），揭示了不同任务需要截然不同的结构化上下文模式。<br>**[limitation/future]** The approach relies heavily on explicit tree-structured reply threads, limiting applicability in flat discussion formats, and may face cold-start challenges in early-stage conversations with sparse context.<br>\[翻译\] 该方法严重依赖显式的树状回复线索，限制了其在扁平化讨论形式中的适用性，并且在上下文稀疏的对话早期阶段可能面临冷启动挑战。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【基础技术—上下文感知方法】可用于所有内容理解任务，论文中的实验用的是是否受欢迎二分类<br>\[分类考量\]最终本质上是对评论的性质进行分类，属于语用分析，ai说具体是幽默检测<br>\[引用文\]To better bridge pattern recognition with social interaction structures, Agarwal et al. \(2025\) proposed Conversation Kernels, an end-to-end framework designed to extract fine-grained context from conversation trees. By dynamically retrieving and weighting specific topological neighborhoods \(e.g., ancestors or siblings\) rather than ingesting linear history, their method effectively filters noise inherent in social discussions. This structural selectivity demonstrates that incorporating explicit interaction topologies is crucial for accurately decoding the nature of online conversations, yielding performance that surpasses even general-purpose Large Language Models like GPT-4.<br>\[翻译\]<br>为了更好地将模式识别与社会互动结构联系起来，Agarwal等人 \(2025\) 提出了“对话核（Conversation Kernels）”，这是一种旨在从对话树中提取细粒度上下文的端到端框架。通过动态检索并加权特定的拓扑邻域（如祖先或兄弟节点）而非摄入线性历史，该方法有效地过滤了社会讨论中固有的噪声。这种结构选择性证明，结合显式的互动拓扑对于准确解读在线对话的性质至关重要，其表现甚至超越了像 GPT-4 这样的通用大语言模型。</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Findings%202023-blue)]()<br>[Contrastive learning of sociopragmatic meaning in social media](https://aclanthology.org/2023.findings-acl.152/) <br> Chiyu Zhang, Muhammad Abdul-Mageed, Ganesh Jawahar <br> 2023-07||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Discourse and Pragmatic Analysis"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Contrastive learning of sociopragmatic meaning in social media",<br>    "publication_date": "2023-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Recent progress in representation and contrastive learning in NLP has not widely considered the class of sociopragmatic meaning \(i.e., meaning in interaction within different language communities\). To bridge this gap, we propose a novel framework for learning task-agnostic representations transferable to a wide range of sociopragmatic tasks \(e.g., emotion, hate speech, humor, sarcasm\). Our framework outperforms other contrastive learning frameworks for both in-domain and out-of-domain data, acro"<br>}</div></details></div>|

###### Micro-Level Pragmatic Expressions (17 papers)

| Title & Info | Analogy Summary | Pipeline | Summary |
|:--| :---: | :----: | :---: |
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202025-blue)]()<br>[Sheep's skin, wolf's deeds: Are LLMs ready for metaphorical implicit hate speech?](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.814/) <br> Jingjie Zeng, Liang Yang, Zekun Wang, Yuanyuan Sun, Hongfei Lin <br> 2025-07 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Hate Speech Analysis](#Hate-Speech-Analysis-34-papers), [Micro-Level Pragmatic Expressions](#Micro-Level-Pragmatic-Expressions-17-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Metaphorical implicit hate speech detection: LLMs struggle to detect hate speech disguised as metaphorical rhetoric, revealing a critical vulnerability in current AI safety mechanisms. \[翻译\] 隐喻性隐式仇恨言论检测：大语言模型难以识别伪装为隐喻修辞的仇恨言论，暴露了当前AI安全机制的关键漏洞。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper investigates the vulnerability of state-of-the-art Large Language Models \(LLMs\) in detecting metaphorical implicit hate speech, a covert form of harmful content that bypasses conventional safety filters by disguising hateful intent within seemingly benign metaphorical expressions. \[翻译\] 本文研究了先进大语言模型在检测隐喻性隐式仇恨言论方面的脆弱性，这类有害内容通过将仇恨意图伪装在看似良性的隐喻表达中，从而规避常规安全过滤器。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes a novel method for generating metaphorical implicit hate speech by combining Jailbreaking strategies with Energy-based Constrained Decoding, and designs a small Metaphor Analysis Module to guide the generation. This approach exposes critical vulnerabilities in state-of-the-art LLMs and specialized safety models, which frequently fail to detect such rhetorically disguised hate content. \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种结合越狱策略与基于能量的约束解码的新方法，用于生成隐喻性隐式仇恨言论，并设计了一个小型隐喻分析模块来引导生成。该方法揭示了当前最先进的大语言模型和专用安全模型的关键漏洞，这些模型经常无法检测此类修辞性伪装的仇恨内容。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors propose a method to generate metaphorical implicit hate speech by combining Jailbreaking strategies with Energy-based Constrained Decoding. A white-box attack is implemented on a 7B-parameter LLM \(Vicuna-7B-v1.5\) using Langevin dynamics to optimize an adversarial prompt. The core of the method is a custom energy function that balances three objectives: maximizing the attack success rate \(E_att\), maintaining prompt fluency \(E_flu\), and ensuring the generated output uses effective metaphorical rhetoric to convey hate \(E_met\). The metaphorical quality is evaluated by a small, fine-tuned Metaphor Analysis Module \(approx. 1.2B parameters\), which combines a contrastive learning model \(bert-large-uncased\) to measure semantic similarity and a seq2seq model \(flan-t5-large\) to interpret metaphors, guiding the generation towards covert hateful expressions.  \[翻译\] 作者提出了一种结合越狱策略和基于能量的约束解码来生成隐喻性隐 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper reveals that current LLMs are vulnerable to metaphorical implicit hate speech, which often evades detection by disguising hateful intent within seemingly harmless metaphorical expressions. The authors propose a generation method combining jailbreaking and energy-based constrained decoding to produce such content, and demonstrate that even state-of-the-art LLMs and specialized safety models frequently fail to identify or block it, highlighting a critical gap in AI safety mechanisms.  \[翻译\] 本文揭示了当前大语言模型对隐喻性隐式仇恨言论的脆弱性，这类言论常通过将仇恨意图伪装在看似无害的隐喻表达中来逃避检测。作者提出了一种结合越狱攻击和基于能量的约束解码的生成方法来制造此类内容，并证明即使是先进的大语言模型和专门的安全模型也常常无法识别或阻止它，凸显了AI安全机制中的一个关键缺陷。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] Current LLMs, including advanced models like GPT-4o and specialized detectors like ShieldGemma, exhibit significant vulnerabilities in detecting metaphorical implicit hate speech. They often misinterpret such content as harmless, failing to prevent its propagation. The proposed generation method, while effective for research, also highlights the risk of LLMs being exploited to produce such covert hate speech. \[翻译\] 当前的大语言模型，包括GPT-4o等先进模型和ShieldGemma等专用检测器，在检测隐喻性隐式仇恨言论方面存在显著漏洞。它们常将此类内容误判为无害，未能有效阻止其传播。论文提出的生成方法虽对研究有效，但也凸显了大语言模型被利用来生成此类隐蔽仇恨言论的风险。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper investigates the vulnerability of state-of-the-art Large Language Models \(LLMs\) in detecting metaphorical implicit hate speech, a covert form of harmful content that bypasses conventional safety filters by disguising hateful intent within seemingly benign metaphorical expressions. \[翻译\] 本文研究了先进大语言模型在检测隐喻性隐式仇恨言论方面的脆弱性，这类有害内容通过将仇恨意图伪装在看似良性的隐喻表达中，从而规避常规安全过滤器。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes a novel method for generating metaphorical implicit hate speech by combining Jailbreaking strategies with Energy-based Constrained Decoding, and designs a small Metaphor Analysis Module to guide the generation. This approach exposes critical vulnerabilities in state-of-the-art LLMs and specialized safety models, which frequently fail to detect such rhetorically disguised hate content. \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种结合越狱策略与基于能量的约束解码的新方法，用于生成隐喻性隐式仇恨言论，并设计了一个小型隐喻分析模块来引导生成。该方法揭示了当前最先进的大语言模型和专用安全模型的关键漏洞，这些模型经常无法检测此类修辞性伪装的仇恨内容。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors propose a method to generate metaphorical implicit hate speech by combining Jailbreaking strategies with Energy-based Constrained Decoding. A white-box attack is implemented on a 7B-parameter LLM \(Vicuna-7B-v1.5\) using Langevin dynamics to optimize an adversarial prompt. The core of the method is a custom energy function that balances three objectives: maximizing the attack success rate \(E_att\), maintaining prompt fluency \(E_flu\), and ensuring the generated output uses effective metaphorical rhetoric to convey hate \(E_met\). The metaphorical quality is evaluated by a small, fine-tuned Metaphor Analysis Module \(approx. 1.2B parameters\), which combines a contrastive learning model \(bert-large-uncased\) to measure semantic similarity and a seq2seq model \(flan-t5-large\) to interpret metaphors, guiding the generation towards covert hateful expressions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>作者提出了一种结合越狱策略和基于能量的约束解码来生成隐喻性隐<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper reveals that current LLMs are vulnerable to metaphorical implicit hate speech, which often evades detection by disguising hateful intent within seemingly harmless metaphorical expressions. The authors propose a generation method combining jailbreaking and energy-based constrained decoding to produce such content, and demonstrate that even state-of-the-art LLMs and specialized safety models frequently fail to identify or block it, highlighting a critical gap in AI safety mechanisms.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文揭示了当前大语言模型对隐喻性隐式仇恨言论的脆弱性，这类言论常通过将仇恨意图伪装在看似无害的隐喻表达中来逃避检测。作者提出了一种结合越狱攻击和基于能量的约束解码的生成方法来制造此类内容，并证明即使是先进的大语言模型和专门的安全模型也常常无法识别或阻止它，凸显了AI安全机制中的一个关键缺陷。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] Current LLMs, including advanced models like GPT-4o and specialized detectors like ShieldGemma, exhibit significant vulnerabilities in detecting metaphorical implicit hate speech. They often misinterpret such content as harmless, failing to prevent its propagation. The proposed generation method, while effective for research, also highlights the risk of LLMs being exploited to produce such covert hate speech. \[翻译\] 当前的大语言模型，包括GPT-4o等先进模型和ShieldGemma等专用检测器，在检测隐喻性隐式仇恨言论方面存在显著漏洞。它们常将此类内容误判为无害，未能有效阻止其传播。论文提出的生成方法虽对研究有效，但也凸显了大语言模型被利用来生成此类隐蔽仇恨言论的风险。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【隐式】使用一种方法成功生成看似无害的隐喻性仇恨内容，成功绕过最先进的LLM的检测，揭示了当前的LLM仍然在深层隐喻语义理解上有所欠缺，<br>This paper proposes a novel method for generating metaphorical implicit hate speech by combining Jailbreaking strategies with Energy-based Constrained Decoding</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202023-blue)]()<br>[Just like a human would, direct access to sarcasm augmented with potential result and reaction](https://aclanthology.org/2023.acl-long.566/) <br> Changrong Min, Ximing Li, Liang Yang, Zhilin Wang, Bo Xu, Hongfei Lin <br> 2023-07||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Sarcasm Detection"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Just like a human would, direct access to sarcasm augmented with potential result and reaction",<br>    "publication_date": "2023-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Sarcasm, as a form of irony conveying mockery and contempt, has been widespread in social media such as Twitter and Weibo, where the sarcastic text is commonly characterized as an incongruity between the surface positive and negative situation. Naturally, it has an urgent demand to automatically identify sarcasm from social media, so as to illustrate people's real views toward specific targets. In this paper, we develop a novel sarcasm detection method, namely Sarcasm Detector with Augmentation "<br>}</div></details></div>|

| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-The%202023%20Conference%20on%20Empirical%20Methods%20in%20Natural%20Language%20Processing-blue)]()<br>[Can language models laugh at YouTube short-form videos?](https://openreview.net/forum?id=okV4KG4kMg) <br> Dayoon Ko, Sangho Lee, Gunhee Kim <br> 2023-12-01||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Humor Recognition"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Can language models laugh at YouTube short-form videos?",<br>    "publication_date": "2023/12/01"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "As short-form funny videos on social networks are gaining popularity, it becomes demanding for AI models to understand them for better communication with humans. Using a video filtering pipeline with GPT-3.5, we verify both verbal and visual elements contributing to humo"<br>}</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Findings%202025-blue)]()<br>[FanChuan: A multilingual and graph-structured benchmark for parody detection and analysis](https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-acl.1131/) <br> Yilun Zheng, Sha Li, Fangkun Wu, Yang Ziyi, Lin Hongchao, Zhichao Hu, Cai Xinjun, Ziming Wang, Jinxuan Chen, Sitao Luan, Jiahao Xu, Lihui Chen <br> 2025-07||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Discourse and Pragmatic Analysis"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "FanChuan: A multilingual and graph-structured benchmark for parody detection and analysis",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Parody is an emerging phenomenon on social media, where individuals imitate a role or position opposite to their own, often for humor, provocation, or controversy. However, the study of parody is hindered by limited available data and deficient diversity in current datasets. To bridge this gap, we bu"<br>}</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Findings%202025-blue)]()<br>[Chumor 2.0: Towards better benchmarking Chinese humor understanding from \\(ruo zhi ba\\)](https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-acl.1122/) <br> Ruiqi He, Yushu He, Longju Bai, Jiarui Liu, Zhenjie Sun, Zenghao Tang, He Wang, Hanchen Xia, Rada Mihalcea, Naihao Deng <br> 2025-07||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Humor Recognition"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Chumor 2.0: Towards better benchmarking Chinese humor understanding from \(ruo zhi ba\)",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Existing humor datasets and evaluations predominantly focus on English, leaving limited resources for culturally nuanced humor in non-English languages like Chinese. ###### Macro-Level Discourse Analysis (8 papers)

| Title & Info | Analogy Summary | Pipeline | Summary |

|[![Project](https://img.shields.io/badge/Project-View-blue)](https://netsys.surrey.ac.uk/datasets/slashdot/) [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ICWSM-blue)]()<br>[Conversation Kernels: A Flexible Mechanism to Learn Relevant Context for Online Conversation Unde...](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/ICWSM/article/view/35800) <br> Vibhor Agarwal,Arjoo Gupta,Suparna De,Nishanth Sastry <br> 2025-06-07 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Discourse and Pragmatic Analysis](#Discourse-and-Pragmatic-Analysis-26-papers), [Macro-Level Discourse Analysis](#Macro-Level-Discourse-Analysis-8-papers)]</span>|A flexible, structural context discovery framework that enhances conversation understanding by learning to attend to relevant topological neighborhoods within conversation trees.\[翻译\] 一个灵活的结构化上下文发现框架，通过学习关注对话树内相关的拓扑邻域来增强对话理解能力。|<div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;align-items:center"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/01e95642/Conversation Kernels.png"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/01e95642/Conversation Kernels2.png"></div>| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** Addressing the sparsity and context-dependency of individual online utterances, where traditional models often fail to capture implicit dependencies within conversation trees or introduce noise through indiscriminate context inclusion. \[翻译\] 针对在线言论固有的稀疏性和语境依赖性问题，即传统模型往往难以捕捉对话树内的隐式依赖关系，或因无差别地引入上下文而产生噪声 **[innovation]** The proposal of &quot;Conversation Kernels,&quot; a general-purpose mechanism that employs flexible topological shapes to retrieve fine-grained, structured conversational context, distinguishing itself by identifying the &quot;right&quot; structural neighborhood rather than merely increasing context length. \[翻译\] 提出了“对话核”这一通用机制，利用灵活的拓扑形状来检索细粒度的结构化对话上下文；其独到之处在于通过识别“正确”的结构邻域而非单纯增加上下文长度来理解对话。 **[method]** An end-to-end trained probabilistic framework that first retrieves relevant structural windows \(e.g., ancestors, neighbors\) via similarity scoring, and subsequently fuses these contexts through a weighted marginalization of the predictive distributions generated by a RoBERTa-based encoder. \[翻译\] 一个端到端训练的概率框架，首先通过相似度评分检索相关的结构化窗口（如祖先、邻居），随后通过对RoBERTa编码器生成的预测分布进行加权求和（边缘化），从而融合这些上下文信息。\[通俗核心\]针对目标评论构建回复树，取几个窗口（如父评论窗口、1跳窗口），每个窗口中所有评论与原评论拼接，并进行预测，最后不同窗口与原评论的相关性经过softmax作为权重，对所有预测置信度加权和，得到最终结果 **[conclusion/contribution]** Extensive experiments on Slashdot data demonstrate that context-augmented kernels outperform baseline transformers by up to 20% in accuracy and surpass general-purpose LLMs \(GPT-3.5/4\) in specific categorization tasks, revealing that different tasks require distinct structural context patterns. \[翻译\] 在Slashdot数据上的广泛实验表明，上下文增强的核机制在准确率上比基线Transformer模型高出20%，并在特定分类任务中超越了通用大语言模型（GPT-3.5/4），揭示了不同任务需要截然不同的结构化上下文模式。 **[limitation/future]** The approach relies heavily on explicit tree-structured reply threads, limiting applicability in flat discussion formats, and may face cold-start challenges in early-stage conversations with sparse context. \[翻译\] 该方法严重依赖显式的树状回复线索，限制了其在扁平化讨论形式中的适用性，并且在上下文稀疏的对话早期阶段可能面临冷启动挑战。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** Addressing the sparsity and context-dependency of individual online utterances, where traditional models often fail to capture implicit dependencies within conversation trees or introduce noise through indiscriminate context inclusion.<br>\[翻译\] 针对在线言论固有的稀疏性和语境依赖性问题，即传统模型往往难以捕捉对话树内的隐式依赖关系，或因无差别地引入上下文而产生噪声<br>**[innovation]** The proposal of "Conversation Kernels," a general-purpose mechanism that employs flexible topological shapes to retrieve fine-grained, structured conversational context, distinguishing itself by identifying the "right" structural neighborhood rather than merely increasing context length.<br>\[翻译\] 提出了“对话核”这一通用机制，利用灵活的拓扑形状来检索细粒度的结构化对话上下文；其独到之处在于通过识别“正确”的结构邻域而非单纯增加上下文长度来理解对话。<br>**[method]** An end-to-end trained probabilistic framework that first retrieves relevant structural windows \(e.g., ancestors, neighbors\) via similarity scoring, and subsequently fuses these contexts through a weighted marginalization of the predictive distributions generated by a RoBERTa-based encoder.<br>\[翻译\] 一个端到端训练的概率框架，首先通过相似度评分检索相关的结构化窗口（如祖先、邻居），随后通过对RoBERTa编码器生成的预测分布进行加权求和（边缘化），从而融合这些上下文信息。\[通俗核心\]针对目标评论构建回复树，取几个窗口（如父评论窗口、1跳窗口），每个窗口中所有评论与原评论拼接，并进行预测，最后不同窗口与原评论的相关性经过softmax作为权重，对所有预测置信度加权和，得到最终结果<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** Extensive experiments on Slashdot data demonstrate that context-augmented kernels outperform baseline transformers by up to 20% in accuracy and surpass general-purpose LLMs \(GPT-3.5/4\) in specific categorization tasks, revealing that different tasks require distinct structural context patterns.<br>\[翻译\] 在Slashdot数据上的广泛实验表明，上下文增强的核机制在准确率上比基线Transformer模型高出20%，并在特定分类任务中超越了通用大语言模型（GPT-3.5/4），揭示了不同任务需要截然不同的结构化上下文模式。<br>**[limitation/future]** The approach relies heavily on explicit tree-structured reply threads, limiting applicability in flat discussion formats, and may face cold-start challenges in early-stage conversations with sparse context.<br>\[翻译\] 该方法严重依赖显式的树状回复线索，限制了其在扁平化讨论形式中的适用性，并且在上下文稀疏的对话早期阶段可能面临冷启动挑战。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【基础技术—上下文感知方法】可用于所有内容理解任务，论文中的实验用的是是否受欢迎二分类<br>\[分类考量\]最终本质上是对评论的性质进行分类，属于语用分析，ai说具体是幽默检测<br>\[引用文\]To better bridge pattern recognition with social interaction structures, Agarwal et al. \(2025\) proposed Conversation Kernels, an end-to-end framework designed to extract fine-grained context from conversation trees. By dynamically retrieving and weighting specific topological neighborhoods \(e.g., ancestors or siblings\) rather than ingesting linear history, their method effectively filters noise inherent in social discussions. This structural selectivity demonstrates that incorporating explicit interaction topologies is crucial for accurately decoding the nature of online conversations, yielding performance that surpasses even general-purpose Large Language Models like GPT-4.<br>\[翻译\]<br>为了更好地将模式识别与社会互动结构联系起来，Agarwal等人 \(2025\) 提出了“对话核（Conversation Kernels）”，这是一种旨在从对话树中提取细粒度上下文的端到端框架。通过动态检索并加权特定的拓扑邻域（如祖先或兄弟节点）而非摄入线性历史，该方法有效地过滤了社会讨论中固有的噪声。这种结构选择性证明，结合显式的互动拓扑对于准确解读在线对话的性质至关重要，其表现甚至超越了像 GPT-4 这样的通用大语言模型。</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202024-blue)]()<br>[Silent signals, loud impact: LLMs for word-sense disambiguation of coded dog whistles](https://aclanthology.org/2024.acl-long.675/) <br> Julia Kruk, Michela Marchini, Rijul Magu, Caleb Ziems, David Muchlinski, Diyi Yang <br> 2024-08 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Hate Speech Analysis](#Hate-Speech-Analysis-34-papers), [Macro-Level Discourse Analysis](#Macro-Level-Discourse-Analysis-8-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Silent Signals: LLMs decode covert hate speech by disambiguating dog whistles as a word-sense task, creating a large dataset for detection. \[翻译\] Silent Signals：大语言模型通过将狗哨语作为词义消歧任务进行解码，创建了一个用于检测的大型数据集。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper addresses the challenge of detecting coded hate speech, specifically &quot;dog whistles,&quot; in social media and political discourse. Dog whistles are terms with dual meanings—one innocuous and one discriminatory—allowing speakers to evade automated hate speech detection systems while maintaining plausible deniability. The authors aim to develop a method for word-sense disambiguation to reliably identify when such terms are used with their harmful, coded intent.  \[翻译\] 本文旨在解决在社交媒体和政治话语中检测编码仇恨言论（特别是“狗哨”言论）的挑战。“狗哨”术语具有双重含义——一种是无害的，另一种是歧视性的——这使得发言者能够在保持表面合理否认的同时，规避自动仇恨言论检测系统。作者的目标是开发一种词义消歧方法，以可靠地识别这些术语何时被用于其有害的编码意图。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper introduces a novel approach for detecting and disambiguating coded dog whistles in social media and political discourse using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). It frames dog whistle detection as a word-sense disambiguation \(WSD\) task, where the goal is to distinguish between the innocuous and coded, discriminatory meanings of terms. The core innovation is the creation of the &quot;Silent Signals&quot; dataset—the largest collection of disambiguated dog whistle examples \(16,550 instances\)—by applying this LLM-based WSD method to a large corpus of formal \(Congressional records\) and informal \(Reddit\) communication. This work provides a new resource and methodology for improving hate speech detection systems, which often fail to identify such covert language, and for studying the evolution of coded political communication.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种利用大语言模型（LLM）检测和消歧社交媒体及政治话语中编码化“狗哨”（dog whistle）的新方法。它将狗哨检测构建 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors propose a novel method for word-sense disambiguation \(WSD\) of coded dog whistles using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). The core approach involves a multi-stage pipeline: \(1\) collecting a large corpus of potential dog whistle instances from formal \(U.S. Congressional records, 1900-2023\) and informal \(Reddit, 2005-2022\) communication via keyword matching based on an existing glossary; \(2\) designing and evaluating LLM prompts for the specific tasks of automatic dog whistle detection and contrastive disambiguation \(identifying coded vs. non-coded uses of a given term\); \(3\) leveraging the best-performing LLM-based disambiguation method to automatically filter and label the collected instances, thereby constructing the high-confidence &quot;Silent Signals&quot; dataset of 16,550 coded dog whistle examples.  \[翻译\] 作者提出了一种利用大语言模型（LLM）对编码化狗哨语进行词义消歧的新方法。其核心 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper introduces a novel approach for word-sense disambiguation of coded dog whistles using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). It presents the &quot;Silent Signals&quot; dataset, the largest collection of disambiguated dog whistle examples, created by applying LLMs to formal \(Congressional records\) and informal \(Reddit\) communication. The work demonstrates that LLMs, particularly in few-shot settings, can effectively detect and disambiguate dog whistles, outperforming non-expert human baselines. This enables the large-scale creation of labeled data for studying the evolution of coded hate speech and improving content moderation systems. \[翻译\] 本文提出了一种利用大语言模型进行编码化“狗哨”术语词义消歧的新方法。它发布了“Silent Signals”数据集，这是通过将LLM应用于正式（国会记录）和非正式（Reddit）交流而创建的最大规模的已消歧狗哨用例集合。研究表明，LLM（特别是在少样本设置下）能够有效 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The paper presents a novel LLM-based method for word-sense disambiguation of coded dog whistles and constructs the largest dataset of disambiguated examples. However, the approach relies on keyword matching from an existing glossary, which may not capture emerging or context-dependent dog whistles not in the seed list. Additionally, the evaluation of LLMs is limited to a small synthetic dataset, and the performance, while promising, still falls short of expert-level accuracy, indicating room for improvement in model generalization and robustness for this nuanced task.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种基于LLM的新方法，用于对编码的狗哨语进行词义消歧，并构建了最大的消歧示例数据集。然而，该方法依赖于从现有词汇表中进行关键词匹配，可能无法捕捉到不在种子列表中的新兴或上下文相关的狗哨语。此外，对LLM的评估仅限于一个小的合成数据集，其性能虽有前景，但仍未达到专家级准确度，表明在该微妙任务上，">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper addresses the challenge of detecting coded hate speech, specifically "dog whistles," in social media and political discourse. Dog whistles are terms with dual meanings—one innocuous and one discriminatory—allowing speakers to evade automated hate speech detection systems while maintaining plausible deniability. The authors aim to develop a method for word-sense disambiguation to reliably identify when such terms are used with their harmful, coded intent.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文旨在解决在社交媒体和政治话语中检测编码仇恨言论（特别是“狗哨”言论）的挑战。“狗哨”术语具有双重含义——一种是无害的，另一种是歧视性的——这使得发言者能够在保持表面合理否认的同时，规避自动仇恨言论检测系统。作者的目标是开发一种词义消歧方法，以可靠地识别这些术语何时被用于其有害的编码意图。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper introduces a novel approach for detecting and disambiguating coded dog whistles in social media and political discourse using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). It frames dog whistle detection as a word-sense disambiguation \(WSD\) task, where the goal is to distinguish between the innocuous and coded, discriminatory meanings of terms. The core innovation is the creation of the "Silent Signals" dataset—the largest collection of disambiguated dog whistle examples \(16,550 instances\)—by applying this LLM-based WSD method to a large corpus of formal \(Congressional records\) and informal \(Reddit\) communication. This work provides a new resource and methodology for improving hate speech detection systems, which often fail to identify such covert language, and for studying the evolution of coded political communication.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种利用大语言模型（LLM）检测和消歧社交媒体及政治话语中编码化“狗哨”（dog whistle）的新方法。它将狗哨检测构建<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The authors propose a novel method for word-sense disambiguation \(WSD\) of coded dog whistles using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). The core approach involves a multi-stage pipeline: \(1\) collecting a large corpus of potential dog whistle instances from formal \(U.S. Congressional records, 1900-2023\) and informal \(Reddit, 2005-2022\) communication via keyword matching based on an existing glossary; \(2\) designing and evaluating LLM prompts for the specific tasks of automatic dog whistle detection and contrastive disambiguation \(identifying coded vs. non-coded uses of a given term\); \(3\) leveraging the best-performing LLM-based disambiguation method to automatically filter and label the collected instances, thereby constructing the high-confidence "Silent Signals" dataset of 16,550 coded dog whistle examples.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>作者提出了一种利用大语言模型（LLM）对编码化狗哨语进行词义消歧的新方法。其核心<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper introduces a novel approach for word-sense disambiguation of coded dog whistles using Large Language Models \(LLMs\). It presents the "Silent Signals" dataset, the largest collection of disambiguated dog whistle examples, created by applying LLMs to formal \(Congressional records\) and informal \(Reddit\) communication. The work demonstrates that LLMs, particularly in few-shot settings, can effectively detect and disambiguate dog whistles, outperforming non-expert human baselines. This enables the large-scale creation of labeled data for studying the evolution of coded hate speech and improving content moderation systems. \[翻译\] 本文提出了一种利用大语言模型进行编码化“狗哨”术语词义消歧的新方法。它发布了“Silent Signals”数据集，这是通过将LLM应用于正式（国会记录）和非正式（Reddit）交流而创建的最大规模的已消歧狗哨用例集合。研究表明，LLM（特别是在少样本设置下）能够有效<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The paper presents a novel LLM-based method for word-sense disambiguation of coded dog whistles and constructs the largest dataset of disambiguated examples. However, the approach relies on keyword matching from an existing glossary, which may not capture emerging or context-dependent dog whistles not in the seed list. Additionally, the evaluation of LLMs is limited to a small synthetic dataset, and the performance, while promising, still falls short of expert-level accuracy, indicating room for improvement in model generalization and robustness for this nuanced task.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种基于LLM的新方法，用于对编码的狗哨语进行词义消歧，并构建了最大的消歧示例数据集。然而，该方法依赖于从现有词汇表中进行关键词匹配，可能无法捕捉到不在种子列表中的新兴或上下文相关的狗哨语。此外，对LLM的评估仅限于一个小的合成数据集，其性能虽有前景，但仍未达到专家级准确度，表明在该微妙任务上，</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【隐式】</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202025-blue)]()<br>[Graphically speaking: Unmasking abuse in social media with conversation insights](https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.894/) <br> Célia Nouri, Jean-Philippe Cointet, Chloé Clavel <br> 2025-07 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Hate Speech Analysis](#Hate-Speech-Analysis-34-papers), [Macro-Level Discourse Analysis](#Macro-Level-Discourse-Analysis-8-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Modeling social media conversations as graphs to detect abusive language, akin to mapping a conversation's structure to uncover hidden toxicity patterns. \[翻译\] 将社交媒体对话建模为图以检测辱骂性语言，类似于通过对话结构映射揭示隐藏的毒性模式。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/365c16cc/Graphically Speaking.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] Detecting abusive language in social media is challenging as it often depends on conversational context, including the content and reply structure of preceding comments. Traditional models often ignore this context, while recent NLP approaches use oversimplified context representations, leading to inconsistent results. This paper aims to address these limitations by proposing a graph-based framework to model conversations for more accurate abuse detection.  \[翻译\] 社交媒体中辱骂性语言的检测具有挑战性，因为其判断通常依赖于对话上下文，包括先前评论的内容和回复结构。传统模型常忽略此上下文，而近期的NLP方法则使用过于简化的上下文表示，导致结果不一致。本文旨在通过提出一个基于图的框架来建模对话，以解决这些局限，实现更准确的辱骂检测。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes a novel graph-based framework for context-aware abusive language detection \(ALD\) on social media. It models conversation threads as reply-structure graphs and employs Graph Neural Networks \(GNNs\) to propagate contextual information. A key innovation is an affordance-based graph trimming strategy that mimics the user-visible context on platforms like Reddit, optimizing computational efficiency while preserving relevant conversational structure. The method systematically outperforms both context-agnostic and flat context-aware baselines, demonstrating the critical role of structured conversational modeling for ALD.  \[翻译\] 本文提出了一种新颖的、基于图神经网络的社交媒体上下文感知辱骂性语言检测框架。其核心创新在于将对话线程建模为回复结构图，并采用图神经网络进行上下文信息传播。其中，一个关键创新是提出了基于平台交互特性的图剪枝策略，模拟了用户在Reddit等平台上的可见上下文，在保持相关对话结构的同时优化了计算效率。该方法系统性地超越了无视上下文及扁平化上下文 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a graph-based framework for abusive language detection \(ALD\) in social media conversations. It models conversation threads as directed graphs where nodes represent comments and edges represent reply relationships. A key contribution is an &quot;affordance-based&quot; graph trimming strategy that retains only the context likely visible to a user when replying, approximating the cognitive context. The core model is a Graph Attention Network \(GNN\) that aggregates contextual information from neighboring nodes \(comments\) in the graph. Node features are initialized using BERT embeddings of the comment text. The GNN dynamically learns the importance of different contextual comments through attention mechanisms to classify the target comment as abusive or not.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种基于图的社交媒体对话辱骂性语言检测框架。它将对话线程建模为有向图，其中节点代表评论，边代表回复关系。一个关键贡献是“可供性”图剪枝策略，该策略仅保留用户回复时可能看到的上下文，以近似认知上下文。核心 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a graph-based framework for abusive language detection \(ALD\) in social media conversations, modeling reply structures as graphs and employing Graph Neural Networks \(GNNs\). It demonstrates that explicitly modeling conversational topology with GNNs significantly outperforms both context-agnostic and flat context-aware baselines, establishing GNNs as a robust method for context-aware ALD.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种用于社交媒体对话中辱骂性语言检测（ALD）的图神经网络框架，将回复结构建模为图。研究表明，利用GNN显式建模对话拓扑结构，其性能显著优于无视上下文和扁平化上下文感知的基线方法，从而确立了GNN作为上下文感知ALD的稳健方法。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed GNN-based approach for abusive language detection is primarily evaluated on Reddit data, and its generalizability to other social media platforms with different conversation structures \(e.g., non-threaded chats\) requires further validation. Additionally, the computational efficiency of the graph construction and GNN inference, while improved over large generative models, may still pose challenges for real-time, large-scale deployment.  \[翻译\] 该研究提出的基于图神经网络的辱骂性语言检测方法主要在Reddit数据上进行评估，其对于其他具有不同对话结构（例如非线程式聊天）的社交媒体平台的泛化能力仍需进一步验证。此外，图构建和图神经网络推理的计算效率虽然优于大型生成模型，但对于大规模实时部署仍可能构成挑战。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] Detecting abusive language in social media is challenging as it often depends on conversational context, including the content and reply structure of preceding comments. Traditional models often ignore this context, while recent NLP approaches use oversimplified context representations, leading to inconsistent results. This paper aims to address these limitations by proposing a graph-based framework to model conversations for more accurate abuse detection.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>社交媒体中辱骂性语言的检测具有挑战性，因为其判断通常依赖于对话上下文，包括先前评论的内容和回复结构。传统模型常忽略此上下文，而近期的NLP方法则使用过于简化的上下文表示，导致结果不一致。本文旨在通过提出一个基于图的框架来建模对话，以解决这些局限，实现更准确的辱骂检测。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes a novel graph-based framework for context-aware abusive language detection \(ALD\) on social media. It models conversation threads as reply-structure graphs and employs Graph Neural Networks \(GNNs\) to propagate contextual information. A key innovation is an affordance-based graph trimming strategy that mimics the user-visible context on platforms like Reddit, optimizing computational efficiency while preserving relevant conversational structure. The method systematically outperforms both context-agnostic and flat context-aware baselines, demonstrating the critical role of structured conversational modeling for ALD.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文提出了一种新颖的、基于图神经网络的社交媒体上下文感知辱骂性语言检测框架。其核心创新在于将对话线程建模为回复结构图，并采用图神经网络进行上下文信息传播。其中，一个关键创新是提出了基于平台交互特性的图剪枝策略，模拟了用户在Reddit等平台上的可见上下文，在保持相关对话结构的同时优化了计算效率。该方法系统性地超越了无视上下文及扁平化上下文<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a graph-based framework for abusive language detection \(ALD\) in social media conversations. It models conversation threads as directed graphs where nodes represent comments and edges represent reply relationships. A key contribution is an "affordance-based" graph trimming strategy that retains only the context likely visible to a user when replying, approximating the cognitive context. The core model is a Graph Attention Network \(GNN\) that aggregates contextual information from neighboring nodes \(comments\) in the graph. Node features are initialized using BERT embeddings of the comment text. The GNN dynamically learns the importance of different contextual comments through attention mechanisms to classify the target comment as abusive or not.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种基于图的社交媒体对话辱骂性语言检测框架。它将对话线程建模为有向图，其中节点代表评论，边代表回复关系。一个关键贡献是“可供性”图剪枝策略，该策略仅保留用户回复时可能看到的上下文，以近似认知上下文。核心<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a graph-based framework for abusive language detection \(ALD\) in social media conversations, modeling reply structures as graphs and employing Graph Neural Networks \(GNNs\). It demonstrates that explicitly modeling conversational topology with GNNs significantly outperforms both context-agnostic and flat context-aware baselines, establishing GNNs as a robust method for context-aware ALD.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种用于社交媒体对话中辱骂性语言检测（ALD）的图神经网络框架，将回复结构建模为图。研究表明，利用GNN显式建模对话拓扑结构，其性能显著优于无视上下文和扁平化上下文感知的基线方法，从而确立了GNN作为上下文感知ALD的稳健方法。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed GNN-based approach for abusive language detection is primarily evaluated on Reddit data, and its generalizability to other social media platforms with different conversation structures \(e.g., non-threaded chats\) requires further validation. Additionally, the computational efficiency of the graph construction and GNN inference, while improved over large generative models, may still pose challenges for real-time, large-scale deployment.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该研究提出的基于图神经网络的辱骂性语言检测方法主要在Reddit数据上进行评估，其对于其他具有不同对话结构（例如非线程式聊天）的社交媒体平台的泛化能力仍需进一步验证。此外，图构建和图神经网络推理的计算效率虽然优于大型生成模型，但对于大规模实时部署仍可能构成挑战。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Hate Speech Analysis"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Graphically speaking: Unmasking abuse in social media with conversation insights",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Detecting abusive language in social media conversations poses significant challenges, as identifying abusiveness often depends on the conversational context, characterized by the content and topology of preceding comments. Traditional Abusive Language Detection \(ALD\) models often overlook this context, which can lead to unreliable performance metrics. Recent Natural Language Processing \(NLP\) approaches that incorporate conversational context often rely on limited or overly simplified representa"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%202023-blue)]()<br>[From dogwhistles to bullhorns: Unveiling coded rhetoric with language models](https://aclanthology.org/2023.acl-long.845/) <br> Julia Mendelsohn, Ronan Le Bras, Yejin Choi, Maarten Sap <br> 2023-07 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Macro-Level Discourse Analysis](#Macro-Level-Discourse-Analysis-8-papers), [Hate Speech Analysis](#Hate-Speech-Analysis-34-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Dogwhistle detection: Decoding hidden hate speech with language models to bypass algorithmic moderation. \[翻译\] 狗哨检测：用语言模型解码隐藏的仇恨言论，以规避算法审查。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper conducts the first large-scale computational study of dogwhistles, coded expressions that convey hidden, often hateful meanings to in-groups while appearing innocuous to the general public. It aims to develop resources and methods to systematically identify, analyze, and detect such language, which evades both political accountability and algorithmic content moderation, thereby addressing a significant online risk.  \[翻译\] 本文首次对狗哨语（一种向特定群体传达隐含、通常带有仇恨含义，而对大众显得无害的编码表达）进行了大规模计算研究。其目标是开发资源和方法，以系统性地识别、分析和检测此类语言。这类语言既能规避政治问责，又能逃避算法内容审核，从而应对一个重大的在线风险。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper presents the first large-scale computational study of dogwhistles, introducing a typology and a curated glossary of over 300 dogwhistles with contextual examples. It pioneers the evaluation of a large language model \(GPT-3\) for identifying dogwhistles and their meanings, revealing variable performance across different types. Furthermore, it demonstrates that content containing dogwhistles can evade standard toxicity detection, highlighting a significant risk for online content moderation systems.  \[翻译\] 本文首次对狗哨进行了大规模计算研究，提出了一个分类法并整理了一个包含300多个狗哨及其上下文示例的词表。它开创性地评估了大语言模型（GPT-3）在识别狗哨及其含义方面的能力，揭示了其在不同类型狗哨上表现不一。此外，研究证明含有狗哨的内容可以规避标准的毒性检测，凸显了在线内容审核系统面临的一个重大风险。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The study develops a typology of dogwhistles and curates a glossary of over 300 dogwhistles with contextual examples. It then analyzes their usage in historical U.S. political speeches and evaluates GPT-3&#x27;s capability to identify dogwhistles and their meanings. Finally, it assesses the evasion of toxicity detection by content containing dogwhistles.  \[翻译\] 该研究开发了狗哨的类型学，并整理了一个包含300多个狗哨及其语境示例的术语表。随后，它分析了狗哨在美国历史政治演讲中的使用情况，并评估了GPT-3识别狗哨及其含义的能力。最后，它评估了包含狗哨的内容对毒性检测的规避情况。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper presents the first large-scale computational study of dogwhistles, developing a typology and a glossary of over 300 coded expressions. It analyzes their use in U.S. political speeches and evaluates GPT-3&#x27;s ability to identify them, finding performance varies by dogwhistle type and targeted group. The work demonstrates that content containing dogwhistles often evades standard toxicity detection, highlighting online risks and providing resources for future research in NLP and computational social science.  \[翻译\] 本文首次对狗哨语进行了大规模计算研究，建立了其类型学并整理了一个包含300多个编码表达的术语表。研究分析了狗哨语在美国政治演讲中的使用，并评估了GPT-3识别它们的能力，发现其性能因狗哨语类型和目标群体而异。研究表明，包含狗哨语的内容常能规避标准的毒性检测，揭示了其在线风险，并为自然语言处理和计算社会科学领域的未来研究提供了资源。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study&#x27;s primary limitation is its reliance on GPT-3 for evaluation, whose performance in identifying dogwhistles varies significantly across different types and targeted groups, indicating a lack of robustness. Furthermore, the curated glossary, while extensive, may not be exhaustive and requires continuous updates to capture evolving coded language. The analysis is also confined to historical U.S. political speeches, limiting the generalizability of findings to other contexts or contemporary online platforms.  \[翻译\] 该研究的主要局限在于其评估依赖于GPT-3，而该模型在识别不同类别和针对不同群体的狗哨语时表现差异显著，表明其鲁棒性不足。此外，所编纂的词汇表虽然广泛，但可能并不详尽，需要持续更新以捕捉不断演变的编码语言。分析也仅限于美国历史上的政治演讲，限制了研究结论在其他语境或当代在线平台上的普适性。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper conducts the first large-scale computational study of dogwhistles, coded expressions that convey hidden, often hateful meanings to in-groups while appearing innocuous to the general public. It aims to develop resources and methods to systematically identify, analyze, and detect such language, which evades both political accountability and algorithmic content moderation, thereby addressing a significant online risk.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文首次对狗哨语（一种向特定群体传达隐含、通常带有仇恨含义，而对大众显得无害的编码表达）进行了大规模计算研究。其目标是开发资源和方法，以系统性地识别、分析和检测此类语言。这类语言既能规避政治问责，又能逃避算法内容审核，从而应对一个重大的在线风险。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper presents the first large-scale computational study of dogwhistles, introducing a typology and a curated glossary of over 300 dogwhistles with contextual examples. It pioneers the evaluation of a large language model \(GPT-3\) for identifying dogwhistles and their meanings, revealing variable performance across different types. Furthermore, it demonstrates that content containing dogwhistles can evade standard toxicity detection, highlighting a significant risk for online content moderation systems.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文首次对狗哨进行了大规模计算研究，提出了一个分类法并整理了一个包含300多个狗哨及其上下文示例的词表。它开创性地评估了大语言模型（GPT-3）在识别狗哨及其含义方面的能力，揭示了其在不同类型狗哨上表现不一。此外，研究证明含有狗哨的内容可以规避标准的毒性检测，凸显了在线内容审核系统面临的一个重大风险。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The study develops a typology of dogwhistles and curates a glossary of over 300 dogwhistles with contextual examples. It then analyzes their usage in historical U.S. political speeches and evaluates GPT-3's capability to identify dogwhistles and their meanings. Finally, it assesses the evasion of toxicity detection by content containing dogwhistles.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该研究开发了狗哨的类型学，并整理了一个包含300多个狗哨及其语境示例的术语表。随后，它分析了狗哨在美国历史政治演讲中的使用情况，并评估了GPT-3识别狗哨及其含义的能力。最后，它评估了包含狗哨的内容对毒性检测的规避情况。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This paper presents the first large-scale computational study of dogwhistles, developing a typology and a glossary of over 300 coded expressions. It analyzes their use in U.S. political speeches and evaluates GPT-3's ability to identify them, finding performance varies by dogwhistle type and targeted group. The work demonstrates that content containing dogwhistles often evades standard toxicity detection, highlighting online risks and providing resources for future research in NLP and computational social science.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文首次对狗哨语进行了大规模计算研究，建立了其类型学并整理了一个包含300多个编码表达的术语表。研究分析了狗哨语在美国政治演讲中的使用，并评估了GPT-3识别它们的能力，发现其性能因狗哨语类型和目标群体而异。研究表明，包含狗哨语的内容常能规避标准的毒性检测，揭示了其在线风险，并为自然语言处理和计算社会科学领域的未来研究提供了资源。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The study's primary limitation is its reliance on GPT-3 for evaluation, whose performance in identifying dogwhistles varies significantly across different types and targeted groups, indicating a lack of robustness. Furthermore, the curated glossary, while extensive, may not be exhaustive and requires continuous updates to capture evolving coded language. The analysis is also confined to historical U.S. political speeches, limiting the generalizability of findings to other contexts or contemporary online platforms.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该研究的主要局限在于其评估依赖于GPT-3，而该模型在识别不同类别和针对不同群体的狗哨语时表现差异显著，表明其鲁棒性不足。此外，所编纂的词汇表虽然广泛，但可能并不详尽，需要持续更新以捕捉不断演变的编码语言。分析也仅限于美国历史上的政治演讲，限制了研究结论在其他语境或当代在线平台上的普适性。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">【隐式】</div></details></div></div>|

Here, we introduce a new approach to identifying inappropriate communication by explicitly modeling the social relationship between the individuals. We introd"<br>}</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Findings%202024-blue)]()<br>[Paying attention to deflections: Mining pragmatic nuances for whataboutism detection in online di...](https://aclanthology.org/2024.findings-acl.750/) <br> Khiem Phi, Noushin Salek Faramarzi, Chenlu Wang, Ritwik Banerjee <br> 2024-08||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Discourse and Pragmatic Analysis"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Paying attention to deflections: Mining pragmatic nuances for whataboutism detection in online discourse",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-08"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Whataboutism, a potent tool for disrupting narratives and sowing distrust, remains under-explored in quantitative NLP research. Moreover, past work has not distinguished its use as a strategy for misinformation and propaganda from its use as a tool for pragmatic and semantic framing. We introduce new datasets from Twitter/X and YouTube, revealing overlaps as well as distinctions between whataboutism, propaganda, and the tu quoque fallacy. Furthermore, drawing on recent work in linguistic semanti"<br>}</div></details></div>|

##### User Stance Detection (19 papers)
