| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Proceedings%20of%20the%2033rd%20ACM%20International%20Conference%20on%20Multimedia-blue)]()<br>[From individuals to crowds: Dual-level public response prediction in social media](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3746027.3754828) <br> Jinghui Zhang, Kaiyang Wan, Longwei Xu, Ao Li, Zongfang Liu, Xiuying Chen <br> 2025-10-27 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Empirical-and-Statistical-Model-Based Social Simulation](#Empirical-and-Statistical-Model-Based-Social-Simulation-10-papers), [Macro Social Simulation](#Macro-Social-Simulation-7-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers)]</span>|TLDR: Experimental results on SentiWeibo and related LaMP benchmark demonstrate that SocialAlign surpasses strong baselines, showing improved accuracy, interpretability, and generalization in public response prediction.|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/9e6ef8d8/SocialAlign.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to address the limitations of existing public response prediction methods, which lack micro-level personalization and macro-level sentiment distribution analysis. It proposes a unified framework to predict responses at both individual and group levels for better crisis management and social trend analysis.  \[翻译\] 本文旨在解决现有公众反应预测方法的局限性，这些方法缺乏微观层面的个性化分析和宏观层面的情感分布建模。它提出了一个统一的框架，旨在同时预测个体和群体层面的反应，以提升危机管理和社会趋势分析的效果。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] SocialAlign introduces a unified framework for dual-level public response prediction, addressing the limitations of existing methods by simultaneously generating personalized user comments at the micro level and modeling group sentiment distributions at the macro level. Its key innovations include a SocialLLM with a Personalized Analyze-Compose LoRA \(PAC-LoRA\) structure for tailored content generation and the introduction of the SentiWeibo dataset for real-world evaluation. \[翻译\] SocialAlign提出了一个用于双层级公众反应预测的统一框架，通过同时在微观层面生成个性化用户评论和在宏观层面建模群体情感分布，解决了现有方法的局限性。其核心创新包括用于定制化内容生成的、带有个性化分析-组合LoRA（PAC-LoRA）结构的SocialLLM，以及为现实世界评估而引入的SentiWeibo数据集。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] SocialAlign employs a dual-level framework: at the micro level, it uses SocialLLM with a Personalized Analyze-Compose LoRA \(PAC-LoRA\) structure to generate personalized user comments and sentiments; at the macro level, it models group sentiment distributions and aligns them with real-world social media trends. \[翻译\] SocialAlign采用了一个双层框架：在微观层面，它使用带有个性化分析-组合LoRA（PAC-LoRA）结构的SocialLLM来生成个性化的用户评论和情感；在宏观层面，它建模群体情感分布并将其与现实世界的社交媒体趋势对齐。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] SocialAlign introduces a unified framework for dual-level public response prediction, addressing micro-level personalization and macro-level sentiment distribution. It employs a specialized SocialLLM with PAC-LoRA for personalized comment generation and models group sentiment trends. Evaluated on the new SentiWeibo dataset and LaMP benchmark, it demonstrates superior accuracy, interpretability, and generalization.  \[翻译\] SocialAlign提出了一个用于双层级公众反应预测的统一框架，解决了微观个性化与宏观情感分布的难题。它采用带有PAC-LoRA的专用SocialLLM生成个性化评论，并建模群体情感趋势。在新构建的SentiWeibo数据集和LaMP基准上的评估表明，其在准确性、可解释性和泛化性方面表现优异。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] SocialAlign&#x27;s reliance on a single social media platform \(Weibo\) for its primary dataset may limit its generalizability to other platforms with different user demographics and interaction norms. Its macro-level modeling, while innovative, focuses on sentiment distribution and may not fully capture other complex group dynamics like information diffusion or community formation. \[翻译\] SocialAlign 对其主要数据集（微博）的依赖可能限制其推广到其他具有不同用户群体和互动规范平台的能力。其宏观层面的建模虽然创新，但侧重于情感分布，可能无法完全捕捉信息扩散或社区形成等其他复杂的群体动态。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to address the limitations of existing public response prediction methods, which lack micro-level personalization and macro-level sentiment distribution analysis. It proposes a unified framework to predict responses at both individual and group levels for better crisis management and social trend analysis.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文旨在解决现有公众反应预测方法的局限性，这些方法缺乏微观层面的个性化分析和宏观层面的情感分布建模。它提出了一个统一的框架，旨在同时预测个体和群体层面的反应，以提升危机管理和社会趋势分析的效果。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] SocialAlign introduces a unified framework for dual-level public response prediction, addressing the limitations of existing methods by simultaneously generating personalized user comments at the micro level and modeling group sentiment distributions at the macro level. Its key innovations include a SocialLLM with a Personalized Analyze-Compose LoRA \(PAC-LoRA\) structure for tailored content generation and the introduction of the SentiWeibo dataset for real-world evaluation. \[翻译\] SocialAlign提出了一个用于双层级公众反应预测的统一框架，通过同时在微观层面生成个性化用户评论和在宏观层面建模群体情感分布，解决了现有方法的局限性。其核心创新包括用于定制化内容生成的、带有个性化分析-组合LoRA（PAC-LoRA）结构的SocialLLM，以及为现实世界评估而引入的SentiWeibo数据集。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] SocialAlign employs a dual-level framework: at the micro level, it uses SocialLLM with a Personalized Analyze-Compose LoRA \(PAC-LoRA\) structure to generate personalized user comments and sentiments; at the macro level, it models group sentiment distributions and aligns them with real-world social media trends. \[翻译\] SocialAlign采用了一个双层框架：在微观层面，它使用带有个性化分析-组合LoRA（PAC-LoRA）结构的SocialLLM来生成个性化的用户评论和情感；在宏观层面，它建模群体情感分布并将其与现实世界的社交媒体趋势对齐。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] SocialAlign introduces a unified framework for dual-level public response prediction, addressing micro-level personalization and macro-level sentiment distribution. It employs a specialized SocialLLM with PAC-LoRA for personalized comment generation and models group sentiment trends. Evaluated on the new SentiWeibo dataset and LaMP benchmark, it demonstrates superior accuracy, interpretability, and generalization.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>SocialAlign提出了一个用于双层级公众反应预测的统一框架，解决了微观个性化与宏观情感分布的难题。它采用带有PAC-LoRA的专用SocialLLM生成个性化评论，并建模群体情感趋势。在新构建的SentiWeibo数据集和LaMP基准上的评估表明，其在准确性、可解释性和泛化性方面表现优异。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] SocialAlign's reliance on a single social media platform \(Weibo\) for its primary dataset may limit its generalizability to other platforms with different user demographics and interaction norms. Its macro-level modeling, while innovative, focuses on sentiment distribution and may not fully capture other complex group dynamics like information diffusion or community formation. \[翻译\] SocialAlign 对其主要数据集（微博）的依赖可能限制其推广到其他具有不同用户群体和互动规范平台的能力。其宏观层面的建模虽然创新，但侧重于情感分布，可能无法完全捕捉信息扩散或社区形成等其他复杂的群体动态。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">底层匹配以实现宏观现实对齐</div></details></div></div>|
|[OASIS: Open agent social interaction simulations with one million agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2411.11581) <br> Ziyi Yang, Zaibin Zhang, Zirui Zheng, Yuxian Jiang, Ziyue Gan, Zhiyu Wang, Zijian Ling, Jinsong Chen, Martz Ma, Bowen Dong, Prateek Gupta, ... <br> 2025-03-23 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Group-Oriented Social Simulation](#Group-Oriented-Social-Simulation-8-papers), [Macro Social Simulation](#Macro-Social-Simulation-7-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers)]</span>|TLDR: OASIS is a generalizable and scalable social media simulator that replicates various social phenomena, including information spreading, group polarization, and herd effects across X and Reddit platforms, and demonstrates OASIS's potential as a powerful tool for studying complex systems in digital environments.|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/3ebf35ac/OASIS.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] OASIS addresses the limitations of existing LLM-based agent-based models \(ABMs\) for social media simulation, which are often scenario-specific and limited in scale. It aims to provide a generalizable and scalable simulator that mirrors real-world platforms, enabling the study of large-scale social phenomena with up to one million agents.  \[翻译\] OASIS针对现有基于大语言模型（LLM）的社交媒体智能体模拟器（ABMs）的局限性而提出，这些模拟器通常针对特定场景且规模有限。其目标是提供一个通用且可扩展的模拟器，能够模拟真实社交平台，以支持对多达百万级智能体的大规模社会现象进行研究。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] OASIS introduces a generalizable and scalable agent-based social media simulator, designed to overcome the limitations of prior scenario-specific and small-scale LLM-ABMs. Its key innovations include a platform-inspired architecture with dynamic environments and diverse actions, and the capability to simulate up to one million agents, enabling the study of large-scale social phenomena like information spreading and group polarization.  \[翻译\] OASIS 提出了一种通用且可扩展的基于智能体的社交媒体模拟器，旨在克服先前特定场景、小规模LLM-ABM的局限。其核心创新在于一个受真实平台启发的架构，包含动态环境和多样化行动空间，以及模拟多达一百万智能体的能力，从而支持对信息传播、群体极化等大规模社会现象的研究。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] OASIS is a generalizable and scalable agent-based social media simulator. It incorporates dynamically updated environments \(social networks and posts\), diverse action spaces \(e.g., following, commenting\), and recommendation systems \(interest-based and hot-score-based\). Its architecture supports large-scale simulations of up to one million LLM-powered agents, enabling the study of phenomena like information spreading and group polarization across platforms like X and Reddit.  \[翻译\] OASIS 是一个通用且可扩展的基于代理的社交媒体模拟器。它包含动态更新的环境（社交网络和帖子）、多样化的行动空间（例如关注、评论）以及推荐系统（基于兴趣和热度评分）。其架构支持多达一百万个由大语言模型驱动的代理进行大规模模拟，从而能够在 X 和 Reddit 等平台上研究信息传播和群体极化等现象。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] OASIS demonstrates that a generalizable and scalable LLM-based agent framework can effectively simulate large-scale social media dynamics, replicating phenomena like information spreading and group polarization across platforms. Its ability to model up to one million agents reveals that larger agent populations enhance group dynamics and opinion diversity. \[翻译\] OASIS 证明了一个通用、可扩展的基于大语言模型的智能体框架能够有效模拟大规模社交媒体动态，并在不同平台上复现信息传播、群体极化等现象。其模拟百万级用户的能力表明，更大的智能体规模会增强群体动态和观点多样性。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] OASIS demonstrates strong generalizability and scalability, but its reliance on LLM agents introduces computational costs and potential biases from the underlying language models. The simulation&#x27;s realism, while improved, may still diverge from the nuanced, long-term dynamics of real-world social platforms. \[翻译\] OASIS展现了强大的通用性和可扩展性，但其对LLM智能体的依赖带来了较高的计算成本，且底层语言模型可能引入潜在偏见。该模拟的真实性虽有所提升，但仍可能与现实社交平台长期、细微的动态演化存在差距。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] OASIS addresses the limitations of existing LLM-based agent-based models \(ABMs\) for social media simulation, which are often scenario-specific and limited in scale. It aims to provide a generalizable and scalable simulator that mirrors real-world platforms, enabling the study of large-scale social phenomena with up to one million agents.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>OASIS针对现有基于大语言模型（LLM）的社交媒体智能体模拟器（ABMs）的局限性而提出，这些模拟器通常针对特定场景且规模有限。其目标是提供一个通用且可扩展的模拟器，能够模拟真实社交平台，以支持对多达百万级智能体的大规模社会现象进行研究。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] OASIS introduces a generalizable and scalable agent-based social media simulator, designed to overcome the limitations of prior scenario-specific and small-scale LLM-ABMs. Its key innovations include a platform-inspired architecture with dynamic environments and diverse actions, and the capability to simulate up to one million agents, enabling the study of large-scale social phenomena like information spreading and group polarization.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>OASIS 提出了一种通用且可扩展的基于智能体的社交媒体模拟器，旨在克服先前特定场景、小规模LLM-ABM的局限。其核心创新在于一个受真实平台启发的架构，包含动态环境和多样化行动空间，以及模拟多达一百万智能体的能力，从而支持对信息传播、群体极化等大规模社会现象的研究。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] OASIS is a generalizable and scalable agent-based social media simulator. It incorporates dynamically updated environments \(social networks and posts\), diverse action spaces \(e.g., following, commenting\), and recommendation systems \(interest-based and hot-score-based\). Its architecture supports large-scale simulations of up to one million LLM-powered agents, enabling the study of phenomena like information spreading and group polarization across platforms like X and Reddit.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>OASIS 是一个通用且可扩展的基于代理的社交媒体模拟器。它包含动态更新的环境（社交网络和帖子）、多样化的行动空间（例如关注、评论）以及推荐系统（基于兴趣和热度评分）。其架构支持多达一百万个由大语言模型驱动的代理进行大规模模拟，从而能够在 X 和 Reddit 等平台上研究信息传播和群体极化等现象。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] OASIS demonstrates that a generalizable and scalable LLM-based agent framework can effectively simulate large-scale social media dynamics, replicating phenomena like information spreading and group polarization across platforms. Its ability to model up to one million agents reveals that larger agent populations enhance group dynamics and opinion diversity. \[翻译\] OASIS 证明了一个通用、可扩展的基于大语言模型的智能体框架能够有效模拟大规模社交媒体动态，并在不同平台上复现信息传播、群体极化等现象。其模拟百万级用户的能力表明，更大的智能体规模会增强群体动态和观点多样性。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] OASIS demonstrates strong generalizability and scalability, but its reliance on LLM agents introduces computational costs and potential biases from the underlying language models. The simulation's realism, while improved, may still diverge from the nuanced, long-term dynamics of real-world social platforms. \[翻译\] OASIS展现了强大的通用性和可扩展性，但其对LLM智能体的依赖带来了较高的计算成本，且底层语言模型可能引入潜在偏见。该模拟的真实性虽有所提升，但仍可能与现实社交平台长期、细微的动态演化存在差距。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Macro Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Group-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "OASIS: Open agent social interaction simulations with one million agents",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-03-23"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "There has been a growing interest in enhancing rule-based agent-based models \(ABMs\) for social media platforms \(i.e., X, Reddit\) with more realistic large language model \(LLM\) agents, thereby allowing for a more nuanced study of complex systems. As a result, several LLM-based ABMs have been proposed in the past year. | [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-The%20Fourteenth%20International%20Conference%20on%20Learning%20Representations-blue)]()<br>[Social agents: Collective intelligence improves LLM predictions](https://openreview.net/forum?id=73J3hsato3) <br> Aanisha Bhattacharyya, Abhilekh Borah, Yaman Kumar Singla, Rajiv Ratn Shah, Changyou Chen, Balaji Krishnamurthy <br> 2025-10-08 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Social Agents: Simulating a diverse synthetic society to aggregate opinions, improving LLM predictions via collective intelligence. \[翻译\] 社会智能体：通过模拟多样化的合成社会来聚合观点，借助集体智慧提升大语言模型的预测能力。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/7d44cfb3/COLLECTIVE INTELLIGENCE.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to improve the accuracy and realism of LLM-based behavioral predictions by simulating a diverse crowd of synthetic personas, moving beyond single, definitive LLM outputs to better capture the heterogeneity of human judgment. \[翻译\] 该论文旨在通过模拟一个多样化的合成人物群体，超越单一、确定的LLM输出，以更好地捕捉人类判断的异质性，从而提高基于LLM的行为预测的准确性和真实性。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of diverse human-like personas to improve LLM predictions. By aggregating independent judgments from these agents, it applies the &quot;Wisdom of Crowds&quot; principle to behavioral forecasting, outperforming single-LLM baselines across multiple tasks and achieving higher alignment with human judgments.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了Social Agents，一个多智能体框架，通过模拟一个由多样化类人角色组成的合成社会来改进LLM预测。通过聚合这些智能体的独立判断，它将“群体智慧”原则应用于行为预测，在多项任务上超越了单LLM基线，并实现了与人类判断更高的对齐性。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of human-like personas with diverse demographic and psychographic attributes. Each persona independently evaluates a stimulus \(e.g., an advertisement, video, webpage\) and provides a quantitative score and qualitative rationale. The final prediction is obtained by aggregating the individual opinions from this simulated crowd, leveraging the &quot;Wisdom of Crowds&quot; principle to improve accuracy and better mirror real human preference distributions.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了Social Agents，一个多智能体框架，用于模拟一个由具有多样化人口统计学和心理统计学特征的人类化角色组成的合成社会。每个角色独立评估一个刺激物（如广告、视频、网页），并提供量化评分和定性理由。最终预测通过聚合这个模拟群体的个体意见得出，利用“群体智慧”原则来提高准确性，并更好地反映真实的人类偏好分布。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of diverse personas to improve LLM predictions. By aggregating independent judgments from these agents, it achieves significant performance gains across various behavioral prediction tasks, demonstrating that computational crowd simulation can effectively mirror human collective intelligence.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了Social Agents，一个模拟多样化角色合成社会的多智能体框架，旨在提升大语言模型的预测能力。通过聚合这些智能体的独立判断，该框架在多种行为预测任务上取得了显著的性能提升，证明了计算群体模拟能够有效反映人类集体智慧。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The framework&#x27;s reliance on LLMs inherits their inherent biases, which may skew the simulated crowd&#x27;s demographic and psychographic diversity. Its computational cost scales with the number of agents, and the synthetic personas&#x27; behaviors, while correlated with human judgments, are not fully validated against real-world, long-term social dynamics.  \[翻译\] 该框架对LLM的依赖继承了其固有偏见，可能扭曲模拟人群的人口统计学和心理特征多样性。其计算成本随智能体数量增加而增加，且合成角色的行为虽与人类判断相关，但未在真实世界的长期社会动态中得到充分验证。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to improve the accuracy and realism of LLM-based behavioral predictions by simulating a diverse crowd of synthetic personas, moving beyond single, definitive LLM outputs to better capture the heterogeneity of human judgment. \[翻译\] 该论文旨在通过模拟一个多样化的合成人物群体，超越单一、确定的LLM输出，以更好地捕捉人类判断的异质性，从而提高基于LLM的行为预测的准确性和真实性。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of diverse human-like personas to improve LLM predictions. By aggregating independent judgments from these agents, it applies the "Wisdom of Crowds" principle to behavioral forecasting, outperforming single-LLM baselines across multiple tasks and achieving higher alignment with human judgments.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了Social Agents，一个多智能体框架，通过模拟一个由多样化类人角色组成的合成社会来改进LLM预测。通过聚合这些智能体的独立判断，它将“群体智慧”原则应用于行为预测，在多项任务上超越了单LLM基线，并实现了与人类判断更高的对齐性。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of human-like personas with diverse demographic and psychographic attributes. Each persona independently evaluates a stimulus \(e.g., an advertisement, video, webpage\) and provides a quantitative score and qualitative rationale. The final prediction is obtained by aggregating the individual opinions from this simulated crowd, leveraging the "Wisdom of Crowds" principle to improve accuracy and better mirror real human preference distributions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了Social Agents，一个多智能体框架，用于模拟一个由具有多样化人口统计学和心理统计学特征的人类化角色组成的合成社会。每个角色独立评估一个刺激物（如广告、视频、网页），并提供量化评分和定性理由。最终预测通过聚合这个模拟群体的个体意见得出，利用“群体智慧”原则来提高准确性，并更好地反映真实的人类偏好分布。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of diverse personas to improve LLM predictions. By aggregating independent judgments from these agents, it achieves significant performance gains across various behavioral prediction tasks, demonstrating that computational crowd simulation can effectively mirror human collective intelligence.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了Social Agents，一个模拟多样化角色合成社会的多智能体框架，旨在提升大语言模型的预测能力。通过聚合这些智能体的独立判断，该框架在多种行为预测任务上取得了显著的性能提升，证明了计算群体模拟能够有效反映人类集体智慧。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The framework's reliance on LLMs inherits their inherent biases, which may skew the simulated crowd's demographic and psychographic diversity. Its computational cost scales with the number of agents, and the synthetic personas' behaviors, while correlated with human judgments, are not fully validated against real-world, long-term social dynamics.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该框架对LLM的依赖继承了其固有偏见，可能扭曲模拟人群的人口统计学和心理特征多样性。其计算成本随智能体数量增加而增加，且合成角色的行为虽与人类判断相关，但未在真实世界的长期社会动态中得到充分验证。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">目的是通过agent小团体形式集体决策实现LLM本身预测能力提升，主要是处理社媒相关任务（如CTR）</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Conference%20on%20Robot%20Learning-blue)]()<br>[Social interactions as recursive MDPs](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v164/tejwani22a.html) <br> Ravi Tejwani, Yen-Ling Kuo, Tianmin Shu, Boris Katz, Andrei Barbu <br> 2022-01-11 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Social MDPs: Extending traditional MDPs by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents' goals to model social interactions like helping or hindering. \[翻译\] 社会性MDP：通过纳入对其他智能体目标的递归推理来扩展传统MDP，以建模如帮助或阻碍等社会交互。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/e588ac3d/Social MDP-[1].png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to formalize social reasoning within the framework of Markov decision processes \(MDPs\) by introducing recursive reasoning about other agents&#x27; goals. It seeks to bridge the gap between well-defined physical tasks and the pre-theoretic nature of social interactions, providing a mathematical model to specify social behaviors like helping or hindering others through reward functions that incorporate estimates of other agents&#x27; goals. This enables zero-shot social inferences and offers a foundation for developing socially aware AI systems.  \[翻译\] 本文旨在通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理，将社会性推理形式化地纳入马尔可夫决策过程框架。它试图弥合明确定义的物理任务与社会互动的前理论性质之间的差距，提供一个数学模型，通过融入对其他智能体目标估计的奖励函数来指定帮助或阻碍他人等社会行为。这使得零样本社会推理成为可能，并为开发具有社会意识的AI系统奠定了基础。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social MDPs, a novel framework that extends traditional Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents&#x27; goals. Its core innovation lies in defining a reward function that combines an agent&#x27;s physical goals with social goals, which are functions of other agents&#x27; estimated reward structures. This allows for the explicit modeling of complex social interactions—such as helping, hindering, or polite/aggressive variants thereof—within a precise mathematical model, enabling zero-shot social inferences and providing a foundation for socially aware AI agents.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了Social MDPs，这是一个新颖的框架，它通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理，扩展了传统的马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）。其核心创新在于定义了一个奖励函数，该函数将智能体的物理目标与社会目标相结合，而社会目标是其他智能体估计的奖励结构的函数。这使得能够在精确的数学模型内显式地建模复杂的社会互动（如帮助、阻碍或其礼貌 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes Social MDPs, a framework that extends Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents&#x27; goals. The core method involves augmenting the traditional reward function to include both physical goals \(related to the configuration space\) and social goals \(defined relative to the estimated reward functions of other agents\). This allows an agent to model interactions such as helping or hindering others by maximizing or minimizing their estimated rewards, while balancing these social objectives with its own physical goals. The formulation supports arbitrary functions over other agents&#x27; reward structures, enabling complex social behaviors.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了Social MDPs框架，通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理来扩展马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）。其核心方法在于扩展传统的奖励函数，使其同时包含物理目标（与配置空间相关）和社会目标（相对于其他智能体的估计奖励函数来定义）。这使得智能体能够通过最大化或最小化其他智能体的估计奖励来建模诸如帮助或 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes Social MDPs, a novel framework that extends traditional Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents&#x27; goals. This allows agents to balance physical objectives with social goals, such as helping or hindering others, enabling zero-shot social inferences and providing a mathematical model for studying social interactions.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了社会性马尔可夫决策过程（Social MDPs），这是一个新颖的框架，通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理，扩展了传统的马尔可夫决策过程。这使得智能体能够平衡物理目标与社会性目标（如帮助或阻碍他人），实现了零样本社会推理，并为研究社会互动提供了一个数学模型。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The paper does not explicitly list limitations. Based on the abstract, a key limitation is the unknown scope of the proposed Social MDPs and their potential POMDP variants in accounting for all possible social interactions. The framework&#x27;s effectiveness for highly complex, multi-agent, or partially observable social scenarios remains to be fully explored. \[翻译\] 该论文未明确列出局限性。基于摘要，一个关键局限在于所提出的Social MDPs及其潜在的POMDP变体在多大程度上能涵盖所有可能的社会互动仍是未知的。该框架对于高度复杂、多智能体或部分可观察的社会场景的有效性尚待充分探索。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to formalize social reasoning within the framework of Markov decision processes \(MDPs\) by introducing recursive reasoning about other agents' goals. It seeks to bridge the gap between well-defined physical tasks and the pre-theoretic nature of social interactions, providing a mathematical model to specify social behaviors like helping or hindering others through reward functions that incorporate estimates of other agents' goals. This enables zero-shot social inferences and offers a foundation for developing socially aware AI systems.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文旨在通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理，将社会性推理形式化地纳入马尔可夫决策过程框架。它试图弥合明确定义的物理任务与社会互动的前理论性质之间的差距，提供一个数学模型，通过融入对其他智能体目标估计的奖励函数来指定帮助或阻碍他人等社会行为。这使得零样本社会推理成为可能，并为开发具有社会意识的AI系统奠定了基础。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social MDPs, a novel framework that extends traditional Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents' goals. Its core innovation lies in defining a reward function that combines an agent's physical goals with social goals, which are functions of other agents' estimated reward structures. This allows for the explicit modeling of complex social interactions—such as helping, hindering, or polite/aggressive variants thereof—within a precise mathematical model, enabling zero-shot social inferences and providing a foundation for socially aware AI agents.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了Social MDPs，这是一个新颖的框架，它通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理，扩展了传统的马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）。其核心创新在于定义了一个奖励函数，该函数将智能体的物理目标与社会目标相结合，而社会目标是其他智能体估计的奖励结构的函数。这使得能够在精确的数学模型内显式地建模复杂的社会互动（如帮助、阻碍或其礼貌<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes Social MDPs, a framework that extends Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents' goals. The core method involves augmenting the traditional reward function to include both physical goals \(related to the configuration space\) and social goals \(defined relative to the estimated reward functions of other agents\). This allows an agent to model interactions such as helping or hindering others by maximizing or minimizing their estimated rewards, while balancing these social objectives with its own physical goals. The formulation supports arbitrary functions over other agents' reward structures, enabling complex social behaviors.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了Social MDPs框架，通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理来扩展马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）。其核心方法在于扩展传统的奖励函数，使其同时包含物理目标（与配置空间相关）和社会目标（相对于其他智能体的估计奖励函数来定义）。这使得智能体能够通过最大化或最小化其他智能体的估计奖励来建模诸如帮助或<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes Social MDPs, a novel framework that extends traditional Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents' goals. This allows agents to balance physical objectives with social goals, such as helping or hindering others, enabling zero-shot social inferences and providing a mathematical model for studying social interactions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了社会性马尔可夫决策过程（Social MDPs），这是一个新颖的框架，通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理，扩展了传统的马尔可夫决策过程。这使得智能体能够平衡物理目标与社会性目标（如帮助或阻碍他人），实现了零样本社会推理，并为研究社会互动提供了一个数学模型。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The paper does not explicitly list limitations. Based on the abstract, a key limitation is the unknown scope of the proposed Social MDPs and their potential POMDP variants in accounting for all possible social interactions. The framework's effectiveness for highly complex, multi-agent, or partially observable social scenarios remains to be fully explored. \[翻译\] 该论文未明确列出局限性。基于摘要，一个关键局限在于所提出的Social MDPs及其潜在的POMDP变体在多大程度上能涵盖所有可能的社会互动仍是未知的。该框架对于高度复杂、多智能体或部分可观察的社会场景的有效性尚待充分探索。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Micro Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Individual-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Other Fields"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": "",<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Social interactions as recursive MDPs",<br>    "publication_date": null<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "While machines and robots must interact with humans, providing them with social skills has been a largely overlooked topic. This is mostly a consequence of the fact that tasks such as navigation, command following, and even game playing are well-deﬁned, while social reasoning still mostly remains a pre-theoretic problem. We demonstrate how social interactions can be effectively incorporated into MDPs \(Markov decision processes\) by reasoning recursively about the goals of other agents. In essence"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-2022%20IEEE%20International%20Conference%20on%20Robotics%20and%20Automation%20%28ICRA%29-blue)]()<br>[Incorporating rich social interactions into MDPs](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9811991/) <br> Ravi Tejwani, Yen-Ling Kuo, Tianmin Shu, Bennett Stankovits, Dan Gutfreund, Joshua B. Tenenbaum, Boris Katz, Andrei Barbu <br> 2022-05-23 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Social MDPs: A formal framework for robots to reason about and execute complex social interactions zero-shot, akin to a "social interaction grammar" for machines. \[翻译\] 社会MDP：一种形式化框架，使机器人能够零样本地推理和执行复杂的社会互动，类似于机器的“社会互动语法”。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/6e88c8a1/Social MDP-[2].png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to formalize rich social interactions from microsociology and economics within a Markov Decision Process \(MDP\) framework for robotics. Its core motivation is to enable robots to engage in complex, zero-shot social interactions—such as cooperation, conflict, coercion, competition, and exchange—in novel environments, mirroring human social capabilities. This approach also seeks to provide concrete mathematical definitions for social phenomena and align computational judgments with human perceptions of social situations.  \[翻译\] 该论文旨在将微观社会学和经济学中的丰富社会互动形式化，纳入机器人学的马尔可夫决策过程框架。其核心动机是使机器人能够在新的环境中进行复杂的、零样本的社会互动（如合作、冲突、强制、竞争和交换），以模仿人类的社会能力。该方法还试图为社会现象提供具体的数学定义，并使计算判断与人类对社会情境的感知保持一致。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The work formalizes five fundamental social interactions \(cooperation, conflict, coercion, competition, exchange\) from microsociology and economics into a unified mathematical framework by extending nested Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\). This Social MDP allows agents to reason about each other&#x27;s hidden rewards, enabling zero-shot execution of rich social interactions in novel environments without prior examples. \[翻译\] 该研究将微观社会学和经济学中的五种基本社会互动（合作、冲突、强制、竞争、交换）形式化，通过扩展嵌套马尔可夫决策过程（MDPs）将其纳入统一的数学框架。这种社会MDP使智能体能够推理彼此的隐藏奖励，从而能在没有先验示例的新环境中零样本执行复杂的社会互动。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The method extends a nested Markov Decision Process \(MDP\) framework to formalize social interactions. It enables agents to reason about arbitrary functions of each other&#x27;s hidden rewards, thereby encoding five fundamental social interaction types from microsociology: cooperation, conflict, coercion, competition, and exchange. This Social MDP allows for zero-shot execution of social interactions in novel environments.  \[翻译\] 该方法扩展了嵌套马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）框架以形式化社会交互。它使智能体能够推理彼此隐藏奖励的任意函数，从而编码微观社会学中的五种基本社会交互类型：合作、冲突、强制、竞争和交换。这种社会MDP允许在全新环境中零样本执行社会交互。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This work formalizes core social interactions from microsociology into a Social MDP framework, enabling zero-shot execution of social behaviors by robotic agents and demonstrating alignment with human judgments. \[翻译\] 该研究将微观社会学中的核心社会互动形式化到一个社会MDP框架中，使机器人智能体能够零样本执行社会行为，并证明了其与人类判断的一致性。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed Social MDP framework, while providing a formal mathematical basis for social interactions, relies on reasoning about hidden rewards, which may be computationally intensive and require strong assumptions about agent knowledge. Its zero-shot capability, though promising, has not been empirically validated in complex, large-scale, or long-horizon real-world social scenarios.  \[翻译\] 所提出的社会MDP框架虽然为社会互动提供了形式化的数学基础，但其依赖于对隐藏奖励的推理，这可能带来较高的计算复杂度，且需要对智能体的知识做出较强假设。其零样本能力尽管前景广阔，但尚未在复杂、大规模或长周期的真实世界社会场景中得到实证验证。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to formalize rich social interactions from microsociology and economics within a Markov Decision Process \(MDP\) framework for robotics. Its core motivation is to enable robots to engage in complex, zero-shot social interactions—such as cooperation, conflict, coercion, competition, and exchange—in novel environments, mirroring human social capabilities. This approach also seeks to provide concrete mathematical definitions for social phenomena and align computational judgments with human perceptions of social situations.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文旨在将微观社会学和经济学中的丰富社会互动形式化，纳入机器人学的马尔可夫决策过程框架。其核心动机是使机器人能够在新的环境中进行复杂的、零样本的社会互动（如合作、冲突、强制、竞争和交换），以模仿人类的社会能力。该方法还试图为社会现象提供具体的数学定义，并使计算判断与人类对社会情境的感知保持一致。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The work formalizes five fundamental social interactions \(cooperation, conflict, coercion, competition, exchange\) from microsociology and economics into a unified mathematical framework by extending nested Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\). This Social MDP allows agents to reason about each other's hidden rewards, enabling zero-shot execution of rich social interactions in novel environments without prior examples. \[翻译\] 该研究将微观社会学和经济学中的五种基本社会互动（合作、冲突、强制、竞争、交换）形式化，通过扩展嵌套马尔可夫决策过程（MDPs）将其纳入统一的数学框架。这种社会MDP使智能体能够推理彼此的隐藏奖励，从而能在没有先验示例的新环境中零样本执行复杂的社会互动。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The method extends a nested Markov Decision Process \(MDP\) framework to formalize social interactions. It enables agents to reason about arbitrary functions of each other's hidden rewards, thereby encoding five fundamental social interaction types from microsociology: cooperation, conflict, coercion, competition, and exchange. This Social MDP allows for zero-shot execution of social interactions in novel environments.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该方法扩展了嵌套马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）框架以形式化社会交互。它使智能体能够推理彼此隐藏奖励的任意函数，从而编码微观社会学中的五种基本社会交互类型：合作、冲突、强制、竞争和交换。这种社会MDP允许在全新环境中零样本执行社会交互。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This work formalizes core social interactions from microsociology into a Social MDP framework, enabling zero-shot execution of social behaviors by robotic agents and demonstrating alignment with human judgments. \[翻译\] 该研究将微观社会学中的核心社会互动形式化到一个社会MDP框架中，使机器人智能体能够零样本执行社会行为，并证明了其与人类判断的一致性。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed Social MDP framework, while providing a formal mathematical basis for social interactions, relies on reasoning about hidden rewards, which may be computationally intensive and require strong assumptions about agent knowledge. Its zero-shot capability, though promising, has not been empirically validated in complex, large-scale, or long-horizon real-world social scenarios.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>所提出的社会MDP框架虽然为社会互动提供了形式化的数学基础，但其依赖于对隐藏奖励的推理，这可能带来较高的计算复杂度，且需要对智能体的知识做出较强假设。其零样本能力尽管前景广阔，但尚未在复杂、大规模或长周期的真实世界社会场景中得到实证验证。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">\[social MDP连续工作1\]</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-AAAI-blue)]()<br>[Zero-Shot Linear Combinations of Grounded Social Interactions with Linear Social MDPs](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/25080) <br> Ravi Tejwani, Yen-Ling Kuo, Tianmin Shu, Bennett Stankovits, Dan Gutfreund, Joshua B. Tenenbaum, Boris Katz, Andrei Barbu <br> 2023-06-26 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers)]</span>|TLDR: A new model, the Linear Social MDP, enables zero-shot reasoning about complex social interactions, provides a mathematical basis for the long-standing intuition that social interactions should compose, and leads to interesting new behaviors that are validated using human observers.|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/473fbb19/Social MDP-[3].png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes the Linear Social MDP, a computational model that enables zero-shot reasoning about complex social interactions by dynamically combining basic social interactions based on the estimated goals of other agents. It aims to provide a mathematical foundation for the composition of social behaviors, which is crucial for developing socially intelligent robotic applications.  \[翻译\] 本文提出了线性社会MDP模型，该模型能够根据对其他智能体目标的动态估计，组合基本社会交互，从而实现对复杂社会交互的零样本推理。其研究目标是为社会行为的组合提供数学基础，这对于开发具有社会智能的机器人应用至关重要。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Linear Social MDPs, a novel computational framework that enables zero-shot composition of basic social interactions through linear combinations, grounded in the estimated goals of other agents. This provides a mathematical foundation for the intuitive theory that complex social behaviors emerge from simpler components. \[翻译\] 该论文提出了线性社会MDPs，这是一个新颖的计算框架，它通过对基本社会交互进行线性组合，并基于对其他智能体目标的实时估计，实现了复杂社会交互的零样本组合。这为“复杂社会行为由简单组件涌现”的直觉理论提供了数学基础。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes the Linear Social MDP, which extends the Social MDP framework. Its core method involves computing linear combinations of basic social interactions. The weights for these combinations are dynamically determined based on the agent&#x27;s real-time estimation of other agents&#x27; goals, enabling zero-shot reasoning about complex, composite social behaviors.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了线性社会MDP，它扩展了社会MDP框架。其核心方法在于计算基本社会交互的线性组合。这些组合的权重根据智能体对其他智能体目标的实时估计动态确定，从而实现了对复杂、复合社会行为的零样本推理。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes Linear Social MDPs, a computational model enabling zero-shot composition of basic social interactions. It provides a mathematical framework for complex social reasoning in agents, validated by human evaluation. \[翻译\] 该论文提出了线性社会MDP模型，实现了基础社会交互的零样本组合。它为智能体进行复杂社会推理提供了数学框架，并通过人类评估验证了其有效性。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The model&#x27;s theoretical contribution is significant, but its practical validation remains limited to controlled simulations and human observer studies, lacking real-world deployment or large-scale empirical testing. \[翻译\] 该模型的理论贡献显著，但其实际验证仍局限于受控模拟和人类观察者研究，缺乏真实世界部署或大规模实证测试。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes the Linear Social MDP, a computational model that enables zero-shot reasoning about complex social interactions by dynamically combining basic social interactions based on the estimated goals of other agents. It aims to provide a mathematical foundation for the composition of social behaviors, which is crucial for developing socially intelligent robotic applications.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文提出了线性社会MDP模型，该模型能够根据对其他智能体目标的动态估计，组合基本社会交互，从而实现对复杂社会交互的零样本推理。其研究目标是为社会行为的组合提供数学基础，这对于开发具有社会智能的机器人应用至关重要。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Linear Social MDPs, a novel computational framework that enables zero-shot composition of basic social interactions through linear combinations, grounded in the estimated goals of other agents. This provides a mathematical foundation for the intuitive theory that complex social behaviors emerge from simpler components. \[翻译\] 该论文提出了线性社会MDPs，这是一个新颖的计算框架，它通过对基本社会交互进行线性组合，并基于对其他智能体目标的实时估计，实现了复杂社会交互的零样本组合。这为“复杂社会行为由简单组件涌现”的直觉理论提供了数学基础。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes the Linear Social MDP, which extends the Social MDP framework. Its core method involves computing linear combinations of basic social interactions. The weights for these combinations are dynamically determined based on the agent's real-time estimation of other agents' goals, enabling zero-shot reasoning about complex, composite social behaviors.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了线性社会MDP，它扩展了社会MDP框架。其核心方法在于计算基本社会交互的线性组合。这些组合的权重根据智能体对其他智能体目标的实时估计动态确定，从而实现了对复杂、复合社会行为的零样本推理。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes Linear Social MDPs, a computational model enabling zero-shot composition of basic social interactions. It provides a mathematical framework for complex social reasoning in agents, validated by human evaluation. \[翻译\] 该论文提出了线性社会MDP模型，实现了基础社会交互的零样本组合。它为智能体进行复杂社会推理提供了数学框架，并通过人类评估验证了其有效性。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The model's theoretical contribution is significant, but its practical validation remains limited to controlled simulations and human observer studies, lacking real-world deployment or large-scale empirical testing. \[翻译\] 该模型的理论贡献显著，但其实际验证仍局限于受控模拟和人类观察者研究，缺乏真实世界部署或大规模实证测试。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">\[social MDP连续工作3\]</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-EMNLP%202025-blue)]()<br>[A symbolic adversarial learning framework for evolving fake news generation and detection](https://aclanthology.org/2025.emnlp-main.619/) <br> Chong Tian, Qirong Ho, Xiuying Chen <br> 2025-11 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers), [Misinformation Analysis](#Misinformation-Analysis-9-papers), [Misinformation Generation](#Misinformation-Generation-2-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] SALF simulates an adversarial arms race between fake news generators and detectors through symbolic agent learning, evolving both sides without neural updates. \[翻译\] SALF 通过符号化智能体学习模拟假新闻生成器与检测器之间的对抗性军备竞赛，无需神经更新即可实现双方共同进化。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/0769fb9d/SALF.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The rapid advancement of LLMs has significantly increased the risk of sophisticated fake news generation, posing a major challenge to existing detection methods that struggle with its dynamically evolving nature. This work proposes a novel adversarial learning framework to address this challenge by simultaneously improving fake news generation and detection through iterative symbolic optimization.  \[翻译\] 大型语言模型（LLM）的快速发展显著增加了生成复杂虚假新闻的风险，这对现有检测方法构成了重大挑战，这些方法难以应对其动态演变的特性。本研究提出了一种新颖的对抗学习框架，通过迭代的符号化优化过程，同时改进虚假新闻的生成与检测，以应对这一挑战。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces a Symbolic Adversarial Learning Framework \(SALF\) that innovates by using agent symbolic learning to simulate adversarial training for fake news generation and detection. It replaces traditional neural network weight updates with operations on natural language representations of prompts, gradients, and loss, enabling iterative refinement through structured debates between generation and detection agents.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出的符号对抗学习框架（SALF）的创新之处在于，它利用智能体符号学习来模拟假新闻生成与检测的对抗训练。该框架用对提示词、梯度和损失的自然语言表示进行操作，取代了传统的神经网络权重更新，使得生成智能体与检测智能体能够通过结构化辩论进行迭代优化。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The Symbolic Adversarial Learning Framework \(SALF\) employs an agent-based adversarial training paradigm. It uses a generation agent to craft deceptive narratives and a detection agent that employs structured debates to identify logical and factual flaws. The agents are represented via agent symbolic learning, where learnable weights are defined by prompts, and optimization is simulated by operating on natural language representations of weights, loss, and gradients, rather than traditional numerical updates.  \[翻译\] 符号对抗学习框架（SALF）采用了一种基于智能体的对抗训练范式。它使用一个生成智能体来制作欺骗性叙事，以及一个检测智能体，该智能体通过结构化辩论来识别逻辑和事实缺陷。智能体通过智能体符号学习来表示，其中可学习的权重由提示定义，优化过程通过对权重、损失和梯度的自然语言表示进行操作来模拟，而非传统的数值更新。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The proposed Symbolic Adversarial Learning Framework \(SALF\) introduces a novel agent-based adversarial training paradigm for fake news generation and detection. It replaces traditional neural updates with agent symbolic learning, where prompts define learnable weights, and operations on natural language representations simulate back-propagation. Experiments show SALF effectively generates sophisticated fake news that degrades state-of-the-art detectors and can also refine them through adversarial interactions.  \[翻译\] 所提出的符号对抗学习框架（SALF）引入了一种新颖的、基于智能体的对抗训练范式，用于虚假新闻的生成与检测。它用智能体符号学习替代了传统的神经更新，其中可学习的权重由提示词定义，并通过自然语言表征上的操作来模拟反向传播。实验表明，SALF能有效生成复杂的虚假新闻以降低最先进检测器的性能，并能通过对抗性交互来优化这些检测器。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed symbolic adversarial learning framework relies on agent prompts to define learnable weights, which may limit its scalability and adaptability to more complex, multi-turn adversarial scenarios beyond the structured debate format. Its effectiveness is demonstrated primarily on multilingual fake news benchmarks, leaving its generalizability to other evolving misinformation domains \(e.g., multimodal or highly contextual\) unverified.  \[翻译\] 所提出的符号对抗学习框架依赖智能体提示来定义可学习权重，这可能限制了其在结构化辩论格式之外、更复杂的多轮对抗场景中的可扩展性和适应性。其有效性主要在多语言假新闻基准数据集上得到验证，其在其他动态演化的虚假信息领域（如多模态或高语境领域）的泛化能力尚未得到检验。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The rapid advancement of LLMs has significantly increased the risk of sophisticated fake news generation, posing a major challenge to existing detection methods that struggle with its dynamically evolving nature. This work proposes a novel adversarial learning framework to address this challenge by simultaneously improving fake news generation and detection through iterative symbolic optimization.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>大型语言模型（LLM）的快速发展显著增加了生成复杂虚假新闻的风险，这对现有检测方法构成了重大挑战，这些方法难以应对其动态演变的特性。本研究提出了一种新颖的对抗学习框架，通过迭代的符号化优化过程，同时改进虚假新闻的生成与检测，以应对这一挑战。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces a Symbolic Adversarial Learning Framework \(SALF\) that innovates by using agent symbolic learning to simulate adversarial training for fake news generation and detection. It replaces traditional neural network weight updates with operations on natural language representations of prompts, gradients, and loss, enabling iterative refinement through structured debates between generation and detection agents.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出的符号对抗学习框架（SALF）的创新之处在于，它利用智能体符号学习来模拟假新闻生成与检测的对抗训练。该框架用对提示词、梯度和损失的自然语言表示进行操作，取代了传统的神经网络权重更新，使得生成智能体与检测智能体能够通过结构化辩论进行迭代优化。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The Symbolic Adversarial Learning Framework \(SALF\) employs an agent-based adversarial training paradigm. It uses a generation agent to craft deceptive narratives and a detection agent that employs structured debates to identify logical and factual flaws. The agents are represented via agent symbolic learning, where learnable weights are defined by prompts, and optimization is simulated by operating on natural language representations of weights, loss, and gradients, rather than traditional numerical updates.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>符号对抗学习框架（SALF）采用了一种基于智能体的对抗训练范式。它使用一个生成智能体来制作欺骗性叙事，以及一个检测智能体，该智能体通过结构化辩论来识别逻辑和事实缺陷。智能体通过智能体符号学习来表示，其中可学习的权重由提示定义，优化过程通过对权重、损失和梯度的自然语言表示进行操作来模拟，而非传统的数值更新。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The proposed Symbolic Adversarial Learning Framework \(SALF\) introduces a novel agent-based adversarial training paradigm for fake news generation and detection. It replaces traditional neural updates with agent symbolic learning, where prompts define learnable weights, and operations on natural language representations simulate back-propagation. Experiments show SALF effectively generates sophisticated fake news that degrades state-of-the-art detectors and can also refine them through adversarial interactions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>所提出的符号对抗学习框架（SALF）引入了一种新颖的、基于智能体的对抗训练范式，用于虚假新闻的生成与检测。它用智能体符号学习替代了传统的神经更新，其中可学习的权重由提示词定义，并通过自然语言表征上的操作来模拟反向传播。实验表明，SALF能有效生成复杂的虚假新闻以降低最先进检测器的性能，并能通过对抗性交互来优化这些检测器。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed symbolic adversarial learning framework relies on agent prompts to define learnable weights, which may limit its scalability and adaptability to more complex, multi-turn adversarial scenarios beyond the structured debate format. Its effectiveness is demonstrated primarily on multilingual fake news benchmarks, leaving its generalizability to other evolving misinformation domains \(e.g., multimodal or highly contextual\) unverified.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>所提出的符号对抗学习框架依赖智能体提示来定义可学习权重，这可能限制了其在结构化辩论格式之外、更复杂的多轮对抗场景中的可扩展性和适应性。其有效性主要在多语言假新闻基准数据集上得到验证，其在其他动态演化的虚假信息领域（如多模态或高语境领域）的泛化能力尚未得到检验。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">多智能体方式，法庭模式、对抗模式进行假新闻生成与检测<br><br>\[\]【形象比喻：类似我们不断修正提示词以期获得理想输出的方法；借用对抗生成思想在自然语言空间体现该过程】📊A Symbolic Adversarial Learning Framework for  Evolving Fake News Generation and Detection<br>    📊10作者<br>    需要了解的基本信息<br>        会议/期刊：EMNLP<br>        年份：2025<br>        类型：“对抗”生成框架，提示词工程，ABM<br>        领域：虚假内容分析<br>        阅读深入程度：精读<br>        评价：⭐️️️️⭐️️️️⭐️️️️⭐️️️️<br><br>    \[主线概括\]<br>        \[目的\]：为了解决静态检测模型难以适应动态演化的虚假新闻的问题<br>            📊11之前的方法为什么会出现该问题？<br>                模型或策略的参数/知识是静态的，只能学到静态数据集中的能力，而虚假新闻的生成是动态演化的，存在根本矛盾。<br><br><br>        \[方法\]：使用了基于提示词符号优化和多角色辩论机制的对抗学习框架Symbolic Adversarial Learning Framework \(SALF\)<br>            🔒方法具体步骤略<br>            🔒❔方法形象总结<br>                类似我们不断修正提示词以期获得理想输出的方法；使用自定义符号系统，借用对抗生成思想在自然语言空间体现该过程。直到构建的奖励函数收敛或达到最大迭代次数<br><br>            ❔这个方法为什么可以解决问题？<br>                🔍如何使LLM的输出结果足够稳定，标准足够统一？<br>                    和很多其他论文一样，作者难以给出理论分析，只是闷头用良好的实验结果证明：确实够用了！<br><br><br>            ❔有什么值得注意的细节吗？<br>                检测器提示词仅在没有识别出虚假新闻的轮次更新：1.成功识别情况更新方向模糊，意义较小；2.减少资源消耗；3.控制更新幅度，防止变化太大，过拟合<br><br><br>        \[效果\]：达成了协同进化的效果，生成器效果尤其好，直接让基线检测器准确率减半<br>            ❔论文有哪里让人眼前一亮？<br>                基于提示词工程，在自然语言空间中定义符号系统模拟对抗生成<br>                辩论作为检测者，激发了LLM的更为深度的检测能力<br><br>            ❔论文的思路和方法哪里做的不够好？/局限性<br>                【提示词工程的固有局限性】：生成、检测、优化、评估都使用提示词，过度依赖LLM本身的能力，且因LLM本身的概率性，存在输出不稳定问题<br><br>            ❔论文对我的专业知识有什么启发？<br>                prompt作为模型这个大函数的输入，直接影响模型输出，本身就具有研究价值<br>                这个方法利用了LLM的强大能力，整个框架的构建会降低门槛，是一个讨巧的方式<br>                论文方法的【可行性本质】在于：【自然语言】本身是相当完善但存在主观性且含义易混淆的符号系统，它可以显式描述【任何】事物、规律、方法。在符号空间中进行类机器学习，能够白盒可解释地利用这个强大的系统。但这依赖于LLM的足够的自然语言理解和生成能力<br><br>            ❔我可以怎么利用和改进它的思路？<br>                论文说使用符号化系统是因为在大型LLM上做参数微调较为困难，为解决该问题，还有一种常见思路：从教师LLM中将假新闻识别和生成能力蒸馏到两个小LLM上（减少微调成本），使用正常的GAN方法进行微调，这种似乎难以实现辩论机制，因为它是黑箱，没有显式可见的符号逻辑系统</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Proceedings%20of%20the%202025%20Conference%20on%20Empirical%20Methods%20in%20Natural%20Language%20Processing-blue)]()<br>[The stepwise deception: Simulating the evolution from true news to fake news with LLM agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2410.19064) <br> Yuhan Liu, Zirui Song, Juntian Zhang, Xiaoqing Zhang, Xiuying Chen, Rui Yan <br> 2025 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers), [Misinformation Generation](#Misinformation-Generation-2-papers)]</span>|TLDR: A novel Large Language Model-based simulation approach explicitly focusing on fake news evolution from real news, which effectively captures fake news evolution patterns and accurately reproduces known fake news, aligning closely with human evaluations.|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/67ebdd0a/FUSE.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes FUSE, a novel LLM-based agent simulation framework, to address the critical gap in understanding the gradual evolutionary process from true news to fake news within social networks, rather than assuming fake news as a pre-existing entity. \[翻译\] 本文提出FUSE，一个新颖的基于大语言模型的智能体仿真框架，旨在解决理解真实新闻在社交网络中如何逐步演变为虚假新闻这一关键研究空白，而非将虚假新闻视为预先存在的实体。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces FUSE, a novel LLM-based agent simulation framework that explicitly models the stepwise evolution of fake news from true news, rather than assuming its pre-existence. It innovates by designing a social network with four distinct LLM agent types \(spreaders, commentators, verifiers, bystanders\) to simulate realistic daily interactions that progressively distort information. A key contribution is the development of FUSE-EVAL, a comprehensive evaluation framework to quantify truth deviation across multiple linguistic and semantic dimensions.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了FUSE，一个新颖的基于LLM的智能体仿真框架，其创新在于明确模拟了假新闻从真实新闻逐步演变的过程，而非假定其预先存在。它通过设计一个包含四种不同类型LLM智能体（传播者、评论者、验证者、旁观者）的社交网络来模拟导致信息逐步失真的日常互动。一个核心贡献是开发了FUSE-EVAL，一个用于从多维度语言和语义层面 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The FUSE framework employs an LLM-based agent simulation to model the evolution of fake news. It constructs a social network with four distinct LLM agent types \(spreaders, commentators, verifiers, bystanders\) to simulate daily interactions that progressively distort true information. The framework quantifies this distortion using FUSE-EVAL, a multi-dimensional evaluation metric for truth deviation.  \[翻译\] FUSE框架采用基于大语言模型的智能体模拟来建模虚假新闻的演化过程。它构建了一个包含四种不同类型智能体（传播者、评论者、验证者、旁观者）的社交网络，以模拟逐步扭曲真实信息的日常互动。该框架使用FUSE-EVAL（一个衡量真实性偏离的多维度评估指标）来量化这种信息扭曲。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The FUSE framework demonstrates that LLM-based agent simulation can effectively model the gradual evolution of true news into fake news within a social network, highlighting the critical importance of early intervention to prevent misinformation spread. \[翻译\] FUSE框架表明，基于大语言模型的智能体模拟能够有效建模真实新闻在社交网络中逐步演变为虚假新闻的过程，凸显了早期干预对于防止错误信息传播的关键重要性。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The FUSE framework relies on LLM agents whose behaviors are simulated rather than empirically validated against real human data, potentially limiting the realism of the simulated evolution process. Its evaluation, while comprehensive, is primarily based on reproducing known fake news scenarios and may not fully capture the complexity of novel, emergent misinformation in dynamic online environments.  \[翻译\] FUSE框架依赖于LLM智能体，其行为是模拟而非基于真实人类数据进行实证验证的，这可能限制了模拟演化过程的真实性。其评估虽然全面，但主要基于复现已知的虚假新闻场景，可能无法完全捕捉动态在线环境中新颖、涌现的错误信息的复杂性。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] This paper proposes FUSE, a novel LLM-based agent simulation framework, to address the critical gap in understanding the gradual evolutionary process from true news to fake news within social networks, rather than assuming fake news as a pre-existing entity. \[翻译\] 本文提出FUSE，一个新颖的基于大语言模型的智能体仿真框架，旨在解决理解真实新闻在社交网络中如何逐步演变为虚假新闻这一关键研究空白，而非将虚假新闻视为预先存在的实体。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces FUSE, a novel LLM-based agent simulation framework that explicitly models the stepwise evolution of fake news from true news, rather than assuming its pre-existence. It innovates by designing a social network with four distinct LLM agent types \(spreaders, commentators, verifiers, bystanders\) to simulate realistic daily interactions that progressively distort information. A key contribution is the development of FUSE-EVAL, a comprehensive evaluation framework to quantify truth deviation across multiple linguistic and semantic dimensions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了FUSE，一个新颖的基于LLM的智能体仿真框架，其创新在于明确模拟了假新闻从真实新闻逐步演变的过程，而非假定其预先存在。它通过设计一个包含四种不同类型LLM智能体（传播者、评论者、验证者、旁观者）的社交网络来模拟导致信息逐步失真的日常互动。一个核心贡献是开发了FUSE-EVAL，一个用于从多维度语言和语义层面<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The FUSE framework employs an LLM-based agent simulation to model the evolution of fake news. It constructs a social network with four distinct LLM agent types \(spreaders, commentators, verifiers, bystanders\) to simulate daily interactions that progressively distort true information. The framework quantifies this distortion using FUSE-EVAL, a multi-dimensional evaluation metric for truth deviation.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>FUSE框架采用基于大语言模型的智能体模拟来建模虚假新闻的演化过程。它构建了一个包含四种不同类型智能体（传播者、评论者、验证者、旁观者）的社交网络，以模拟逐步扭曲真实信息的日常互动。该框架使用FUSE-EVAL（一个衡量真实性偏离的多维度评估指标）来量化这种信息扭曲。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The FUSE framework demonstrates that LLM-based agent simulation can effectively model the gradual evolution of true news into fake news within a social network, highlighting the critical importance of early intervention to prevent misinformation spread. \[翻译\] FUSE框架表明，基于大语言模型的智能体模拟能够有效建模真实新闻在社交网络中逐步演变为虚假新闻的过程，凸显了早期干预对于防止错误信息传播的关键重要性。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The FUSE framework relies on LLM agents whose behaviors are simulated rather than empirically validated against real human data, potentially limiting the realism of the simulated evolution process. Its evaluation, while comprehensive, is primarily based on reproducing known fake news scenarios and may not fully capture the complexity of novel, emergent misinformation in dynamic online environments.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>FUSE框架依赖于LLM智能体，其行为是模拟而非基于真实人类数据进行实证验证的，这可能限制了模拟演化过程的真实性。其评估虽然全面，但主要基于复现已知的虚假新闻场景，可能无法完全捕捉动态在线环境中新颖、涌现的错误信息的复杂性。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">agent模拟方法研究假新闻演变生成过程<br><br>\[\]【形象比喻：一个封闭的新闻传播仿真ABM系统，扮演四种角色，模拟假新闻形成过程】📊The Stepwise Deception: Simulating the Evolution from  True News to Fake News with LLM Agents<br>    📊20作者<br>    需要了解的基本信息<br>        会议/期刊：EMNLP<br>        年份：2024，2025<br>        类型：信息传播分析（虚假信息）<br>        领域：ABM<br>        阅读深入程度：略读<br>        评价：⭐️️️️⭐️️️️⭐️️️️<br><br>    \[主线概括\]<br>        \[目的\]：<br>            📊21之前的方法为什么会出现该问题？<br>                以往的研究通常假定虚假新闻固有存在且静态不变，研究其传播，而没有探讨其从原始新闻逐渐形成的过程。<br><br><br>        \[方法\]：涌现思路，使用多角色扮演模拟假新闻形成过程的FUSE仿真系统，提出了 FUSE-EVAL评估框架<br>            🔒方法具体步骤略，已列在别处<br>            🔒❔方法有哪些步骤？/方法由哪些组件构成<br>                ❔他是什么原理？<br>                    赋予每个LLM智能体一个固定的社会角色（传播者、评论者、验证者、旁观者）和个性化的人格档案（基于大五人格模型），来模拟真实人群中个体在信息处理方式、可信度判断和传播动机上的差异。<br>                    智能体根据预定义的社交网络结构 G = \(A, E\) 连接<br><br><br><br>        \[效果\]：能够再现已知的虚假新闻演化场景，与人类判断高度吻合<br>            ❔效果评估过程有什么值得注意的吗？<br>                为评估这个比较\\textbf{新颖}的研究领域，论文提出 FUSE-EVAL，从六个维度评估\\textbf{事实偏离程度}，这些维度值都是GPT-4o-mini评估得到的，是个\\textbf{自动过程}，为实现及时干预，每轮都会进行评估：<br>                    情感偏移（SS）<br>                    新信息引入（NII）<br>                    确定性偏移（CS）<br>                    风格偏移（STS）<br>                    时间偏移（TS）<br>                    视角偏离（PD）<br><br><br>            🔒❔实验关键发现<br>                （1）新闻表现出明显的累积失真效应，即内容在传播过程中逐渐偏离其原始形式<br>                （2）真实新闻演变为虚假新闻在高聚类网络中比在无尺度或随机网络中发生得更快<br>                （3）与其他主题（恐怖主义、自然灾害、科学和金融）相比，政治新闻显示出明显更快的演变速度<br>                消融评论员后，偏差降幅最大，证明其在假新闻进化中的关键作用<br>                提前干预的有效性得到证明<br><br>            ❔论文有哪里让人眼前一亮？<br>                FUSE在假新闻演化模拟上填补了“过程性研究”的空白，这是它的最大贡献<br>                论文实现了：在新闻事实偏移达到一定阈值时提前进行干预，而不是事后阻断传播，具有很强实用性<br><br>            ❔论文的思路和方法哪里做的不够好？/局限性<br>                【Weakness】仿真方法的固有局限：只能逼近，难以真正还原现实世界<br>                    论文模型明显简化了真实世界信息流动：每个agent信息的获取聚合自所有邻居的观点，这与真实情况不太符合。比如现实中大量旁观者不参与讨论，最多只是默默点赞，它的观点应较难获取<br>                        至少可以为每个角色设定一个观点传播权重<br>                        引入精力机制，限定每个agent一天可获取他人观点<br>                        引入观点置信机制，点赞量影响置信度<br>                        在更完善的大型ABM系统上实现<br><br>                    论文关注的是固有误传，没有考虑恶意造谣者？（不是简单的更易扭曲新闻的传播者，他会主动，目的性地编造信息），应该考虑并讨论<br><br>                【来自论文】缺乏相应的数据集，因此只能使用FUSE-EVAL衡量偏移程度，使用少量真实假新闻比较<br><br>            ❔论文对我的专业知识有什么启发？<br>                这是一个很简单的框架（800行代码），只是用来验证一个新颖研究领域的可行性已经足够</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-ACL%20Findings%202025-blue)]()<br>[GA-S^3: Comprehensive social network simulation with group agents](https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-acl.468/) <br> Yunyao Zhang, Zikai Song, Hang Zhou, Wenfeng Ren, Yi-Ping Phoebe Chen, Junqing Yu, Wei Yang <br> 2025-07 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Group-Oriented Social Simulation](#Group-Oriented-Social-Simulation-8-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] GA-S^3 employs group agents as computational units to simulate large-scale social networks, analogous to using representative samples to model population dynamics. \[翻译\] GA-S^3 采用群体智能体作为计算单元来模拟大规模社交网络，类似于使用代表性样本来建模群体动态。|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a comprehensive social network simulation system \(GA-S^3\) to address the challenge of modeling large-scale, complex social networks with billions of interacting individuals. Its core motivation is to enable accurate and realistic simulations of online social phenomena at a manageable computational cost by introducing a novel abstraction: group agents that model collections of individuals with similar behaviors, rather than simulating each individual separately.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出一个全面的社交网络模拟系统（GA-S^3），旨在解决对包含数十亿交互个体的大规模复杂社交网络进行建模的挑战。其核心动机是通过引入一种新颖的抽象——对具有相似行为的个体集合进行建模的群体智能体，而非单独模拟每个个体——从而以可控的计算成本实现对在线社交现象的准确且高度真实的模拟。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces a novel agent-based social simulation system \(GA-S^3\) that employs &quot;Group Agents&quot; to model collections of individuals with similar behaviors, enabling large-scale, realistic social network simulations at a reduced computational cost compared to individual-level agent models. It also contributes a new social network benchmark dataset derived from 2024 online events for evaluation.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了一种新颖的基于智能体的社会模拟系统（GA-S^3），该系统采用“群体智能体”来建模具有相似行为的个体集合，与个体级智能体模型相比，能以更低计算成本实现大规模、高真实性的社交网络模拟。同时，论文贡献了一个基于2024年在线事件构建的新社交网络基准数据集用于评估。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] GA-S³ employs a group agent-based simulation approach, where each agent models a collection of individuals with similar behaviors rather than a single entity. This design enables the simulation of large-scale social network phenomena with complex interactions at a manageable computational cost. The system leverages these agents to make intelligent decisions regarding various online events, facilitating accurate and realistic predictions.  \[翻译\] GA-S³采用了一种基于群体智能体的模拟方法，其中每个智能体模拟的是一组具有相似行为的个体集合，而非单一实体。这种设计使得系统能够以可控的计算成本模拟具有复杂交互的大规模社交网络现象。该系统利用这些智能体对各种在线事件做出智能决策，从而实现准确且高度真实的预测。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] GA-S^3 introduces a group agent paradigm for large-scale social network simulation, achieving accurate predictions of online event dynamics with manageable computational cost. \[翻译\] GA-S^3 提出了用于大规模社交网络仿真的群体智能体范式，以可控的计算成本实现了对在线事件动态的准确预测。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed group agent abstraction, while reducing computational cost, may oversimplify individual heterogeneity within groups, potentially limiting the simulation&#x27;s fidelity to fine-grained, individual-level social dynamics. \[翻译\] 所提出的群体智能体抽象虽然降低了计算成本，但可能过度简化了群体内部的个体异质性，这可能限制了模拟对细粒度、个体层面社会动态的保真度。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a comprehensive social network simulation system \(GA-S^3\) to address the challenge of modeling large-scale, complex social networks with billions of interacting individuals. Its core motivation is to enable accurate and realistic simulations of online social phenomena at a manageable computational cost by introducing a novel abstraction: group agents that model collections of individuals with similar behaviors, rather than simulating each individual separately.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出一个全面的社交网络模拟系统（GA-S^3），旨在解决对包含数十亿交互个体的大规模复杂社交网络进行建模的挑战。其核心动机是通过引入一种新颖的抽象——对具有相似行为的个体集合进行建模的群体智能体，而非单独模拟每个个体——从而以可控的计算成本实现对在线社交现象的准确且高度真实的模拟。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces a novel agent-based social simulation system \(GA-S^3\) that employs "Group Agents" to model collections of individuals with similar behaviors, enabling large-scale, realistic social network simulations at a reduced computational cost compared to individual-level agent models. It also contributes a new social network benchmark dataset derived from 2024 online events for evaluation.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种新颖的基于智能体的社会模拟系统（GA-S^3），该系统采用“群体智能体”来建模具有相似行为的个体集合，与个体级智能体模型相比，能以更低计算成本实现大规模、高真实性的社交网络模拟。同时，论文贡献了一个基于2024年在线事件构建的新社交网络基准数据集用于评估。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] GA-S³ employs a group agent-based simulation approach, where each agent models a collection of individuals with similar behaviors rather than a single entity. This design enables the simulation of large-scale social network phenomena with complex interactions at a manageable computational cost. The system leverages these agents to make intelligent decisions regarding various online events, facilitating accurate and realistic predictions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>GA-S³采用了一种基于群体智能体的模拟方法，其中每个智能体模拟的是一组具有相似行为的个体集合，而非单一实体。这种设计使得系统能够以可控的计算成本模拟具有复杂交互的大规模社交网络现象。该系统利用这些智能体对各种在线事件做出智能决策，从而实现准确且高度真实的预测。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] GA-S^3 introduces a group agent paradigm for large-scale social network simulation, achieving accurate predictions of online event dynamics with manageable computational cost. \[翻译\] GA-S^3 提出了用于大规模社交网络仿真的群体智能体范式，以可控的计算成本实现了对在线事件动态的准确预测。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed group agent abstraction, while reducing computational cost, may oversimplify individual heterogeneity within groups, potentially limiting the simulation's fidelity to fine-grained, individual-level social dynamics. \[翻译\] 所提出的群体智能体抽象虽然降低了计算成本，但可能过度简化了群体内部的个体异质性，这可能限制了模拟对细粒度、个体层面社会动态的保真度。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Group-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "GA-S^3: Comprehensive social network simulation with group agents",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Social network simulation is developed to provide a comprehensive understanding of social networks in the real world, which can be leveraged for a wide range of applications such as group behavior emergence, policy optimization, and business strategy development. However, billions of individuals and their evolving interactions involved in social networks pose challenges in accurately reflecting real-world complexities. In this study, we propose a comprehensive Social network Simulation System \(G"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Humanit%20Soc%20Sci%20Commun-blue)]()<br>[Large language models empowered agent-based modeling and simulation: a survey and perspectives](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41599-024-03611-3) <br> Chen Gao, Xiaochong Lan, Nian Li, Yuan Yuan, Jingtao Ding, Zhilun Zhou, Fengli Xu, Yong Li <br> 2024-09-27 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Group-Oriented Social Simulation](#Group-Oriented-Social-Simulation-8-papers), [Social Simulation for Other Fields](#Social-Simulation-for-Other-Fields-1-papers), [Other](#-Other-7-papers)]</span>|TLDR: This paper surveys the landscape of utilizing large language models in agent-based modeling and simulation, discussing their challenges and promising future directions and provides a comprehensive overview of the recent works of large language model-empowered agent-based modeling and simulation in multiple scenarios.|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\) is motivated by the need to overcome the limitations of traditional simulation agents, which often lack the autonomy, social intelligence, and adaptive reasoning required for realistic modeling of complex systems. LLM-empowered agents offer enhanced capabilities in environment perception, human-like decision-making, and adaptive learning, enabling more nuanced and scalable simulations across social, physical, cyber, and hybrid domains.  \[翻译\] 将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中，其动机源于需要克服传统仿真主体的局限性。传统主体通常缺乏自主性、社会智能和自适应推理能力，而这些对于复杂系统的真实建模至关重要。LLM赋能的主体在环境感知、类人决策和自适应学习方面提供了增强的能力，从而能够在社会、物理、网络和混合领域实现更细致、可扩展的仿真。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This survey pioneers a systematic review of integrating Large Language Models \(LLM\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\), establishing a foundational taxonomy across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains. It uniquely identifies and analyzes core technical challenges—environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation—providing a structured framework for this nascent, interdisciplinary field. The work also curates a comprehensive repository of representative papers and code, serving as a key resource for future research.  \[翻译\] 本综述开创性地系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究，建立了涵盖网络、物理、社会及混合领域的基础分类体系。它独特地识别并分析了环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成与评估等核心技术挑战，为这一新兴的跨学科领域提供了结构化框架。该工作还整理了一个包含代表性论文与代码的综合资源库，为未来研究提供了关键资源。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the literature by first establishing interdisciplinary background knowledge, then analyzing the motivation and core challenges of integrating LLMs into ABMS. It provides a comprehensive overview of recent works categorized into four simulation domains \(cyber, physical, social, hybrid\) and discusses how they address key challenges. Finally, it outlines open problems and future research directions.  \[翻译\] 本综述采用系统性的方法：首先建立跨学科的背景知识，随后分析将大语言模型融入基于主体的建模与仿真的动机与核心挑战。它提供了一个全面的近期工作概览，这些工作被分为四个仿真领域（网络、物理、社会、混合），并讨论了它们如何应对关键挑战。最后，文章概述了开放性问题与未来的研究方向。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\). It highlights how LLM-empowered agents enhance simulation realism by addressing core challenges in environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation. The paper provides a comprehensive overview of recent applications across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains, and discusses open problems and promising future directions for this rapidly evolving interdisciplinary field.  \[翻译\] 本综述系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLMs）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究。文章强调了LLM赋能的主体如何通过解决环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成和评估等核心挑战来提升仿真的真实性。论文全面概述了在信息、物理、社会和混合领域的最新应用，并讨论了这一快速发展的跨学科领域所面临的开放性问题与未来前景。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The survey primarily focuses on conceptual frameworks and high-level applications, lacking a systematic empirical evaluation of LLM agents&#x27; performance across diverse simulation scenarios. It also does not deeply address the computational costs, ethical risks, and potential biases inherent in deploying LLMs for large-scale, realistic social simulations.  \[翻译\] 本综述主要关注概念框架和高层应用，缺乏对不同模拟场景下大语言模型智能体性能的系统性实证评估。同时，也未深入探讨部署大语言模型进行大规模、现实社会模拟所涉及的计算成本、伦理风险及潜在偏见问题。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\) is motivated by the need to overcome the limitations of traditional simulation agents, which often lack the autonomy, social intelligence, and adaptive reasoning required for realistic modeling of complex systems. LLM-empowered agents offer enhanced capabilities in environment perception, human-like decision-making, and adaptive learning, enabling more nuanced and scalable simulations across social, physical, cyber, and hybrid domains.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中，其动机源于需要克服传统仿真主体的局限性。传统主体通常缺乏自主性、社会智能和自适应推理能力，而这些对于复杂系统的真实建模至关重要。LLM赋能的主体在环境感知、类人决策和自适应学习方面提供了增强的能力，从而能够在社会、物理、网络和混合领域实现更细致、可扩展的仿真。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This survey pioneers a systematic review of integrating Large Language Models \(LLM\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\), establishing a foundational taxonomy across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains. It uniquely identifies and analyzes core technical challenges—environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation—providing a structured framework for this nascent, interdisciplinary field. The work also curates a comprehensive repository of representative papers and code, serving as a key resource for future research.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述开创性地系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究，建立了涵盖网络、物理、社会及混合领域的基础分类体系。它独特地识别并分析了环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成与评估等核心技术挑战，为这一新兴的跨学科领域提供了结构化框架。该工作还整理了一个包含代表性论文与代码的综合资源库，为未来研究提供了关键资源。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the literature by first establishing interdisciplinary background knowledge, then analyzing the motivation and core challenges of integrating LLMs into ABMS. It provides a comprehensive overview of recent works categorized into four simulation domains \(cyber, physical, social, hybrid\) and discusses how they address key challenges. Finally, it outlines open problems and future research directions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述采用系统性的方法：首先建立跨学科的背景知识，随后分析将大语言模型融入基于主体的建模与仿真的动机与核心挑战。它提供了一个全面的近期工作概览，这些工作被分为四个仿真领域（网络、物理、社会、混合），并讨论了它们如何应对关键挑战。最后，文章概述了开放性问题与未来的研究方向。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\). It highlights how LLM-empowered agents enhance simulation realism by addressing core challenges in environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation. The paper provides a comprehensive overview of recent applications across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains, and discusses open problems and promising future directions for this rapidly evolving interdisciplinary field.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLMs）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究。文章强调了LLM赋能的主体如何通过解决环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成和评估等核心挑战来提升仿真的真实性。论文全面概述了在信息、物理、社会和混合领域的最新应用，并讨论了这一快速发展的跨学科领域所面临的开放性问题与未来前景。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The survey primarily focuses on conceptual frameworks and high-level applications, lacking a systematic empirical evaluation of LLM agents' performance across diverse simulation scenarios. It also does not deeply address the computational costs, ethical risks, and potential biases inherent in deploying LLMs for large-scale, realistic social simulations.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述主要关注概念框架和高层应用，缺乏对不同模拟场景下大语言模型智能体性能的系统性实证评估。同时，也未深入探讨部署大语言模型进行大规模、现实社会模拟所涉及的计算成本、伦理风险及潜在偏见问题。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Group-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Other Fields"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Large language models empowered agent-based modeling and simulation: a survey and perspectives",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-09-27"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Abstract Agent-based modeling and simulation have evolved as a powerful tool for modeling complex systems, offering insights into emergent behaviors and interactions among diverse agents.
