The system leverages these agents to make intelligent decisions regarding various online events, facilitating accurate and realistic predictions.  \[翻译\] GA-S³采用了一种基于群体智能体的模拟方法，其中每个智能体模拟的是一组具有相似行为的个体集合，而非单一实体。这种设计使得系统能够以可控的计算成本模拟具有复杂交互的大规模社交网络现象。该系统利用这些智能体对各种在线事件做出智能决策，从而实现准确且高度真实的预测。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] GA-S^3 introduces a group agent paradigm for large-scale social network simulation, achieving accurate predictions of online event dynamics with manageable computational cost. \[翻译\] GA-S^3 提出了用于大规模社交网络仿真的群体智能体范式，以可控的计算成本实现了对在线事件动态的准确预测。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed group agent abstraction, while reducing computational cost, may oversimplify individual heterogeneity within groups, potentially limiting the simulation&#x27;s fidelity to fine-grained, individual-level social dynamics. \[翻译\] 所提出的群体智能体抽象虽然降低了计算成本，但可能过度简化了群体内部的个体异质性，这可能限制了模拟对细粒度、个体层面社会动态的保真度。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes a comprehensive social network simulation system \(GA-S^3\) to address the challenge of modeling large-scale, complex social networks with billions of interacting individuals. Its core motivation is to enable accurate and realistic simulations of online social phenomena at a manageable computational cost by introducing a novel abstraction: group agents that model collections of individuals with similar behaviors, rather than simulating each individual separately.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出一个全面的社交网络模拟系统（GA-S^3），旨在解决对包含数十亿交互个体的大规模复杂社交网络进行建模的挑战。其核心动机是通过引入一种新颖的抽象——对具有相似行为的个体集合进行建模的群体智能体，而非单独模拟每个个体——从而以可控的计算成本实现对在线社交现象的准确且高度真实的模拟。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces a novel agent-based social simulation system \(GA-S^3\) that employs "Group Agents" to model collections of individuals with similar behaviors, enabling large-scale, realistic social network simulations at a reduced computational cost compared to individual-level agent models. It also contributes a new social network benchmark dataset derived from 2024 online events for evaluation.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了一种新颖的基于智能体的社会模拟系统（GA-S^3），该系统采用“群体智能体”来建模具有相似行为的个体集合，与个体级智能体模型相比，能以更低计算成本实现大规模、高真实性的社交网络模拟。同时，论文贡献了一个基于2024年在线事件构建的新社交网络基准数据集用于评估。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] GA-S³ employs a group agent-based simulation approach, where each agent models a collection of individuals with similar behaviors rather than a single entity. This design enables the simulation of large-scale social network phenomena with complex interactions at a manageable computational cost. The system leverages these agents to make intelligent decisions regarding various online events, facilitating accurate and realistic predictions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>GA-S³采用了一种基于群体智能体的模拟方法，其中每个智能体模拟的是一组具有相似行为的个体集合，而非单一实体。这种设计使得系统能够以可控的计算成本模拟具有复杂交互的大规模社交网络现象。该系统利用这些智能体对各种在线事件做出智能决策，从而实现准确且高度真实的预测。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] GA-S^3 introduces a group agent paradigm for large-scale social network simulation, achieving accurate predictions of online event dynamics with manageable computational cost. \[翻译\] GA-S^3 提出了用于大规模社交网络仿真的群体智能体范式，以可控的计算成本实现了对在线事件动态的准确预测。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The proposed group agent abstraction, while reducing computational cost, may oversimplify individual heterogeneity within groups, potentially limiting the simulation's fidelity to fine-grained, individual-level social dynamics. \[翻译\] 所提出的群体智能体抽象虽然降低了计算成本，但可能过度简化了群体内部的个体异质性，这可能限制了模拟对细粒度、个体层面社会动态的保真度。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Group-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "GA-S^3: Comprehensive social network simulation with group agents",<br>    "publication_date": "2025-07"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Social network simulation is developed to provide a comprehensive understanding of social networks in the real world, which can be leveraged for a wide range of applications such as group behavior emergence, policy optimization, and business strategy development. However, billions of individuals and their evolving interactions involved in social networks pose challenges in accurately reflecting real-world complexities. In this study, we propose a comprehensive Social network Simulation System \(G"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Humanit%20Soc%20Sci%20Commun-blue)]()<br>[Large language models empowered agent-based modeling and simulation: a survey and perspectives](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41599-024-03611-3) <br> Chen Gao, Xiaochong Lan, Nian Li, Yuan Yuan, Jingtao Ding, Zhilun Zhou, Fengli Xu, Yong Li <br> 2024-09-27 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Group-Oriented Social Simulation](#Group-Oriented-Social-Simulation-8-papers), [Social Simulation for Other Fields](#Social-Simulation-for-Other-Fields-1-papers), [Other](#-Other-7-papers)]</span>|TLDR: This paper surveys the landscape of utilizing large language models in agent-based modeling and simulation, discussing their challenges and promising future directions and provides a comprehensive overview of the recent works of large language model-empowered agent-based modeling and simulation in multiple scenarios.|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\) is motivated by the need to overcome the limitations of traditional simulation agents, which often lack the autonomy, social intelligence, and adaptive reasoning required for realistic modeling of complex systems. LLM-empowered agents offer enhanced capabilities in environment perception, human-like decision-making, and adaptive learning, enabling more nuanced and scalable simulations across social, physical, cyber, and hybrid domains.  \[翻译\] 将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中，其动机源于需要克服传统仿真主体的局限性。传统主体通常缺乏自主性、社会智能和自适应推理能力，而这些对于复杂系统的真实建模至关重要。LLM赋能的主体在环境感知、类人决策和自适应学习方面提供了增强的能力，从而能够在社会、物理、网络和混合领域实现更细致、可扩展的仿真。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This survey pioneers a systematic review of integrating Large Language Models \(LLM\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\), establishing a foundational taxonomy across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains. It uniquely identifies and analyzes core technical challenges—environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation—providing a structured framework for this nascent, interdisciplinary field. The work also curates a comprehensive repository of representative papers and code, serving as a key resource for future research.  \[翻译\] 本综述开创性地系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究，建立了涵盖网络、物理、社会及混合领域的基础分类体系。它独特地识别并分析了环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成与评估等核心技术挑战，为这一新兴的跨学科领域提供了结构化框架。该工作还整理了一个包含代表性论文与代码的综合资源库，为未来研究提供了关键资源。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the literature by first establishing interdisciplinary background knowledge, then analyzing the motivation and core challenges of integrating LLMs into ABMS. It provides a comprehensive overview of recent works categorized into four simulation domains \(cyber, physical, social, hybrid\) and discusses how they address key challenges. Finally, it outlines open problems and future research directions.  \[翻译\] 本综述采用系统性的方法：首先建立跨学科的背景知识，随后分析将大语言模型融入基于主体的建模与仿真的动机与核心挑战。它提供了一个全面的近期工作概览，这些工作被分为四个仿真领域（网络、物理、社会、混合），并讨论了它们如何应对关键挑战。最后，文章概述了开放性问题与未来的研究方向。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\). It highlights how LLM-empowered agents enhance simulation realism by addressing core challenges in environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation. The paper provides a comprehensive overview of recent applications across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains, and discusses open problems and promising future directions for this rapidly evolving interdisciplinary field.  \[翻译\] 本综述系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLMs）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究。文章强调了LLM赋能的主体如何通过解决环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成和评估等核心挑战来提升仿真的真实性。论文全面概述了在信息、物理、社会和混合领域的最新应用，并讨论了这一快速发展的跨学科领域所面临的开放性问题与未来前景。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The survey primarily focuses on conceptual frameworks and high-level applications, lacking a systematic empirical evaluation of LLM agents&#x27; performance across diverse simulation scenarios. It also does not deeply address the computational costs, ethical risks, and potential biases inherent in deploying LLMs for large-scale, realistic social simulations.  \[翻译\] 本综述主要关注概念框架和高层应用，缺乏对不同模拟场景下大语言模型智能体性能的系统性实证评估。同时，也未深入探讨部署大语言模型进行大规模、现实社会模拟所涉及的计算成本、伦理风险及潜在偏见问题。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\) is motivated by the need to overcome the limitations of traditional simulation agents, which often lack the autonomy, social intelligence, and adaptive reasoning required for realistic modeling of complex systems. LLM-empowered agents offer enhanced capabilities in environment perception, human-like decision-making, and adaptive learning, enabling more nuanced and scalable simulations across social, physical, cyber, and hybrid domains.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中，其动机源于需要克服传统仿真主体的局限性。传统主体通常缺乏自主性、社会智能和自适应推理能力，而这些对于复杂系统的真实建模至关重要。LLM赋能的主体在环境感知、类人决策和自适应学习方面提供了增强的能力，从而能够在社会、物理、网络和混合领域实现更细致、可扩展的仿真。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This survey pioneers a systematic review of integrating Large Language Models \(LLM\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\), establishing a foundational taxonomy across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains. It uniquely identifies and analyzes core technical challenges—environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation—providing a structured framework for this nascent, interdisciplinary field. The work also curates a comprehensive repository of representative papers and code, serving as a key resource for future research.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述开创性地系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究，建立了涵盖网络、物理、社会及混合领域的基础分类体系。它独特地识别并分析了环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成与评估等核心技术挑战，为这一新兴的跨学科领域提供了结构化框架。该工作还整理了一个包含代表性论文与代码的综合资源库，为未来研究提供了关键资源。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the literature by first establishing interdisciplinary background knowledge, then analyzing the motivation and core challenges of integrating LLMs into ABMS. It provides a comprehensive overview of recent works categorized into four simulation domains \(cyber, physical, social, hybrid\) and discusses how they address key challenges. Finally, it outlines open problems and future research directions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述采用系统性的方法：首先建立跨学科的背景知识，随后分析将大语言模型融入基于主体的建模与仿真的动机与核心挑战。它提供了一个全面的近期工作概览，这些工作被分为四个仿真领域（网络、物理、社会、混合），并讨论了它们如何应对关键挑战。最后，文章概述了开放性问题与未来的研究方向。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\). It highlights how LLM-empowered agents enhance simulation realism by addressing core challenges in environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation. The paper provides a comprehensive overview of recent applications across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains, and discusses open problems and promising future directions for this rapidly evolving interdisciplinary field.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLMs）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究。文章强调了LLM赋能的主体如何通过解决环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成和评估等核心挑战来提升仿真的真实性。论文全面概述了在信息、物理、社会和混合领域的最新应用，并讨论了这一快速发展的跨学科领域所面临的开放性问题与未来前景。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The survey primarily focuses on conceptual frameworks and high-level applications, lacking a systematic empirical evaluation of LLM agents' performance across diverse simulation scenarios. It also does not deeply address the computational costs, ethical risks, and potential biases inherent in deploying LLMs for large-scale, realistic social simulations.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述主要关注概念框架和高层应用，缺乏对不同模拟场景下大语言模型智能体性能的系统性实证评估。同时，也未深入探讨部署大语言模型进行大规模、现实社会模拟所涉及的计算成本、伦理风险及潜在偏见问题。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Group-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Other Fields"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Large language models empowered agent-based modeling and simulation: a survey and perspectives",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-09-27"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Abstract Agent-based modeling and simulation have evolved as a powerful tool for modeling complex systems, offering insights into emergent behaviors and interactions among diverse agents. Recently, integrating large language models into agent-based modeling and simulation presents a promising avenue for enhancing simulation capabilities. This paper surveys the landscape of utilizing large language models in agent-based modeling and simulation, discussing their challenges and promising future dir"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-EMNLP%202024-blue)]()<br>[PREDICT: Multi-agent-based debate simulation for generalized hate speech detection](https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-main.1166/) <br> Someen Park, Jaehoon Kim, Seungwan Jin, Sohyun Park, Kyungsik Han <br> 2024-11 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Hate Speech Analysis](#Hate-Speech-Analysis-34-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] PREDICT simulates a debate among agents with diverse labeling perspectives to reach a consensus for generalized hate speech detection. \[翻译\] PREDICT 通过模拟具有不同标注视角的智能体之间的辩论，以达成共识，实现泛化的仇恨言论检测。|<div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;align-items:center"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/4379fd73/PREDICT.png"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/4379fd73/PREDICT2.png"></div>| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The PREDICT framework is motivated by the challenge of generalizing hate speech detection models across datasets with divergent labeling criteria. It proposes a multi-agent debate simulation to integrate diverse perspectives and reach consensus, aiming to improve detection robustness and interpretability. \[翻译\] PREDICT框架的动机源于解决仇恨言论检测模型在不同标注标准数据集间泛化能力不足的挑战。它提出了一种多智能体辩论模拟方法，旨在整合多样化的视角并达成共识，以提升检测的鲁棒性和可解释性。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] PREDICT introduces a multi-agent debate framework for generalized hate speech detection, addressing dataset labeling inconsistencies by simulating pluralistic decision-making. It first creates agents with distinct perspectives derived from different datasets&#x27; labeling criteria to generate stances and reasons. Then, agents engage in structured debates using these incongruent references, with a judge agent making the final classification and providing a balanced rationale. This approach outperforms methods relying on specific criteria or majority voting in cross-dataset evaluations.  \[翻译\] PREDICT提出了一种用于泛化仇恨言论检测的多智能体辩论框架，通过模拟多元化决策来解决数据集标注标准不一致的问题。该框架首先基于不同数据集的标注标准创建具有独特视角的智能体，以生成立场和理由。随后，智能体利用这些不一致的参考进行结构化辩论，并由一个裁判智能体做出最终分类并提供平衡的推理。该方法在跨数据集评估中优于依赖特定标准或多数投票的方法。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] PREDICT is a multi-agent debate simulation framework for generalized hate speech detection. It consists of two phases: PRE \(Perspective-based Reasoning\), where agents are created based on the labeling criteria of different datasets to generate stances and reasons; and DICT \(Debate using InCongruenT references\), where agents debate based on these stances, and a judge agent makes the final classification and provides a balanced reason. \[翻译\] PREDICT是一个用于广义仇恨言论检测的多智能体辩论模拟框架。它包含两个阶段：PRE（基于视角的推理），根据不同数据集的标注标准创建智能体以生成立场和理由；以及DICT（使用不一致参考的辩论），智能体基于这些立场进行辩论，并由一个法官智能体做出最终分类并提供平衡的理由。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] PREDICT introduces a multi-agent debate framework for generalized hate speech detection, effectively mediating diverse labeling criteria from different datasets to achieve consensus and superior cross-dataset performance. \[翻译\] PREDICT提出了一种用于广义仇恨言论检测的多智能体辩论框架，通过调解不同数据集的多样化标注标准达成共识，实现了卓越的跨数据集性能。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The PREDICT framework relies on manually refined labeling criteria from each dataset to define agent perspectives, which may not fully capture the nuanced and evolving nature of hate speech definitions across different cultural and social contexts. Its performance is also contingent on the reasoning and debate capabilities of the underlying LLM, which may inherit biases or exhibit inconsistent reasoning. \[翻译\] PREDICT框架依赖于从各数据集中手动提炼的标注标准来定义智能体视角，这可能无法完全捕捉不同文化和社会背景下仇恨言论定义的细微差别和动态演变。其性能也取决于底层大语言模型的推理和辩论能力，而该模型可能继承偏见或表现出不一致的推理。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The PREDICT framework is motivated by the challenge of generalizing hate speech detection models across datasets with divergent labeling criteria. It proposes a multi-agent debate simulation to integrate diverse perspectives and reach consensus, aiming to improve detection robustness and interpretability. \[翻译\] PREDICT框架的动机源于解决仇恨言论检测模型在不同标注标准数据集间泛化能力不足的挑战。它提出了一种多智能体辩论模拟方法，旨在整合多样化的视角并达成共识，以提升检测的鲁棒性和可解释性。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] PREDICT introduces a multi-agent debate framework for generalized hate speech detection, addressing dataset labeling inconsistencies by simulating pluralistic decision-making. It first creates agents with distinct perspectives derived from different datasets' labeling criteria to generate stances and reasons. Then, agents engage in structured debates using these incongruent references, with a judge agent making the final classification and providing a balanced rationale. This approach outperforms methods relying on specific criteria or majority voting in cross-dataset evaluations.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>PREDICT提出了一种用于泛化仇恨言论检测的多智能体辩论框架，通过模拟多元化决策来解决数据集标注标准不一致的问题。该框架首先基于不同数据集的标注标准创建具有独特视角的智能体，以生成立场和理由。随后，智能体利用这些不一致的参考进行结构化辩论，并由一个裁判智能体做出最终分类并提供平衡的推理。该方法在跨数据集评估中优于依赖特定标准或多数投票的方法。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] PREDICT is a multi-agent debate simulation framework for generalized hate speech detection. It consists of two phases: PRE \(Perspective-based Reasoning\), where agents are created based on the labeling criteria of different datasets to generate stances and reasons; and DICT \(Debate using InCongruenT references\), where agents debate based on these stances, and a judge agent makes the final classification and provides a balanced reason. \[翻译\] PREDICT是一个用于广义仇恨言论检测的多智能体辩论模拟框架。它包含两个阶段：PRE（基于视角的推理），根据不同数据集的标注标准创建智能体以生成立场和理由；以及DICT（使用不一致参考的辩论），智能体基于这些立场进行辩论，并由一个法官智能体做出最终分类并提供平衡的理由。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] PREDICT introduces a multi-agent debate framework for generalized hate speech detection, effectively mediating diverse labeling criteria from different datasets to achieve consensus and superior cross-dataset performance. \[翻译\] PREDICT提出了一种用于广义仇恨言论检测的多智能体辩论框架，通过调解不同数据集的多样化标注标准达成共识，实现了卓越的跨数据集性能。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The PREDICT framework relies on manually refined labeling criteria from each dataset to define agent perspectives, which may not fully capture the nuanced and evolving nature of hate speech definitions across different cultural and social contexts. Its performance is also contingent on the reasoning and debate capabilities of the underlying LLM, which may inherit biases or exhibit inconsistent reasoning. \[翻译\] PREDICT框架依赖于从各数据集中手动提炼的标注标准来定义智能体视角，这可能无法完全捕捉不同文化和社会背景下仇恨言论定义的细微差别和动态演变。其性能也取决于底层大语言模型的推理和辩论能力，而该模型可能继承偏见或表现出不一致的推理。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Hate Speech Analysis",<br>    "Micro Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Individual-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "PREDICT: Multi-agent-based debate simulation for generalized hate speech detection",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-11"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "While a few public benchmarks have been proposed for training hate speech detection models, the differences in labeling criteria between these benchmarks pose challenges for generalized learning, limiting the applicability of the models. Previous research has presented methods to generalize models through data integration or augmentation, but overcoming the differences in labeling criteria between datasets remains a limitation. To address these challenges, we propose PREDICT, a novel framework t"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-LREC--COLING%202024-blue)]()<br>[Leveraging social context for humor recognition and sense of humor evaluation in social media wit...](https://aclanthology.org/2024.lrec-main.908/) <br> Zeyuan Zeng, Zefeng Li, Liang Yang, Hongfei Lin <br> 2024-05 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Micro-Level Pragmatic Expressions](#Micro-Level-Pragmatic-Expressions-17-papers)]</span>||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Humor Recognition",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media",<br>    "Micro Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Leveraging social context for humor recognition and sense of humor evaluation in social media with a new Chinese humor corpus - HumorWB",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-05"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "With the development of the Internet, social media has produced a large amount of user-generated data, which brings new challenges for humor computing. Traditional humor computing research mainly focuses on the content, while neglecting the information of interaction relationships in social media. In addition, both content and users are important in social media, while existing humor computing research mainly focuses on content rather than people. To address these problems, we model the informat"<br>}</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-LREC--COLING%202024-blue)]()<br>[Continuous relational diffusion driven topic model with multi-grained text for microblog](https://aclanthology.org/2024.lrec-main.345/) <br> Chenhao Wu, Ruifang He, Chang Liu, Bo Wang <br> 2024-05 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Information Diffusion Analysis](#Information-Diffusion-Analysis-17-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers), [Topic Modeling](#Topic-Modeling-6-papers)]</span>|连续关系扩散驱动的微博多粒度话题模型|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Topic Modeling",<br>    "Information Diffusion Analysis",<br>    "Micro Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Individual-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Continuous relational diffusion driven topic model with multi-grained text for microblog",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-05"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Topic model is a statistical model that leverages unsupervised learning to mine hidden topics in document collections. The data sparsity and colloquialism of social texts make it difficult to accurately mine the topics. Traditional methods assume that there are only 0/1-state relationships between the two parties in the social networks, but the relationship status in real life is more complicated, such as continuously changing relationships with different degrees of intimacy. This paper proposes"<br>}</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-THE%20WEB%20CONFERENCE%202025-blue)]()<br>[Thematic-LM: A LLM-based multi-agent system for large-scale thematic analysis](https://openreview.net/forum?id=jiv0Gl6sto#discussion) <br> Tingrui Qiao, Caroline Walker, Chris W. Cunningham, Yun Sing Koh <br> 2025-01-29 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers)]</span>||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Individual-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Thematic-LM: A LLM-based multi-agent system for large-scale thematic analysis",<br>    "publication_date": "2025/01/29"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Thematic analysis \(TA\) is a widely used qualitative method for identifying underlying meanings within unstructured text. However, TA requires manual processes, which become increasingly labour-intensive and time-consuming as datasets grow. While large language models \(LLMs\) have been introduced to assist with TA on small-scale datasets, three key limitations hinder their effectiveness on larger datasets. First, current approaches often depend on interactions between an LLM agent and a human code"<br>}</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Proceedings%20of%20the%20AAAI%20Conference%20on%20Artificial%20Intelligence-blue)]()<br>[Graph bayesian optimization for multiplex influence maximization](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/30255) <br> Zirui Yuan, Minglai Shao, Zhiqian Chen <br> 2024-03-24 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers), [Information Diffusion Analysis](#Information-Diffusion-Analysis-17-papers)]</span>|TLDR: This paper first formulates the Multiplex Influence Maximization \(Multi-IM\) problem using multiplex diffusion models with an information association mechanism, and proposes Graph Bayesian Optimization for Multi-IM \(GBIM\).|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Social Simulation",<br>    "Micro Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Individual-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Graph bayesian optimization for multiplex influence maximization",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-03-24"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Influence maximization \(IM\) is the problem of identifying a limited number of initial influential users within a social network to maximize the number of influenced users. However, previous research has mostly focused on individual information propagation, neglecting the simultaneous and interactive dissemination of multiple information items. In reality, when users encounter a piece of information, such as a smartphone product, they often associate it with related products in their minds, such "<br>}</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Proceedings%20of%20the%2032nd%20ACM%20International%20Conference%20on%20Multimedia-blue)]()<br>[Mitigating social hazards: Early detection of fake news via diffusion-guided propagation path gen...](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3664647.3681087) <br> Litian Zhang, Xiaoming Zhang, Chaozhuo Li, Ziyi Zhou, Jiacheng Liu, Feiran Huang, Xi Zhang <br> 十月 28, 2024 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Group-Oriented Social Simulation](#Group-Oriented-Social-Simulation-8-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers), [Information Diffusion Analysis](#Information-Diffusion-Analysis-17-papers)]</span>||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Group-oriented Social Simulation"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Mitigating social hazards: Early detection of fake news via diffusion-guided propagation path generation",<br>    "publication_date": "十月 28, 2024"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "The detection of fake news has emerged as a pressing issue in the era of online social media. To detect meticulously fabricated fake news, propagation paths are introduced to provide nuanced social context to complement the pure semantics within news content. However, existing propagation-enhanced models face a dilemma between detection efficacy and social hazard. In this paper, we investigate the novel problem of early fake news detection via propagation path generation, capable of enjoying the"<br>}</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-The%202023%20Conference%20on%20Empirical%20Methods%20in%20Natural%20Language%20Processing-blue)]()<br>[From values to opinions: Predicting human behaviors and stances using value-injected large langua...](https://openreview.net/forum?id=1BMj6opwbj) <br> Dongjun Kang, Joonsuk Park, Yohan Jo, JinYeong Bak <br> 2023-12-01 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers), [Social Simulation for Sociology](#Social-Simulation-for-Sociology-3-papers), [User Stance Detection](#User-Stance-Detection-19-papers)]</span>||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Social Simulation",<br>    "Micro Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Individual-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Sociology"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "From values to opinions: Predicting human behaviors and stances using value-injected large language models",<br>    "publication_date": "2023/12/01"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Being able to predict people's opinions on issues and behaviors in realistic scenarios can be helpful in various domains, such as politics and marketing. However, conducting large-scale surveys like the European Social Survey to solicit people's opinions on individual issues can incur prohibitive costs. Leveraging prior research showing influence of core human values on individual decisions and actions, we propose to use value-injected large language models \(LLM\) to predict opinions and behavior"<br>}</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Findings%202024-blue)]()<br>[I'm sure you're a real scholar yourself: Exploring ironic content generation by large language mo...](https://aclanthology.org/2024.findings-emnlp.847/) <br> Pier Felice Balestrucci, Silvia Casola, Soda Marem Lo, Valerio Basile, Alessandro Mazzei <br> 2024-11 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Humor Generation](#Humor-Generation-4-papers)]</span>||| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Social Simulation for Social Media",<br>    "Micro Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Individual-oriented Social Simulation"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "I'm sure you're a real scholar yourself: Exploring ironic content generation by large language models",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-11"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Generating ironic content is challenging: it requires a nuanced understanding of context and implicit references and balancing seriousness and playfulness. Moreover, irony is highly subjective and can depend on various factors, such as social, cultural, or generational aspects. This paper explores whether Large Language Models \(LLMs\) can learn to generate ironic responses to social media posts. To do so, we fine-tune two models to generate ironic and non-ironic content and deeply analyze their o"<br>}</div></details></div>|

##### Individual-Oriented Social Simulation (16 papers)

| Title & Info | Analogy Summary | Pipeline | Summary |
|:--| :---: | :----: | :---: |
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-The%20Fourteenth%20International%20Conference%20on%20Learning%20Representations-blue)]()<br>[Social agents: Collective intelligence improves LLM predictions](https://openreview.net/forum?id=73J3hsato3) <br> Aanisha Bhattacharyya, Abhilekh Borah, Yaman Kumar Singla, Rajiv Ratn Shah, Changyou Chen, Balaji Krishnamurthy <br> 2025-10-08 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers), [Social Simulation for Social Media](#Social-Simulation-for-Social-Media-24-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Social Agents: Simulating a diverse synthetic society to aggregate opinions, improving LLM predictions via collective intelligence. \[翻译\] 社会智能体：通过模拟多样化的合成社会来聚合观点，借助集体智慧提升大语言模型的预测能力。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/7d44cfb3/COLLECTIVE INTELLIGENCE.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to improve the accuracy and realism of LLM-based behavioral predictions by simulating a diverse crowd of synthetic personas, moving beyond single, definitive LLM outputs to better capture the heterogeneity of human judgment. \[翻译\] 该论文旨在通过模拟一个多样化的合成人物群体，超越单一、确定的LLM输出，以更好地捕捉人类判断的异质性，从而提高基于LLM的行为预测的准确性和真实性。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of diverse human-like personas to improve LLM predictions. By aggregating independent judgments from these agents, it applies the &quot;Wisdom of Crowds&quot; principle to behavioral forecasting, outperforming single-LLM baselines across multiple tasks and achieving higher alignment with human judgments.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了Social Agents，一个多智能体框架，通过模拟一个由多样化类人角色组成的合成社会来改进LLM预测。通过聚合这些智能体的独立判断，它将“群体智慧”原则应用于行为预测，在多项任务上超越了单LLM基线，并实现了与人类判断更高的对齐性。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of human-like personas with diverse demographic and psychographic attributes. Each persona independently evaluates a stimulus \(e.g., an advertisement, video, webpage\) and provides a quantitative score and qualitative rationale. The final prediction is obtained by aggregating the individual opinions from this simulated crowd, leveraging the &quot;Wisdom of Crowds&quot; principle to improve accuracy and better mirror real human preference distributions.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了Social Agents，一个多智能体框架，用于模拟一个由具有多样化人口统计学和心理统计学特征的人类化角色组成的合成社会。每个角色独立评估一个刺激物（如广告、视频、网页），并提供量化评分和定性理由。最终预测通过聚合这个模拟群体的个体意见得出，利用“群体智慧”原则来提高准确性，并更好地反映真实的人类偏好分布。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of diverse personas to improve LLM predictions. By aggregating independent judgments from these agents, it achieves significant performance gains across various behavioral prediction tasks, demonstrating that computational crowd simulation can effectively mirror human collective intelligence.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了Social Agents，一个模拟多样化角色合成社会的多智能体框架，旨在提升大语言模型的预测能力。通过聚合这些智能体的独立判断，该框架在多种行为预测任务上取得了显著的性能提升，证明了计算群体模拟能够有效反映人类集体智慧。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The framework&#x27;s reliance on LLMs inherits their inherent biases, which may skew the simulated crowd&#x27;s demographic and psychographic diversity. Its computational cost scales with the number of agents, and the synthetic personas&#x27; behaviors, while correlated with human judgments, are not fully validated against real-world, long-term social dynamics.  \[翻译\] 该框架对LLM的依赖继承了其固有偏见，可能扭曲模拟人群的人口统计学和心理特征多样性。其计算成本随智能体数量增加而增加，且合成角色的行为虽与人类判断相关，但未在真实世界的长期社会动态中得到充分验证。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to improve the accuracy and realism of LLM-based behavioral predictions by simulating a diverse crowd of synthetic personas, moving beyond single, definitive LLM outputs to better capture the heterogeneity of human judgment. \[翻译\] 该论文旨在通过模拟一个多样化的合成人物群体，超越单一、确定的LLM输出，以更好地捕捉人类判断的异质性，从而提高基于LLM的行为预测的准确性和真实性。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of diverse human-like personas to improve LLM predictions. By aggregating independent judgments from these agents, it applies the "Wisdom of Crowds" principle to behavioral forecasting, outperforming single-LLM baselines across multiple tasks and achieving higher alignment with human judgments.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了Social Agents，一个多智能体框架，通过模拟一个由多样化类人角色组成的合成社会来改进LLM预测。通过聚合这些智能体的独立判断，它将“群体智慧”原则应用于行为预测，在多项任务上超越了单LLM基线，并实现了与人类判断更高的对齐性。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of human-like personas with diverse demographic and psychographic attributes. Each persona independently evaluates a stimulus \(e.g., an advertisement, video, webpage\) and provides a quantitative score and qualitative rationale. The final prediction is obtained by aggregating the individual opinions from this simulated crowd, leveraging the "Wisdom of Crowds" principle to improve accuracy and better mirror real human preference distributions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了Social Agents，一个多智能体框架，用于模拟一个由具有多样化人口统计学和心理统计学特征的人类化角色组成的合成社会。每个角色独立评估一个刺激物（如广告、视频、网页），并提供量化评分和定性理由。最终预测通过聚合这个模拟群体的个体意见得出，利用“群体智慧”原则来提高准确性，并更好地反映真实的人类偏好分布。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social Agents, a multi-agent framework that simulates a synthetic society of diverse personas to improve LLM predictions. By aggregating independent judgments from these agents, it achieves significant performance gains across various behavioral prediction tasks, demonstrating that computational crowd simulation can effectively mirror human collective intelligence.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了Social Agents，一个模拟多样化角色合成社会的多智能体框架，旨在提升大语言模型的预测能力。通过聚合这些智能体的独立判断，该框架在多种行为预测任务上取得了显著的性能提升，证明了计算群体模拟能够有效反映人类集体智慧。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The framework's reliance on LLMs inherits their inherent biases, which may skew the simulated crowd's demographic and psychographic diversity. Its computational cost scales with the number of agents, and the synthetic personas' behaviors, while correlated with human judgments, are not fully validated against real-world, long-term social dynamics.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该框架对LLM的依赖继承了其固有偏见，可能扭曲模拟人群的人口统计学和心理特征多样性。其计算成本随智能体数量增加而增加，且合成角色的行为虽与人类判断相关，但未在真实世界的长期社会动态中得到充分验证。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">目的是通过agent小团体形式集体决策实现LLM本身预测能力提升，主要是处理社媒相关任务（如CTR）</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Conference%20on%20Robot%20Learning-blue)]()<br>[Social interactions as recursive MDPs](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v164/tejwani22a.html) <br> Ravi Tejwani, Yen-Ling Kuo, Tianmin Shu, Boris Katz, Andrei Barbu <br> 2022-01-11 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Individual-Oriented Social Simulation](#Individual-Oriented-Social-Simulation-16-papers), [Micro Social Simulation](#Micro-Social-Simulation-20-papers)]</span>|\[AI generated\] Social MDPs: Extending traditional MDPs by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents' goals to model social interactions like helping or hindering. \[翻译\] 社会性MDP：通过纳入对其他智能体目标的递归推理来扩展传统MDP，以建模如帮助或阻碍等社会交互。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/e588ac3d/Social MDP-[1].png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to formalize social reasoning within the framework of Markov decision processes \(MDPs\) by introducing recursive reasoning about other agents&#x27; goals. It seeks to bridge the gap between well-defined physical tasks and the pre-theoretic nature of social interactions, providing a mathematical model to specify social behaviors like helping or hindering others through reward functions that incorporate estimates of other agents&#x27; goals. This enables zero-shot social inferences and offers a foundation for developing socially aware AI systems.  \[翻译\] 本文旨在通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理，将社会性推理形式化地纳入马尔可夫决策过程框架。它试图弥合明确定义的物理任务与社会互动的前理论性质之间的差距，提供一个数学模型，通过融入对其他智能体目标估计的奖励函数来指定帮助或阻碍他人等社会行为。这使得零样本社会推理成为可能，并为开发具有社会意识的AI系统奠定了基础。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social MDPs, a novel framework that extends traditional Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents&#x27; goals. Its core innovation lies in defining a reward function that combines an agent&#x27;s physical goals with social goals, which are functions of other agents&#x27; estimated reward structures. This allows for the explicit modeling of complex social interactions—such as helping, hindering, or polite/aggressive variants thereof—within a precise mathematical model, enabling zero-shot social inferences and providing a foundation for socially aware AI agents.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了Social MDPs，这是一个新颖的框架，它通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理，扩展了传统的马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）。其核心创新在于定义了一个奖励函数，该函数将智能体的物理目标与社会目标相结合，而社会目标是其他智能体估计的奖励结构的函数。这使得能够在精确的数学模型内显式地建模复杂的社会互动（如帮助、阻碍或其礼貌 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes Social MDPs, a framework that extends Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents&#x27; goals. The core method involves augmenting the traditional reward function to include both physical goals \(related to the configuration space\) and social goals \(defined relative to the estimated reward functions of other agents\). This allows an agent to model interactions such as helping or hindering others by maximizing or minimizing their estimated rewards, while balancing these social objectives with its own physical goals. The formulation supports arbitrary functions over other agents&#x27; reward structures, enabling complex social behaviors.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了Social MDPs框架，通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理来扩展马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）。其核心方法在于扩展传统的奖励函数，使其同时包含物理目标（与配置空间相关）和社会目标（相对于其他智能体的估计奖励函数来定义）。这使得智能体能够通过最大化或最小化其他智能体的估计奖励来建模诸如帮助或 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes Social MDPs, a novel framework that extends traditional Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents&#x27; goals. This allows agents to balance physical objectives with social goals, such as helping or hindering others, enabling zero-shot social inferences and providing a mathematical model for studying social interactions.  \[翻译\] 该论文提出了社会性马尔可夫决策过程（Social MDPs），这是一个新颖的框架，通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理，扩展了传统的马尔可夫决策过程。这使得智能体能够平衡物理目标与社会性目标（如帮助或阻碍他人），实现了零样本社会推理，并为研究社会互动提供了一个数学模型。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The paper does not explicitly list limitations. Based on the abstract, a key limitation is the unknown scope of the proposed Social MDPs and their potential POMDP variants in accounting for all possible social interactions. The framework&#x27;s effectiveness for highly complex, multi-agent, or partially observable social scenarios remains to be fully explored. \[翻译\] 该论文未明确列出局限性。基于摘要，一个关键局限在于所提出的Social MDPs及其潜在的POMDP变体在多大程度上能涵盖所有可能的社会互动仍是未知的。该框架对于高度复杂、多智能体或部分可观察的社会场景的有效性尚待充分探索。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The paper aims to formalize social reasoning within the framework of Markov decision processes \(MDPs\) by introducing recursive reasoning about other agents' goals. It seeks to bridge the gap between well-defined physical tasks and the pre-theoretic nature of social interactions, providing a mathematical model to specify social behaviors like helping or hindering others through reward functions that incorporate estimates of other agents' goals. This enables zero-shot social inferences and offers a foundation for developing socially aware AI systems.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本文旨在通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理，将社会性推理形式化地纳入马尔可夫决策过程框架。它试图弥合明确定义的物理任务与社会互动的前理论性质之间的差距，提供一个数学模型，通过融入对其他智能体目标估计的奖励函数来指定帮助或阻碍他人等社会行为。这使得零样本社会推理成为可能，并为开发具有社会意识的AI系统奠定了基础。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] The paper introduces Social MDPs, a novel framework that extends traditional Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents' goals. Its core innovation lies in defining a reward function that combines an agent's physical goals with social goals, which are functions of other agents' estimated reward structures. This allows for the explicit modeling of complex social interactions—such as helping, hindering, or polite/aggressive variants thereof—within a precise mathematical model, enabling zero-shot social inferences and providing a foundation for socially aware AI agents.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了Social MDPs，这是一个新颖的框架，它通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理，扩展了传统的马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）。其核心创新在于定义了一个奖励函数，该函数将智能体的物理目标与社会目标相结合，而社会目标是其他智能体估计的奖励结构的函数。这使得能够在精确的数学模型内显式地建模复杂的社会互动（如帮助、阻碍或其礼貌<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes Social MDPs, a framework that extends Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents' goals. The formulation supports arbitrary functions over other agents' reward structures, enabling complex social behaviors.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>该论文提出了Social MDPs框架，通过引入对其他智能体目标的递归推理来扩展马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）。其核心方法在于扩展传统的奖励函数，使其同时包含物理目标（与配置空间相关）和社会目标（相对于其他智能体的估计奖励函数来定义）。这使得智能体能够通过最大化或最小化其他智能体的估计奖励来建模诸如帮助或<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] The paper proposes Social MDPs, a novel framework that extends traditional Markov Decision Processes \(MDPs\) by incorporating recursive reasoning about other agents' goals.
