### | Other (7 papers)

| Title & Info | Analogy Summary | Pipeline | Summary |
|:--| :---: | :----: | :---: |
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-AAAI-blue)]()<br>[Backdoor Attack on Propagation-based Rumor Detectors](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/33944) <br> Di Jin, Yujun Zhang, Bingdao Feng, Xiaobao Wang, Dongxiao He, Zhen Wang <br> 2025-04-11|\[AI generated\] Backdoor Attack on Propagation-based Rumor Detectors: Like a sleeper agent in a social network, it lies dormant until triggered to manipulate rumor detection outcomes. \[翻译\] 基于传播的谣言检测器后门攻击：如同潜伏在社交网络中的休眠特工，平时隐蔽，一旦触发即可操控谣言判定结果。|<img width="1200" alt="pipeline" src="assets/f3d65574/IBAttack.png">| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The proliferation of misinformation on social media necessitates robust rumor detection systems. Propagation-based detectors, while effective, are vulnerable to adversarial manipulation. Existing research has focused on adversarial attacks, but the more stealthy and persistent threat of backdoor attacks remains unexplored for these models due to the complexity of graph-structured data. This work is motivated by the critical need to investigate and expose this novel security vulnerability to enhance the resilience of rumor detection systems. \[翻译\] 社交媒体上虚假信息的扩散需要强大的谣言检测系统。基于传播结构的检测器虽然有效，但易受对抗性操纵。现有研究集中于对抗攻击，但由于图结构数据的复杂性，针对此类模型的、更具隐蔽性和持久性的后门攻击威胁尚未被探索。本工作的动机在于迫切需要研究和揭示这一新的安全漏洞，以增强谣言检测系统的鲁棒性。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper pioneers the first backdoor attack framework targeting propagation-based rumor detectors. Its core innovation is an adaptive discrete trigger generator that strategically injects trigger nodes into critical positions within a propagation tree. This method maintains the model&#x27;s general performance while enabling evasive, targeted manipulation, and demonstrates strong transferability across different detector architectures. \[翻译\] 本文开创了首个针对基于传播的谣言检测器的后门攻击框架。其核心创新在于一种自适应的离散触发器生成器，能策略性地将触发节点注入传播树的关键位置。该方法在保持模型整体性能的同时，实现了隐蔽的定向操控，并在不同检测器架构间展现出强大的可迁移性。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] The proposed method introduces an adaptive discrete trigger generator that strategically injects trigger nodes into critical positions within propagation trees. This approach ensures stealthiness and transferability by modifying structural features without altering semantic content, enabling effective evasion of detection while maintaining overall model performance. \[翻译\] 该方法提出了一种自适应离散触发器生成器，通过将触发节点策略性地注入传播树的关键位置，在不改变语义内容的前提下修改结构特征，从而确保攻击的隐蔽性和可迁移性，在维持模型整体性能的同时实现有效规避检测。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This study proposes the first backdoor attack framework targeting propagation-based rumor detectors. By adaptively injecting discrete trigger nodes into critical positions of propagation trees, the attack successfully compromises detector performance on specific rumors while maintaining overall functionality, demonstrating high evasion and transferability across architectures. \[翻译\] 本研究首次提出了针对基于传播的谣言检测器的后门攻击框架。通过将离散触发器节点自适应注入传播树的关键位置，该攻击能有效破坏检测器对特定谣言的性能，同时保持整体功能，在不同架构间展现出高规避性与可迁移性。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] **Summary of Limitations:**   The study primarily focuses on propagation-based rumor detectors, leaving other detection paradigms \(e.g., content- or user-based methods\) unexplored. The proposed attack framework assumes access to critical nodes in propagation trees, which may not always be feasible in real-world adversarial scenarios. Additionally, the transferability of triggers across vastly different model architectures requires further validation under more diverse conditions.   \[翻译\]   **局限性总结：**   本研究主要针对基于传播结构的谣言检测器，未探索其他检测范式（如基于内容或用户的方法）。所提出的攻击框架假设可访问传播树中的关键节点，这在现实对抗场景中未必可行。此外，触发器在不同模型架构间的可迁移性需在更多样化条件下进一步验证。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The proliferation of misinformation on social media necessitates robust rumor detection systems. Propagation-based detectors, while effective, are vulnerable to adversarial manipulation. Existing research has focused on adversarial attacks, but the more stealthy and persistent threat of backdoor attacks remains unexplored for these models due to the complexity of graph-structured data. This work is motivated by the critical need to investigate and expose this novel security vulnerability to enhance the resilience of rumor detection systems. \[翻译\] 社交媒体上虚假信息的扩散需要强大的谣言检测系统。基于传播结构的检测器虽然有效，但易受对抗性操纵。现有研究集中于对抗攻击，但由于图结构数据的复杂性，针对此类模型的、更具隐蔽性和持久性的后门攻击威胁尚未被探索。本工作的动机在于迫切需要研究和揭示这一新的安全漏洞，以增强谣言检测系统的鲁棒性。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This paper pioneers the first backdoor attack framework targeting propagation-based rumor detectors. Its core innovation is an adaptive discrete trigger generator that strategically injects trigger nodes into critical positions within a propagation tree. This method maintains the model's general performance while enabling evasive, targeted manipulation, and demonstrates strong transferability across different detector architectures. \[翻译\] 本文开创了首个针对基于传播的谣言检测器的后门攻击框架。其核心创新在于一种自适应的离散触发器生成器，能策略性地将触发节点注入传播树的关键位置。该方法在保持模型整体性能的同时，实现了隐蔽的定向操控，并在不同检测器架构间展现出强大的可迁移性。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] The proposed method introduces an adaptive discrete trigger generator that strategically injects trigger nodes into critical positions within propagation trees. This approach ensures stealthiness and transferability by modifying structural features without altering semantic content, enabling effective evasion of detection while maintaining overall model performance. \[翻译\] 该方法提出了一种自适应离散触发器生成器，通过将触发节点策略性地注入传播树的关键位置，在不改变语义内容的前提下修改结构特征，从而确保攻击的隐蔽性和可迁移性，在维持模型整体性能的同时实现有效规避检测。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This study proposes the first backdoor attack framework targeting propagation-based rumor detectors. By adaptively injecting discrete trigger nodes into critical positions of propagation trees, the attack successfully compromises detector performance on specific rumors while maintaining overall functionality, demonstrating high evasion and transferability across architectures. \[翻译\] 本研究首次提出了针对基于传播的谣言检测器的后门攻击框架。通过将离散触发器节点自适应注入传播树的关键位置，该攻击能有效破坏检测器对特定谣言的性能，同时保持整体功能，在不同架构间展现出高规避性与可迁移性。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] **Summary of Limitations:**  <br>The study primarily focuses on propagation-based rumor detectors, leaving other detection paradigms \(e.g., content- or user-based methods\) unexplored. The proposed attack framework assumes access to critical nodes in propagation trees, which may not always be feasible in real-world adversarial scenarios. Additionally, the transferability of triggers across vastly different model architectures requires further validation under more diverse conditions.  <br>\[翻译\]  <br>**局限性总结：**  <br>本研究主要针对基于传播结构的谣言检测器，未探索其他检测范式（如基于内容或用户的方法）。所提出的攻击框架假设可访问传播树中的关键节点，这在现实对抗场景中未必可行。此外，触发器在不同模型架构间的可迁移性需在更多样化条件下进一步验证。</div></details></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-IEEE%20Trans.%20Inf.%20Forensics%20Security-blue)]()<br>[A<sup>2</sup> S<sup>2</sup> -GNN: Rigging GNN-based social status by adversarial attacks in signe...](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9936655/) <br> Xiaoyan Yin, Wanyu Lin, Kexin Sun, Chun Wei, Yanjiao Chen <br> 2023|方法研究社交网络图中的地位关系（由有向边决定方向），尝试找出使目标用户节点地位提升最多或下降最多的边增删策略。不研究任何社交内容|<div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;align-items:center"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/0c551ac4/A2S2-GNN-1.png"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/0c551ac4/A2S2-GNN-2.png"></div>| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** While adversarial attacks on Graph Neural Networks \(GNNs\) have been extensively studied for classification in unsigned graphs, the manipulation of social status ranking in signed directed networks remains underexplored. \[翻译\] 尽管针对图神经网络（GNN）的对抗攻击在无符号图分类任务中已被广泛研究，但在符号有向网络中对社交地位排名的操纵仍缺乏探索。 **[innovation]** This study presents the first white-box adversarial framework for social status, integrating Status Theory into GNNs to differentiate four types of neighbor influences \(positive/negative\) and extending the attack scope to 2-hop neighbors. \[翻译\] 本研究提出了首个针对社交地位的白盒对抗框架，将地位理论融入GNN以区分四种类型的邻居影响（正/负向），并将攻击范围扩展至二跳邻居。 **[method]** The framework,  $A^2S^2-GNN$, aggregates features from four neighbor categories \(in/out-degree friends/foes\) via mean-pooling. The attack employs a static greedy strategy: it globally calculates and ranks the influence of operable nodes \(1-hop and 2-hop\) once, then iteratively modifies edges based on this fixed ranking under budget constraints. \[翻译\] 该框架 $A^2S^2-GNN$ 通过均值池化聚合来自四类邻居（入度/出度的好友/敌人）的特征。攻击采用静态贪婪策略：一次性全局计算并排序可操作节点（1跳及2跳）的影响力，随后在预算约束下基于此固定排名迭代修改边。 **[conclusion/contribution]** Experimental results on seven datasets indicate that the method outperforms baselines like RTA and DICE, achieving up to 89.36% status promotion and 192.38% demotion, while exhibiting strong transferability to classical ranking algorithms such as PageRank. \[翻译\] 在七个数据集上的实验结果表明，该方法优于RTA和DICE等基准，实现了高达89.36%的地位提升和192.38%的贬低，同时在PageRank等经典排名算法上表现出极强的迁移性。 **[limitation/future]** The reliance on a static greedy algorithm \(ranking nodes only once prior to execution\) sacrifices dynamic optimality for computational efficiency, as it ignores topological shifts and feature updates during the sequential edge modification process. \[翻译\] 依赖静态贪婪算法（仅在执行前对节点排名一次）为了计算效率牺牲了动态最优性，因为它忽略了连续修改边过程中的拓扑结构变化和特征更新。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** While adversarial attacks on Graph Neural Networks \(GNNs\) have been extensively studied for classification in unsigned graphs, the manipulation of social status ranking in signed directed networks remains underexplored.<br>\[翻译\] 尽管针对图神经网络（GNN）的对抗攻击在无符号图分类任务中已被广泛研究，但在符号有向网络中对社交地位排名的操纵仍缺乏探索。<br>**[innovation]** This study presents the first white-box adversarial framework for social status, integrating Status Theory into GNNs to differentiate four types of neighbor influences \(positive/negative\) and extending the attack scope to 2-hop neighbors.<br>\[翻译\] 本研究提出了首个针对社交地位的白盒对抗框架，将地位理论融入GNN以区分四种类型的邻居影响（正/负向），并将攻击范围扩展至二跳邻居。<br>**[method]** The framework, <br>$A^2S^2-GNN$, aggregates features from four neighbor categories \(in/out-degree friends/foes\) via mean-pooling. The attack employs a static greedy strategy: it globally calculates and ranks the influence of operable nodes \(1-hop and 2-hop\) once, then iteratively modifies edges based on this fixed ranking under budget constraints.<br>\[翻译\] 该框架 $A^2S^2-GNN$ 通过均值池化聚合来自四类邻居（入度/出度的好友/敌人）的特征。攻击采用静态贪婪策略：一次性全局计算并排序可操作节点（1跳及2跳）的影响力，随后在预算约束下基于此固定排名迭代修改边。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** Experimental results on seven datasets indicate that the method outperforms baselines like RTA and DICE, achieving up to 89.36% status promotion and 192.38% demotion, while exhibiting strong transferability to classical ranking algorithms such as PageRank.<br>\[翻译\] 在七个数据集上的实验结果表明，该方法优于RTA和DICE等基准，实现了高达89.36%的地位提升和192.38%的贬低，同时在PageRank等经典排名算法上表现出极强的迁移性。<br>**[limitation/future]** The reliance on a static greedy algorithm \(ranking nodes only once prior to execution\) sacrifices dynamic optimality for computational efficiency, as it ignores topological shifts and feature updates during the sequential edge modification process.<br>\[翻译\] 依赖静态贪婪算法（仅在执行前对节点排名一次）为了计算效率牺牲了动态最优性，因为它忽略了连续修改边过程中的拓扑结构变化和特征更新。</div></details></div>|
|[![Star](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/OpenBMB/IoA.svg?style=social&label=Star)](https://github.com/OpenBMB/IoA) [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-The%20Thirteenth%20International%20Conference%20on%20Learning%20Representations-blue)]()<br>[Internet of agents: Weaving a web of heterogeneous agents for collaborative intelligence](https://openreview.net/forum?id=o1Et3MogPw) <br> Weize Chen\*, Ziming You\*, Ran Li\*, Yitong Guan\*, Chen Qian, Chenyang Zhao Cheng Yang, Ruobing Xie, Zhiyuan Liu, Maosong Sun <br> 2024-10-04|agent互联网，升级版ABM系统，采用类似互联网思想，C/S架构，分布化、服务化、平台化|<div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;align-items:center"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/97ae087b/IoA.png"><img width="1000" style="display:block;margin:6px auto" alt="pipeline" src="assets/97ae087b/IoA2.png"></div>| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** 先前的multi-agent系统的局限性，系统化平台化程度不足（缺乏第三方集成支持，无法分布式，通信协议和状态转换依赖于硬编码） **[innovation]** 将互联网的开放、分布式、服务化思想引入，构建一种标准化、可扩展的支持分布式、异构的智能体集成与通信协议。 **[method]** 服务器：智能体注册（分发系统提示词）、管理已注册智能体（专家）、专家发现服务、群聊管理和消息传递；客户端：包装具体智能体，提供通信接口；三层结构；通信即可嵌套灵活群聊；群聊采用**有限状态机**管理流程；平台初始化与注册-&gt;任务触发团队形成-&gt;内部嵌套协作 **[conclusion/contribution]** 在 GAIA 基准测试中，仅使用四个基础 ReAct 智能体即达到最佳性能；在 RAG 任务中，基于 GPT-3.5 的 IoA 达到或超过 GPT-4 的性能 **[limitation/future]** 实验中存在冗余消息，通信 Token 消耗增加近一倍，这证明agent作为对话者而非执行者的本质能力区别；单点服务器可能存在瓶颈；智能体通过注册获取提示词成为不同专家，仍高度依赖人工实验设计，且这种专家的能力是否可靠">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** 先前的multi-agent系统的局限性，系统化平台化程度不足（缺乏第三方集成支持，无法分布式，通信协议和状态转换依赖于硬编码）<br>**[innovation]** 将互联网的开放、分布式、服务化思想引入，构建一种标准化、可扩展的支持分布式、异构的智能体集成与通信协议。<br>**[method]** 服务器：智能体注册（分发系统提示词）、管理已注册智能体（专家）、专家发现服务、群聊管理和消息传递；客户端：包装具体智能体，提供通信接口；三层结构；通信即可嵌套灵活群聊；群聊采用**有限状态机**管理流程；平台初始化与注册->任务触发团队形成->内部嵌套协作<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** 在 GAIA 基准测试中，仅使用四个基础 ReAct 智能体即达到最佳性能；在 RAG 任务中，基于 GPT-3.5 的 IoA 达到或超过 GPT-4 的性能<br>**[limitation/future]** 实验中存在冗余消息，通信 Token 消耗增加近一倍，这证明agent作为对话者而非执行者的本质能力区别；单点服务器可能存在瓶颈；智能体通过注册获取提示词成为不同专家，仍高度依赖人工实验设计，且这种专家的能力是否可靠</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">每个agent被一个客户端包装；服务器不是agent，它只做四件事：注册、发现、建群、路由；相对于传统ABM，这是一个更大型的服务系统，该方法通过基于任务的“群聊”方式组织问题解决，相对传统回合制方式更加自由。本身也是一个高度可扩展系统。问题在于智能体通过注册获取提示词成为不同专家，仍依赖手工设计，且这种专家的能力是否可靠。对于社会模拟任务相对于传统方法有何决定性优势仍未可知</div></details></div></div>|
| [![Publish](https://img.shields.io/badge/Conference-Humanit%20Soc%20Sci%20Commun-blue)]()<br>[Large language models empowered agent-based modeling and simulation: a survey and perspectives](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41599-024-03611-3) <br> Chen Gao, Xiaochong Lan, Nian Li, Yuan Yuan, Jingtao Ding, Zhilun Zhou, Fengli Xu, Yong Li <br> 2024-09-27 <br> <span style="color:cyan">[multi-category：[Agent-Based Social Simulation](#Agent-Based-Social-Simulation-18-papers), [Dynamic Social Simulation](#Dynamic-Social-Simulation-27-papers), [Group-Oriented Social Simulation](#Group-Oriented-Social-Simulation-8-papers), [Social Simulation for Other Fields](#Social-Simulation-for-Other-Fields-1-papers), [Other](#-Other-7-papers)]</span>|TLDR: This paper surveys the landscape of utilizing large language models in agent-based modeling and simulation, discussing their challenges and promising future directions and provides a comprehensive overview of the recent works of large language model-empowered agent-based modeling and simulation in multiple scenarios.|| <div style="line-height: 1.05;font-size: 0.8em"> <details><summary title="**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\) is motivated by the need to overcome the limitations of traditional simulation agents, which often lack the autonomy, social intelligence, and adaptive reasoning required for realistic modeling of complex systems. LLM-empowered agents offer enhanced capabilities in environment perception, human-like decision-making, and adaptive learning, enabling more nuanced and scalable simulations across social, physical, cyber, and hybrid domains.  \[翻译\] 将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中，其动机源于需要克服传统仿真主体的局限性。传统主体通常缺乏自主性、社会智能和自适应推理能力，而这些对于复杂系统的真实建模至关重要。LLM赋能的主体在环境感知、类人决策和自适应学习方面提供了增强的能力，从而能够在社会、物理、网络和混合领域实现更细致、可扩展的仿真。 **[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This survey pioneers a systematic review of integrating Large Language Models \(LLM\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\), establishing a foundational taxonomy across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains. It uniquely identifies and analyzes core technical challenges—environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation—providing a structured framework for this nascent, interdisciplinary field. The work also curates a comprehensive repository of representative papers and code, serving as a key resource for future research.  \[翻译\] 本综述开创性地系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究，建立了涵盖网络、物理、社会及混合领域的基础分类体系。它独特地识别并分析了环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成与评估等核心技术挑战，为这一新兴的跨学科领域提供了结构化框架。该工作还整理了一个包含代表性论文与代码的综合资源库，为未来研究提供了关键资源。 **[method]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the literature by first establishing interdisciplinary background knowledge, then analyzing the motivation and core challenges of integrating LLMs into ABMS. It provides a comprehensive overview of recent works categorized into four simulation domains \(cyber, physical, social, hybrid\) and discusses how they address key challenges. Finally, it outlines open problems and future research directions.  \[翻译\] 本综述采用系统性的方法：首先建立跨学科的背景知识，随后分析将大语言模型融入基于主体的建模与仿真的动机与核心挑战。它提供了一个全面的近期工作概览，这些工作被分为四个仿真领域（网络、物理、社会、混合），并讨论了它们如何应对关键挑战。最后，文章概述了开放性问题与未来的研究方向。 **[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\). It highlights how LLM-empowered agents enhance simulation realism by addressing core challenges in environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation. The paper provides a comprehensive overview of recent applications across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains, and discusses open problems and promising future directions for this rapidly evolving interdisciplinary field.  \[翻译\] 本综述系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLMs）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究。文章强调了LLM赋能的主体如何通过解决环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成和评估等核心挑战来提升仿真的真实性。论文全面概述了在信息、物理、社会和混合领域的最新应用，并讨论了这一快速发展的跨学科领域所面临的开放性问题与未来前景。 **[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The survey primarily focuses on conceptual frameworks and high-level applications, lacking a systematic empirical evaluation of LLM agents&#x27; performance across diverse simulation scenarios. It also does not deeply address the computational costs, ethical risks, and potential biases inherent in deploying LLMs for large-scale, realistic social simulations.  \[翻译\] 本综述主要关注概念框架和高层应用，缺乏对不同模拟场景下大语言模型智能体性能的系统性实证评估。同时，也未深入探讨部署大语言模型进行大规模、现实社会模拟所涉及的计算成本、伦理风险及潜在偏见问题。">**[summary]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">**[motivation]** \[AI generated\] The integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\) is motivated by the need to overcome the limitations of traditional simulation agents, which often lack the autonomy, social intelligence, and adaptive reasoning required for realistic modeling of complex systems. LLM-empowered agents offer enhanced capabilities in environment perception, human-like decision-making, and adaptive learning, enabling more nuanced and scalable simulations across social, physical, cyber, and hybrid domains.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中，其动机源于需要克服传统仿真主体的局限性。传统主体通常缺乏自主性、社会智能和自适应推理能力，而这些对于复杂系统的真实建模至关重要。LLM赋能的主体在环境感知、类人决策和自适应学习方面提供了增强的能力，从而能够在社会、物理、网络和混合领域实现更细致、可扩展的仿真。<br>**[innovation]** \[AI generated\] This survey pioneers a systematic review of integrating Large Language Models \(LLM\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\), establishing a foundational taxonomy across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains. It uniquely identifies and analyzes core technical challenges—environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation—providing a structured framework for this nascent, interdisciplinary field. The work also curates a comprehensive repository of representative papers and code, serving as a key resource for future research.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述开创性地系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLM）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究，建立了涵盖网络、物理、社会及混合领域的基础分类体系。它独特地识别并分析了环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成与评估等核心技术挑战，为这一新兴的跨学科领域提供了结构化框架。该工作还整理了一个包含代表性论文与代码的综合资源库，为未来研究提供了关键资源。<br>**[method]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the literature by first establishing interdisciplinary background knowledge, then analyzing the motivation and core challenges of integrating LLMs into ABMS. It provides a comprehensive overview of recent works categorized into four simulation domains \(cyber, physical, social, hybrid\) and discusses how they address key challenges. Finally, it outlines open problems and future research directions.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述采用系统性的方法：首先建立跨学科的背景知识，随后分析将大语言模型融入基于主体的建模与仿真的动机与核心挑战。它提供了一个全面的近期工作概览，这些工作被分为四个仿真领域（网络、物理、社会、混合），并讨论了它们如何应对关键挑战。最后，文章概述了开放性问题与未来的研究方向。<br>**[conclusion/contribution]** \[AI generated\] This survey systematically reviews the integration of Large Language Models \(LLMs\) into Agent-Based Modeling and Simulation \(ABMS\). It highlights how LLM-empowered agents enhance simulation realism by addressing core challenges in environment perception, human alignment, action generation, and evaluation. The paper provides a comprehensive overview of recent applications across cyber, physical, social, and hybrid domains, and discusses open problems and promising future directions for this rapidly evolving interdisciplinary field.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述系统回顾了将大语言模型（LLMs）集成到基于主体的建模与仿真（ABMS）中的研究。文章强调了LLM赋能的主体如何通过解决环境感知、人类对齐、行动生成和评估等核心挑战来提升仿真的真实性。论文全面概述了在信息、物理、社会和混合领域的最新应用，并讨论了这一快速发展的跨学科领域所面临的开放性问题与未来前景。<br>**[limitation/future]** \[AI generated\] The survey primarily focuses on conceptual frameworks and high-level applications, lacking a systematic empirical evaluation of LLM agents' performance across diverse simulation scenarios. It also does not deeply address the computational costs, ethical risks, and potential biases inherent in deploying LLMs for large-scale, realistic social simulations.<br><br>\[翻译\]<br>本综述主要关注概念框架和高层应用，缺乏对不同模拟场景下大语言模型智能体性能的系统性实证评估。同时，也未深入探讨部署大语言模型进行大规模、现实社会模拟所涉及的计算成本、伦理风险及潜在偏见问题。</div></details><div style="margin-top:6px"><details><summary>**[notes]**</summary><div style="margin-top:6px">catSuggestion<br>{<br>  "target_ids": \[<br>    "Agent-based Social Simulation",<br>    "Dynamic Social Simulation",<br>    "Group-oriented Social Simulation",<br>    "Social Simulation for Other Fields"<br>  \],<br>  "status": "matched",<br>  "suggestion": null,<br>  "meta": {<br>    "title": "Large language models empowered agent-based modeling and simulation: a survey and perspectives",<br>    "publication_date": "2024-09-27"<br>  },<br>  "paper_summary": "Abstract Agent-based modeling and simulation have evolved as a powerful tool for modeling complex systems, offering insights into emergent behaviors and interactions among diverse agents. Recently, integrating large language models into agent-based modeling and simulation presents a promising avenue for enhancing simulation capabilities. This paper surveys the landscape of utilizing large language models in agent-based modeling and simulation, discussing their challenges and promising future dir"<br>}</div></details></div></div>|
